verses.trans: When the waters dry up, they pass into the wind. For the wind absorbs them all. This with reference to the gods.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The cosmic side of the teaching is complete: all luminaries and elements return to wind, the unresting power of the manifest world.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The visible resolution of elements into Vāyu mirrors the final resolution of all into the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Vāyu's universal absorption is delegated might, magnifying the Lord who empowers him.

verses.dev: अथाध्यात्मं प्राणो वाव संवर्गः स यदा स्वपिति प्राणमेव वागप्येति प्राणं चक्षुः प्राणं श्रोत्रं प्राणं मनः प्राणो ह्येवैतान्सर्वान्संवृङ्क्त इति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: athādhyātmaṃ prāṇo vāva saṃvargaḥ sa yadā svapiti prāṇam eva vāg apyeti prāṇaṃ cakṣuḥ prāṇaṃ śrotraṃ prāṇaṃ manaḥ prāṇo hy evaitān sarvān saṃvṛṅkta iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: Now with reference to the self: the breath, verily, is the absorber. When one sleeps, speech passes into the breath; the eye into the breath; the ear into the breath; the mind into the breath. For the breath absorbs them all.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: In sleep the faculties dissolve into breath — nightly proof that the many rest in a prior one. The meditation uses this daily dissolution as a window onto the final unity.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The inner absorber is the Lord's vital presence within; the senses' nightly return to prāṇa is their homage to the indweller.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Prāṇa — again Vāyu within the body — receives the senses in sleep, governing them as the Lord's deputy.

verses.dev: तौ वा एतौ द्वौ संवर्गौ वायुरेव देवेषु प्राणः प्राणेषु ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tau vā etau dvau saṃvargau vāyur eva deveṣu prāṇaḥ prāṇeṣu || 4 ||
verses.trans: These, verily, are the two absorbers: the wind among the gods, the breath among the vital powers.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: One absorbing principle wears two names, cosmic and personal — the lesson of all Upaniṣadic correlation: what is without is within.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The twin absorbers are one minister of the Lord serving in two theatres.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Vāyu and prāṇa are one mighty soul in two offices, supreme among created beings.

verses.dev: अथ ह शौनकं च कापेयमभिप्रतारिणं च काक्षसेनिं परिविष्यमाणौ ब्रह्मचारी बिभिक्षे तस्मा उ ह न ददतुः ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: atha ha śaunakaṃ ca kāpeyam abhipratāriṇaṃ ca kākṣaseniṃ pariviṣyamāṇau brahmacārī bibhikṣe tasmā u ha na dadatuḥ || 5 ||
verses.trans: Once, while Śaunaka Kāpeya and Abhipratārin Kākṣaseni were being served their meal, a student of sacred knowledge begged of them. They gave him nothing.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: A second narrative tests the doctrine: the learned diners are about to be examined on the very vidyā by a beggar at their door.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord sends the student to expose knowledge held without humility.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The refused alms set the stage for the boy's riddle on the Absorber.

verses.dev: स होवाच महात्मनश्चतुरो देव एकः कः स जगार भुवनस्य गोपास्तं कापेय नाभिपश्यन्ति मर्त्या अभिप्रतारिन्बहुधा वसन्तं यस्मै वा एतदन्नं तस्मा एतन्न दत्तमिति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: sa hovāca mahātmanaś caturo deva ekaḥ kaḥ sa jagāra bhuvanasya gopās taṃ kāpeya nābhipaśyanti martyā abhipratārin bahudhā vasantaṃ yasmai vā etad annaṃ tasmā etan na dattam iti || 6 ||
verses.trans: He said: 'One god — who is he? — has swallowed four mighty ones, he the guardian of the world. Him, O Kāpeya, mortals do not see, O Abhipratārin, though he dwells manifoldly. Truly, to him to whom this food belongs, it has not been given.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The riddle: the one Absorber (prāṇa/Vāyu) has swallowed the four great gods (fire, sun, moon, waters — or speech, eye, ear, mind). Food withheld from any feeder is withheld from the universal Eater present in all.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The hidden guardian who eats through every mouth is the Lord as universal sustainer; to refuse the beggar is to refuse Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The boy names Vāyu-prāṇa, the world-guardian under Viṣṇu, dwelling unseen in all eaters; alms denied to him insult his Master.

verses.dev: तदु ह शौनकः कापेयः प्रतिमन्वानः प्रत्येयायात्मा देवानां जनिता प्रजानां हिरण्यदंष्ट्रो बभसोऽनसूरिर्महान्तमस्य महिमानमाहुरनद्यमानो यदनन्नमत्तीति वै वयं ब्रह्मचारिन्नेदमुपास्महे दत्तास्मै भिक्षामिति ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: tad u ha śaunakaḥ kāpeyaḥ pratimanvānaḥ pratyeyāyātmā devānāṃ janitā prajānāṃ hiraṇyadaṃṣṭro babhaso 'nasūrir mahāntam asya mahimānam āhur anadyamāno yad anannam attīti vai vayaṃ brahmacārin nedam upāsmahe dattāsmai bhikṣām iti || 7 ||
verses.trans: Reflecting on this, Śaunaka Kāpeya approached him and said: 'He is the self of the gods, the begetter of creatures, the gold-toothed devourer, truly wise. Great, they say, is his majesty, for unconsumed he eats even what is not food. Thus, O student, do we meditate on this.' (Then he said:) 'Give him alms!'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The diner redeems himself by expounding the Absorber's majesty: uneaten eater, self of gods. Recognising the boy's knowledge, he honours it with food — knowledge acknowledged restores dharma.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Śaunaka's hymn describes the Lord's universal feeding; right understanding immediately issues in generosity.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The 'gold-toothed devourer' is prāṇa empowered by Viṣṇu; the wise diner, seeing this, serves the beggar as the god's representative.

verses.dev: तस्म उ ह ददुस्ते वा एते पञ्चान्ये पञ्चान्ये दश सन्तस्तत्कृतं तस्मात्सर्वासु दिक्ष्वन्नमेव दश कृतं सैषा विराडन्नादी तयेदं सर्वं दृष्टं सर्वमस्येदं दृष्टं भवत्यन्नादो भवति य एवं वेद य एवं वेद ॥ ८ ॥
verses.iast: tasma u ha dadus te vā ete pañcānye pañcānye daśa santas tat kṛtaṃ tasmāt sarvāsu dikṣv annam eva daśa kṛtaṃ saiṣā virāḍ annādī tayedaṃ sarvaṃ dṛṣṭaṃ sarvam asyedaṃ dṛṣṭaṃ bhavaty annādo bhavati ya evaṃ veda ya evaṃ veda || 8 ||
verses.trans: So they gave him alms. Now these five (wind, fire, sun, moon, waters) and those five (breath, speech, eye, ear, mind), being ten, make the kṛta (the perfect throw). Therefore in all the quarters the ten — food itself — are the kṛta. This is Virāj, the eater of food. By her all this is beheld. He who knows thus beholds all this and becomes an eater of food — yes, he who knows thus.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The two pentads unite as the perfect ten — the winning throw that gathers all, echoing the dice-image of the opening story. Virāj, the cosmic totality, is the eater; the knower shares her universal vision and sustenance. The repetition closes the Saṃvarga-vidyā.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The tenfold Virāj is the Lord's cosmic form as universal enjoyer; the meditator beholds all things in her and lacks no nourishment.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ten powers form the Lord's perfect order; he who knows their unity under Viṣṇu receives abundance and vision.

verses.dev: सत्यकामो ह जाबालो जबालां मातरमामन्त्रयांचक्रे ब्रह्मचर्यं भवति विवत्स्यामि किंगोत्रो न्वहमस्मीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: satyakāmo ha jābālo jabālāṃ mātaram āmantrayāṃ cakre brahmacaryaṃ bhavati vivatsyāmi kiṃgotro nv aham asmīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Satyakāma Jābāla addressed his mother Jabālā: 'Revered mother, I wish to live as a student of sacred knowledge. Of what lineage (gotra) am I?'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The boy's first act is a question of truth about himself — fitting for one whose name means 'lover of truth' and whose path will be the knowledge of the true Self.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Desire for brahmacarya is the stirring of the Lord's grace in the young soul; honest inquiry into one's state begins the journey.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The will to study arises from prior merit and divine favour; the boy seeks proper entry through truthful credentials.

verses.dev: सा हैनमुवाच नाहमेतद्वेद तात यद्गोत्रस्त्वमसि बह्वहं चरन्ती परिचारिणी यौवने त्वामलभे साहमेतन्न वेद यद्गोत्रस्त्वमसि जबाला तु नामाहमस्मि सत्यकामो नाम त्वमसि स सत्यकाम एव जाबालो ब्रवीथा इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sā hainam uvāca nāham etad veda tāta yadgotras tvam asi bahv ahaṃ carantī paricāriṇī yauvane tvām alabhe sāham etan na veda yadgotras tvam asi jabālā tu nāmāham asmi satyakāmo nāma tvam asi sa satyakāma eva jābālo bravīthā iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: She said to him: 'I do not know, dear one, of what lineage you are. Serving in many places in my youth, I conceived you; so I do not know your lineage. But my name is Jabālā and your name is Satyakāma. Call yourself, then, Satyakāma Jābāla.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The mother's unflinching honesty is the story's moral hinge: she hides nothing, and her truth becomes the son's credential. Śaṅkara: truthfulness, not birth, is the brāhmaṇa's mark.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Jabālā's candour sanctifies her son; the Lord values the heart's transparency above pedigree.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Truth spoken at cost reveals inner worth; the boy's qualification is being forged in his mother's honesty.

verses.dev: स ह हारिद्रुमतं गौतममेत्योवाच ब्रह्मचर्यं भगवति वत्स्याम्युपेयां भगवन्तमिति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ha hāridrumataṃ gautamam etyovāca brahmacaryaṃ bhagavati vatsyāmy upeyāṃ bhagavantam iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: He came to Hāridrumata Gautama and said: 'I wish to live with you, revered sir, as a student of sacred knowledge. May I approach you?'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Approach to a teacher (upasadana) is the prescribed door to knowledge; the boy follows the eternal protocol.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Seeking the ācārya is itself worship; the Lord receives the seeker through His representative.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Instruction flows only through the rightful channel of a qualified guru.

verses.dev: तं होवाच किंगोत्रो नु सोम्यासीति स होवाच नाहमेतद्वेद भो यद्गोत्रोऽहमस्म्यपृच्छं मातरं सा मा प्रत्यब्रवीद्बह्वहं चरन्ती परिचारिणी यौवने त्वामलभे साहमेतन्न वेद यद्गोत्रस्त्वमसि जबाला तु नामाहमस्मि सत्यकामो नाम त्वमसीति सोऽहं सत्यकामो जाबालोऽस्मि भो इति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ hovāca kiṃgotro nu somyāsīti sa hovāca nāham etad veda bho yadgotro 'ham asmy apṛcchaṃ mātaraṃ sā mā pratyabravīd bahv ahaṃ carantī paricāriṇī yauvane tvām alabhe sāham etan na veda yadgotras tvam asi jabālā tu nāmāham asmi satyakāmo nāma tvam asīti so 'haṃ satyakāmo jābālo 'smi bho iti || 4 ||
verses.trans: He said to him: 'Of what lineage are you, gentle one?' He replied: 'I do not know, sir, of what lineage I am. I asked my mother, and she answered: "Serving in many places in my youth, I conceived you; so I do not know your lineage. But my name is Jabālā and your name is Satyakāma." So I am Satyakāma Jābāla, sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The boy repeats the painful truth without gloss — the conduct of one fit for the highest teaching. Truth-telling under trial is the supreme entrance examination.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: His fearless honesty before the teacher mirrors his mother's; such a soul the Lord swiftly draws near.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Unvarnished truth marks the inner brāhmaṇa; Madhva sees fitness shining through circumstance.

verses.dev: तं होवाच नैतदब्राह्मणो विवक्तुमर्हति समिधं सोम्याहरोप त्वा नेष्ये न सत्यादगा इति तमुपनीय कृशानामबलानां चतुःशता गा निराकृत्योवाचेमाः सोम्यानुसंव्रजेति ता अभिप्रस्थापयन्नुवाच नासहस्रेणावर्तेयेति स ह वर्षगणं प्रोवास ता यदा सहस्रं संपेदुः ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ hovāca naitad abrāhmaṇo vivaktum arhati samidhaṃ somyāharopa tvā neṣye na satyād agā iti tam upanīya kṛśānām abalānāṃ catuḥśatā gā nirākṛtyovācemāḥ somyānusaṃvrajeti tā abhiprasthāpayann uvāca nāsahasreṇāvarteyeti sa ha varṣagaṇaṃ provāsa tā yadā sahasraṃ saṃpeduḥ || 5 ||
verses.trans: The teacher said: 'No one not a brāhmaṇa could speak out thus. Fetch the fuel, gentle one; I will initiate you — you have not departed from truth.' Having initiated him, he set apart four hundred lean and weak cows and said: 'Follow these, gentle one.' Driving them off, Satyakāma said: 'I shall not return without a thousand.' He lived away a number of years, until they had become a thousand.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Gautama's verdict is the Upaniṣad's: truthfulness itself is brāhmaṇa-hood. The cow-herding exile is the teacher's method — solitude, service, and patience ripen the student for the gods' instruction.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Initiation rewards truth; the long service of herding is purificatory discipline under the guru's command, during which grace matures.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The teacher recognises God-given fitness and assigns tapas befitting it; the vow of the thousand shows the student's resolve.

verses.dev: अथ हैनमृषभोऽभ्युवाद सत्यकाम३ इति भगव इति ह प्रतिशुश्राव प्राप्ताः सोम्य सहस्रं स्मः प्रापय न आचार्यकुलम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hainam ṛṣabho 'bhyuvāda satyakāma3 iti bhagava iti ha pratiśuśrāva prāptāḥ somya sahasraṃ smaḥ prāpaya na ācāryakulam || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then the bull (of the herd) addressed him: 'Satyakāma!' — 'Revered sir!' he answered. 'Gentle one, we have reached a thousand; lead us to the teacher's house.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Nature itself begins to teach the purified student: the bull is the deity Vāyu speaking through the herd, says Śaṅkara. When the vessel is ready, instruction comes from every side.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord, pleased with service, speaks through His creatures; the cosmos becomes the devotee's classroom.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Gods assume creaturely voices at Viṣṇu's bidding to instruct the deserving.

verses.dev: ब्रह्मणश्च ते पादं ब्रवाणीति ब्रवीतु मे भगवानिति तस्मै होवाच प्राची दिक्कला प्रतीची दिक्कला दक्षिणा दिक्कलोदीची दिक्कलैष वै सोम्य चतुष्कलः पादो ब्रह्मणः प्रकाशवान्नाम ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: brahmaṇaś ca te pādaṃ bravāṇīti bravītu me bhagavān iti tasmai hovāca prācī dik kalā pratīcī dik kalā dakṣiṇā dik kalodīcī dik kalaiṣa vai somya catuṣkalaḥ pādo brahmaṇaḥ prakāśavān nāma || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'And let me tell you one foot of Brahman.' — 'Tell me, revered sir.' He said to him: 'The eastern quarter is one sixteenth, the western quarter is one sixteenth, the southern quarter is one sixteenth, the northern quarter is one sixteenth. This, gentle one, is one foot of Brahman, consisting of four sixteenths, named the Shining (Prakāśavān).',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Brahman is taught in measured feet — first as the all-pervading quarters. The finite mind climbs by fractions toward the infinite whole.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The quarters are the Lord's pervasive presence; meditating on them as His 'shining foot' trains the soul in His omnipresence.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The directions manifest the Lord's governance; the graded teaching respects the student's capacity.

verses.dev: स य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मणः प्रकाशवानित्युपास्ते प्रकाशवानस्मिँल्लोके भवति प्रकाशवतो ह लोकाञ्जयति य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मणः प्रकाशवानित्युपास्ते ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇaḥ prakāśavān ity upāste prakāśavān asmiṁl loke bhavati prakāśavato ha lokāñ jayati ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇaḥ prakāśavān ity upāste || 3 ||
verses.trans: He who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Shining, becomes shining in this world and conquers shining worlds — he who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Shining.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The meditator partakes of what he meditates upon: contemplating Brahman's radiance, he becomes radiant here and hereafter.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord rewards each meditation in kind — His shining foot confers shining worlds.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Fruits exactly match the worship; the Lord is precise in His grace.

verses.dev: अग्निष्टे पादं वक्तेति स ह श्वोभूते गा अभिप्रस्थापयांचकार ता यत्राभि सायं बभूवुस्तत्राग्निमुपसमाधाय गा उपरुध्य समिधमाधाय पश्चादग्नेः प्राङुपोपविवेश ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: agniṣ ṭe pādaṃ vakteti sa ha śvobhūte gā abhiprasthāpayāṃ cakāra tā yatrābhi sāyaṃ babhūvus tatrāgnim upasamādhāya gā uparudhya samidham ādhāya paścād agneḥ prāṅ upopaviveśa || 1 ||
verses.trans: (The bull said:) 'The fire will tell you another foot.' The next morning he drove the cows onward, and where they came toward evening he kindled a fire, penned the cows, laid on fuel, and sat down behind the fire, facing east.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Each teacher hands the student to the next; the seeker's nightly fire-tending is itself the invocation that summons instruction.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Devout routine — fire, fuel, watchfulness — keeps the channel of grace open.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Agni, the Lord's priest, will speak to the one who serves the sacred fire.

verses.dev: तमग्निरभ्युवाद सत्यकाम३ इति भगव इति ह प्रतिशुश्राव ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tam agnir abhyuvāda satyakāma3 iti bhagava iti ha pratiśuśrāva || 2 ||
verses.trans: The fire addressed him: 'Satyakāma!' — 'Revered sir!' he answered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The element itself speaks: to the purified, all nature is articulate with Brahman.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord within fire calls His servant by name.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Agni instructs by Viṣṇu's commission.

verses.dev: ब्रह्मणः सोम्य ते पादं ब्रवाणीति ब्रवीतु मे भगवानिति तस्मै होवाच पृथिवी कलान्तरिक्षं कला द्यौः कला समुद्रः कलैष वै सोम्य चतुष्कलः पादो ब्रह्मणोऽनन्तवान्नाम ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: brahmaṇaḥ somya te pādaṃ bravāṇīti bravītu me bhagavān iti tasmai hovāca pṛthivī kalāntarikṣaṃ kalā dyauḥ kalā samudraḥ kalaiṣa vai somya catuṣkalaḥ pādo brahmaṇo 'nantavān nāma || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Gentle one, let me tell you one foot of Brahman.' — 'Tell me, revered sir.' He said to him: 'The earth is one sixteenth, the mid-region is one sixteenth, the sky is one sixteenth, the ocean is one sixteenth. This, gentle one, is one foot of Brahman, consisting of four sixteenths, named the Endless (Anantavān).'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The second foot gathers the world's vast expanses — Brahman as the limitless field of all regions.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Earth, air, sky, and sea are the Lord's endless body, taught as one foot of His immensity.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord's infinity is mirrored in the unbounded regions He upholds.

verses.dev: स य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मणोऽनन्तवानित्युपास्तेऽनन्तवानस्मिँल्लोके भवत्यनन्तवतो ह लोकाञ्जयति य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मणोऽनन्तवानित्युपास्ते ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇo 'nantavān ity upāste 'nantavān asmiṁl loke bhavaty anantavato ha lokāñ jayati ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇo 'nantavān ity upāste || 4 ||
verses.trans: He who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Endless, becomes endless in this world and conquers endless worlds — he who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Endless.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Contemplating limitlessness, the meditator outgrows his limits.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Endless foot bestows unending attainments by the Lord's measure.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fruit of endlessness is granted within the soul's own infinite capacity for bliss.

verses.dev: हंसस्ते पादं वक्तेति स ह श्वोभूते गा अभिप्रस्थापयांचकार ता यत्राभि सायं बभूवुस्तत्राग्निमुपसमाधाय गा उपरुध्य समिधमाधाय पश्चादग्नेः प्राङुपोपविवेश ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: haṃsas te pādaṃ vakteti sa ha śvobhūte gā abhiprasthāpayāṃ cakāra tā yatrābhi sāyaṃ babhūvus tatrāgnim upasamādhāya gā uparudhya samidham ādhāya paścād agneḥ prāṅ upopaviveśa || 1 ||
verses.trans: (The fire said:) 'The swan will tell you another foot.' The next morning he drove the cows onward, and where they came toward evening he kindled a fire, penned the cows, laid on fuel, and sat down behind the fire, facing east.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The relay of teachers continues; Śaṅkara identifies the swan as the sun in bird form.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each evening's discipline invites the next messenger of the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The sun-deity will teach in swan's guise, by divine arrangement.

verses.dev: तं हंस उपनिपत्याभ्युवाद सत्यकाम३ इति भगव इति ह प्रतिशुश्राव ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ haṃsa upanipatyābhyuvāda satyakāma3 iti bhagava iti ha pratiśuśrāva || 2 ||
verses.trans: A swan flew down to him and addressed him: 'Satyakāma!' — 'Revered sir!' he answered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The white swan, emblem of the discerning self, brings the doctrine of light.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's messenger descends to the watchful disciple.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Sūrya, servant of the Lord, alights to instruct.

verses.dev: ब्रह्मणः सोम्य ते पादं ब्रवाणीति ब्रवीतु मे भगवानिति तस्मै होवाचाग्निः कला सूर्यः कला चन्द्रः कला विद्युत्कलैष वै सोम्य चतुष्कलः पादो ब्रह्मणो ज्योतिष्मान्नाम ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: brahmaṇaḥ somya te pādaṃ bravāṇīti bravītu me bhagavān iti tasmai hovācāgniḥ kalā sūryaḥ kalā candraḥ kalā vidyut kalaiṣa vai somya catuṣkalaḥ pādo brahmaṇo jyotiṣmān nāma || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Gentle one, let me tell you one foot of Brahman.' — 'Tell me, revered sir.' He said to him: 'Fire is one sixteenth, the sun is one sixteenth, the moon is one sixteenth, lightning is one sixteenth. This, gentle one, is one foot of Brahman, consisting of four sixteenths, named the Luminous (Jyotiṣmān).'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The third foot collects the world's lights — Brahman as the source whose reflection all luminaries are.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The four lights are rays of the Lord's own splendour, taught as His luminous foot.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All lights borrow from the Light of lights, Viṣṇu, whom this foot intimates.

verses.dev: स य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मणो ज्योतिष्मानित्युपास्ते ज्योतिष्मानस्मिँल्लोके भवति ज्योतिष्मतो ह लोकाञ्जयति य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मणो ज्योतिष्मानित्युपास्ते ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇo jyotiṣmān ity upāste jyotiṣmān asmiṁl loke bhavati jyotiṣmato ha lokāñ jayati ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇo jyotiṣmān ity upāste || 4 ||
verses.trans: He who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Luminous, becomes luminous in this world and conquers luminous worlds — he who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Luminous.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Light meditated becomes light possessed.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's radiance illumines His meditator within and without.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Luminous worlds reward luminous worship, by the Lord's exact justice.

verses.dev: मद्गुष्टे पादं वक्तेति स ह श्वोभूते गा अभिप्रस्थापयांचकार ता यत्राभि सायं बभूवुस्तत्राग्निमुपसमाधाय गा उपरुध्य समिधमाधाय पश्चादग्नेः प्राङुपोपविवेश ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: madgus te pādaṃ vakteti sa ha śvobhūte gā abhiprasthāpayāṃ cakāra tā yatrābhi sāyaṃ babhūvus tatrāgnim upasamādhāya gā uparudhya samidham ādhāya paścād agneḥ prāṅ upopaviveśa || 1 ||
verses.trans: (The swan said:) 'The diver-bird (madgu) will tell you another foot.' The next morning he drove the cows onward, and where they came toward evening he kindled a fire, penned the cows, laid on fuel, and sat down behind the fire, facing east.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The last messenger is the water-bird — Śaṅkara identifies it with prāṇa, lord of the inner waters.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The fourth night of patient service brings the final cosmic tutor.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Vāyu-prāṇa in bird form completes the fourfold instruction.

verses.dev: तं मद्गुरुपनिपत्याभ्युवाद सत्यकाम३ इति भगव इति ह प्रतिशुश्राव ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ madgur upanipatyābhyuvāda satyakāma3 iti bhagava iti ha pratiśuśrāva || 2 ||
verses.trans: A diver-bird flew down to him and addressed him: 'Satyakāma!' — 'Revered sir!' he answered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The humble water-bird carries the inmost teaching — the senses and their home.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: No messenger of the Lord is small; the diver brings the foot nearest the soul.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The breath-deity speaks through the least imposing of forms.

verses.dev: ब्रह्मणः सोम्य ते पादं ब्रवाणीति ब्रवीतु मे भगवानिति तस्मै होवाच प्राणः कला चक्षुः कला श्रोत्रं कला मनः कलैष वै सोम्य चतुष्कलः पादो ब्रह्मण आयतनवान्नाम ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: brahmaṇaḥ somya te pādaṃ bravāṇīti bravītu me bhagavān iti tasmai hovāca prāṇaḥ kalā cakṣuḥ kalā śrotraṃ kalā manaḥ kalaiṣa vai somya catuṣkalaḥ pādo brahmaṇa āyatanavān nāma || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Gentle one, let me tell you one foot of Brahman.' — 'Tell me, revered sir.' He said to him: 'The breath is one sixteenth, the eye is one sixteenth, the ear is one sixteenth, the mind is one sixteenth. This, gentle one, is one foot of Brahman, consisting of four sixteenths, named the Abode-possessing (Āyatanavān).'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The final foot turns inward: breath, eye, ear, mind — the faculties in which Brahman has its nearest 'abode'. The teaching has travelled from the quarters of space to the centre of the person.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's most intimate foot is His indwelling in the soul's instruments; He is the abode of all and abides in all.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord dwells in the faculties as their controller; this foot teaches His inner rulership.

verses.dev: स य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मण आयतनवानित्युपास्त आयतनवानस्मिँल्लोके भवत्यायतनवतो ह लोकाञ्जयति य एतमेवं विद्वांश्चतुष्कलं पादं ब्रह्मण आयतनवानित्युपास्ते ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇa āyatanavān ity upāsta āyatanavān asmiṁl loke bhavaty āyatanavato ha lokāñ jayati ya etam evaṃ vidvāṃś catuṣkalaṃ pādaṃ brahmaṇa āyatanavān ity upāste || 4 ||
verses.trans: He who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Abode-possessing, becomes possessed of an abode in this world and conquers worlds with abodes — he who, knowing thus, meditates on this four-sixteenth foot of Brahman as the Abode-possessing.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The sixteen sixteenths are now complete: radiance, endlessness, light, and inner abode — Brahman taught whole through four cosmic teachers.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The fourfold vision rounds into fullness: the Lord pervading directions, regions, lights, and the inner person.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The graded curriculum is finished; the student now needs only the human teacher's seal.

verses.dev: प्राप हाचार्यकुलं तमाचार्योऽभ्युवाद सत्यकाम३ इति भगव इति ह प्रतिशुश्राव ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: prāpa hācāryakulaṃ tam ācāryo 'bhyuvāda satyakāma3 iti bhagava iti ha pratiśuśrāva || 1 ||
verses.trans: He reached the teacher's house. The teacher addressed him: 'Satyakāma!' — 'Revered sir!' he answered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The circle closes at the ācārya's door: cosmic instruction must still be ratified by the human teacher of the lineage.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Return to the guru completes obedience; grace honours the established channel.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: However exalted the interim teachers, the sampradāya's ācārya confirms the knowledge.

verses.dev: ब्रह्मविदिव वै सोम्य भासि को नु त्वानुशशासेत्यन्ये मनुष्येभ्य इति ह प्रतिजज्ञे भगवांस्त्वेव मे कामे ब्रूयात् ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: brahmavid iva vai somya bhāsi ko nu tvānuśaśāsety anye manuṣyebhya iti ha pratijajñe bhagavāṃs tv eva me kāme brūyāt || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Gentle one, you shine like a knower of Brahman. Who, then, has taught you?' — 'Others than men,' he confessed. 'But may it please you, revered sir, to teach me yourself —'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Knowledge transfigures the face; the teacher reads it at a glance. Yet the student, truthful as ever, still asks for the master's own word — the wish of the rightly humble.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Even taught by gods, the disciple seeks his ācārya's teaching: the human guru is the Lord's chosen face for the disciple.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Divine instruction does not dispense with the guru; the student's deference models right order.

verses.dev: श्रुतं ह्येव मे भगवद्दृशेभ्य आचार्याद्धैव विद्या विदिता साधिष्ठं प्रापतीति तस्मै हैतदेवोवाचात्र ह न किञ्चन वीयायेति वीयायेति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: śrutaṃ hy eva me bhagavaddṛśebhya ācāryād dhaiva vidyā viditā sādhiṣṭhaṃ prāpatīti tasmai haitad evovācātra ha na kiñcana vīyāyeti vīyāyeti || 3 ||
verses.trans: '— for I have heard from men like you, revered sir, that knowledge learned from the teacher best reaches its goal.' Then he taught him that very same knowledge, and nothing whatever was left out — nothing was left out.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara cherishes this maxim: ācāryād dhaiva vidyā viditā sādhiṣṭhaṃ prāpati — knowledge from the teacher's lips attains its end most surely. The guru's teaching added nothing new in content, yet completed everything in efficacy.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The school's whole theology of the ācārya rests here: the teacher's grace makes knowledge fruitful; without it, even divine instruction remains a seed unsown.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva affirms: the paramparā's confirmation perfects knowledge. The repetition 'nothing was left out' certifies the transmission whole and entire.

verses.dev: उपकोसलो ह वै कामलायनः सत्यकामे जाबाले ब्रह्मचर्यमुवास तस्य ह द्वादश वर्षाण्यग्नीन्परिचचार स ह स्मान्यानन्तेवासिनः समावर्तयंस्तं ह स्मैव न समावर्तयति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: upakosalo ha vai kāmalāyanaḥ satyakāme jābāle brahmacaryam uvāsa tasya ha dvādaśa varṣāṇy agnīn paricacāra sa ha smānyān antevāsinaḥ samāvartayaṃs taṃ ha smaiva na samāvartayati || 1 ||
verses.trans: Upakosala Kāmalāyana lived as a student of sacred knowledge with Satyakāma Jābāla. For twelve years he tended his teacher's fires; but though the teacher let other students return home, him he did not allow to depart.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Satyakāma, now himself a teacher, withholds graduation from his best student — Śaṅkara reads this as the teacher's deliberate ripening of an exceptional vessel: prolonged service burns away the last impurities before knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The delay is providential: the Lord arranges that Upakosala's teachers shall be the sacred fires themselves, a grace earned by selfless service.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Twelve years of fire-service is tapas appointed by the Lord through the guru; the apparent neglect is preparation for higher instruction.

verses.dev: तं जायोवाच तप्तो ब्रह्मचारी कुशलमग्नीन्परिचचारीन्मा त्वाग्नयः परिप्रवोचन्प्रब्रूह्यस्मा इति तस्मै हाप्रोच्यैव प्रवासांचक्रे ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ jāyovāca tapto brahmacārī kuśalam agnīn paricacārīn mā tvāgnayaḥ paripravocan prabrūhy asmā iti tasmai hāprocyaiva pravāsāṃ cakre || 2 ||
verses.trans: His wife said to him: 'The student is austere and has tended the fires well; teach him, lest the fires reproach you.' But without teaching him, the teacher went away on a journey.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even the household sees the student's worth; the teacher's silence and departure clear the stage for the fires to speak — knowledge arrives by the way providence chooses.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The wife's compassion voices dharma; the teacher's strange silence serves the Lord's deeper plan for the disciple.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord moves through all the actors — wife, teacher, fires — to bring the fit soul its destined teaching.

verses.dev: स ह व्याधिनानशितुं दध्रे तमाचार्यजायोवाच ब्रह्मचारिन्नशान किं नु नाश्नासीति स होवाच बहव इमेऽस्मिन्पुरुषे कामा नानात्यया व्याधीभिः प्रतिपूर्णोऽस्मि नाशिष्यामीति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ha vyādhinānaśituṃ dadhre tam ācāryajāyovāca brahmacārinn aśāna kiṃ nu nāśnāsīti sa hovāca bahava ime 'smin puruṣe kāmā nānātyayā vyādhībhiḥ pratipūrṇo 'smi nāśiṣyāmīti || 3 ||
verses.trans: Out of grief he resolved to fast. The teacher's wife said to him: 'Student, eat! Why do you not eat?' He said: 'In this man (in me) there are many desires turned in different directions; I am full of afflictions. I will not eat.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The student's sorrow is the holy discontent (vairāgya-born grief) that precedes illumination; his fast is the soul's refusal of everything short of truth.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: His confession of inner turmoil is honest self-surrender; such transparency before the Lord's household invites divine intervention.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Genuine longing for knowledge eclipses bodily wants; the Lord hastens to those who hunger thus.

verses.dev: अथ हाग्नयः समूदिरे तप्तो ब्रह्मचारी कुशलं नः पर्यचारीद्धन्तास्मै प्रब्रवामेति तस्मै होचुः प्राणो ब्रह्म कं ब्रह्म खं ब्रह्मेति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: atha hāgnayaḥ samūdire tapto brahmacārī kuśalaṃ naḥ paryacārīd dhantāsmai prabravāmeti tasmai hocuḥ prāṇo brahma kaṃ brahma khaṃ brahmeti || 4 ||
verses.trans: Then the fires said among themselves: 'The student is austere and has tended us well. Come, let us teach him.' And they said to him: 'Breath is Brahman; joy (ka) is Brahman; space (kha) is Brahman.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fires, moved by service, deliver the seed-teaching: Brahman as life, as bliss, as the subtle expanse. Śaṅkara notes that 'ka' (joy) and 'kha' (space) qualify each other — Brahman is the bliss that is not worldly pleasure and the space that is not mere element.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Agni and his fellow fires are the Lord's priests; their triple formula names Him as the soul's life, its joy, and its boundless ground.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fires teach what they know under Viṣṇu: He is the breath of all, the bliss of all, the inner firmament — three names of one Lord.

verses.dev: स होवाच विजानाम्यहं यत्प्राणो ब्रह्म कं च तु खं च न विजानामीति ते होचुर्यद्वाव कं तदेव खं यदेव खं तदेव कमिति प्राणं च हास्मै तदाकाशं चोचुः ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: sa hovāca vijānāmy ahaṃ yat prāṇo brahma kaṃ ca tu khaṃ ca na vijānāmīti te hocur yad vāva kaṃ tad eva khaṃ yad eva khaṃ tad eva kam iti prāṇaṃ ca hāsmai tad ākāśaṃ cocuḥ || 5 ||
verses.trans: He said: 'I understand that breath is Brahman; but joy and space I do not understand.' They said: 'What is joy — that indeed is space; and what is space — that indeed is joy.' Thus they taught him breath and the space (within the heart).

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The mutual definition is decisive for Śaṅkara: the bliss meant is not sensory (which would be fleeting), and the space meant is not physical (which would be insentient). Each corrects the other — Brahman is bliss-as-vastness, the ānanda that is ākāśa of the heart.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Joy and space interpenetrate in the Lord: His joy is unbounded as space, His pervasiveness is blissful — the heart-space where He dwells is the place of delight.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the Lord called 'ka-kha' is bliss itself and the support of space; the equation teaches His attributes, not an impersonal substrate.

verses.dev: अथ हैनं गार्हपत्योऽनुशशास पृथिव्यग्निरन्नमादित्य इति य एष आदित्ये पुरुषो दृश्यते सोऽहमस्मि स एवाहमस्मीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hainaṃ gārhapatyo 'nuśaśāsa pṛthivy agnir annam āditya iti ya eṣa āditye puruṣo dṛśyate so 'ham asmi sa evāham asmīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then the Gārhapatya fire instructed him: 'Earth, fire, food, and the sun (are my forms). The Person seen in the sun — I am he; indeed, I am he.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The household fire discloses its cosmic identity, culminating in the Person in the sun — each fire is a thread leading from the ritual hearth to the universal Light.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The fire's 'I am he' declares the one Lord indwelling hearth and sun alike; worship at home touches the Person in the heavens.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fire identifies its empowering deity — the Lord's presence in the sun — never itself as independent.

verses.dev: स य एतमेवं विद्वानुपास्तेऽपहते पापकृत्यां लोकी भवति सर्वमायुरेति ज्योग्जीवति नास्यावरपुरुषाः क्षीयन्त उप वयं तं भुञ्जामोऽस्मिंश्च लोकेऽमुष्मिंश्च य एतमेवं विद्वानुपास्ते ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etam evaṃ vidvān upāste 'pahate pāpakṛtyāṃ lokī bhavati sarvam āyur eti jyog jīvati nāsyāvarapuruṣāḥ kṣīyanta upa vayaṃ taṃ bhuñjāmo 'smiṃś ca loke 'muṣmiṃś ca ya etam evaṃ vidvān upāste || 2 ||
verses.trans: He who, knowing thus, meditates on it, drives away evil deeds, wins a world, reaches a full span of life, and lives long; his descendants do not perish. 'We support him in this world and in the other' — him who, knowing thus, meditates on it.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fires pledge their protection in both worlds; meditational fruits accompany the seeker until knowledge completes itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's ministers guarantee welfare here and hereafter to the devoted meditator.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Blessings flow through the fire by the Lord's sanction — long life, posterity, and support beyond death.

verses.dev: अथ हैनमन्वाहार्यपचनोऽनुशशासापो दिशो नक्षत्राणि चन्द्रमा इति य एष चन्द्रमसि पुरुषो दृश्यते सोऽहमस्मि स एवाहमस्मीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hainam anvāhāryapacano 'nuśaśāsāpo diśo nakṣatrāṇi candramā iti ya eṣa candramasi puruṣo dṛśyate so 'ham asmi sa evāham asmīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then the Anvāhāryapacana (southern) fire instructed him: 'Water, the quarters, the stars, and the moon (are my forms). The Person seen in the moon — I am he; indeed, I am he.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The southern fire unfolds the watery, lunar line of the cosmos; the Person in the moon is the same Light under a second cosmic mask.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: One indweller shines in sun and moon; the fires teach His many stations.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each fire names the Lord's presence in its own cosmic chain — moon as in sun.

verses.dev: स य एतमेवं विद्वानुपास्तेऽपहते पापकृत्यां लोकी भवति सर्वमायुरेति ज्योग्जीवति नास्यावरपुरुषाः क्षीयन्त उप वयं तं भुञ्जामोऽस्मिंश्च लोकेऽमुष्मिंश्च य एतमेवं विद्वानुपास्ते ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etam evaṃ vidvān upāste 'pahate pāpakṛtyāṃ lokī bhavati sarvam āyur eti jyog jīvati nāsyāvarapuruṣāḥ kṣīyanta upa vayaṃ taṃ bhuñjāmo 'smiṃś ca loke 'muṣmiṃś ca ya etam evaṃ vidvān upāste || 2 ||
verses.trans: He who, knowing thus, meditates on it, drives away evil deeds, wins a world, reaches a full span of life, and lives long; his descendants do not perish. 'We support him in this world and in the other' — him who, knowing thus, meditates on it.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The identical refrain — each fire gives the same pledge, for the goal of every chain is one.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The repeated promise shows the uniform generosity of the Lord through all His agents.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Uniform fruits from different fires confirm a single granting Lord behind them.

verses.dev: अथ हैनमाहवनीयोऽनुशशास प्राण आकाशो द्यौर्विद्युदिति य एष विद्युति पुरुषो दृश्यते सोऽहमस्मि स एवाहमस्मीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hainam āhavanīyo 'nuśaśāsa prāṇa ākāśo dyaur vidyud iti ya eṣa vidyuti puruṣo dṛśyate so 'ham asmi sa evāham asmīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then the Āhavanīya fire instructed him: 'Breath, space, heaven, and lightning (are my forms). The Person seen in lightning — I am he; indeed, I am he.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The offering fire completes the triad with the subtlest line — breath, space, heaven, lightning: the Person flashes nearest to the formless here.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord in lightning is His swiftest, most dazzling station; the fires together have shown Him above, below, and between.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Lightning's Person is the same Lord; the three fires have taught His threefold cosmic majesty.

verses.dev: स य एतमेवं विद्वानुपास्तेऽपहते पापकृत्यां लोकी भवति सर्वमायुरेति ज्योग्जीवति नास्यावरपुरुषाः क्षीयन्त उप वयं तं भुञ्जामोऽस्मिंश्च लोकेऽमुष्मिंश्च य एतमेवं विद्वानुपास्ते ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etam evaṃ vidvān upāste 'pahate pāpakṛtyāṃ lokī bhavati sarvam āyur eti jyog jīvati nāsyāvarapuruṣāḥ kṣīyanta upa vayaṃ taṃ bhuñjāmo 'smiṃś ca loke 'muṣmiṃś ca ya etam evaṃ vidvān upāste || 2 ||
verses.trans: He who, knowing thus, meditates on it, drives away evil deeds, wins a world, reaches a full span of life, and lives long; his descendants do not perish. 'We support him in this world and in the other' — him who, knowing thus, meditates on it.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Third pledge of the third fire: the meditations protect, but the gati — the final way — remains to be told by the teacher.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The fires bless; the human ācārya will crown their teaching with the path to the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Welfare assured, the disciple still awaits the saving doctrine from his guru.

verses.dev: ते होचुरुपकोसलैषा सोम्य तेऽस्मद्विद्यात्मविद्या चाचार्यस्तु ते गतिं वक्तेत्याजगाम हास्याचार्यस्तमाचार्योऽभ्युवादोपकोसल३ इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: te hocur upakosalaiṣā somya te 'smadvidyātmavidyā cācāryas tu te gatiṃ vaktety ājagāma hāsyācāryas tam ācāryo 'bhyuvādopakosala3 iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: The fires said: 'Upakosala, gentle one, this is our knowledge and the knowledge of the Self; but the teacher will tell you the way.' Then his teacher returned, and the teacher addressed him: 'Upakosala!'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fires themselves defer to the ācārya for the gati — the path. Even divine teachers honour the human lineage through which knowledge is sealed.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Cosmic instruction is incomplete without the ācārya's word on the way to the Lord — the school's cardinal principle.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fires teach truths; the guru alone, by the Lord's order, bestows the route of ascent.

verses.dev: भगव इति ह प्रतिशुश्राव ब्रह्मविद इव सोम्य ते मुखं भाति को नु त्वानुशशासेति को नु मानुशिष्याद्भो इतीहापेव निह्नुत इमे नूनमीदृशा अन्यादृशा इतीहाग्नीनभ्यूदे किं नु सोम्य किल तेऽवोचन्निति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: bhagava iti ha pratiśuśrāva brahmavida iva somya te mukhaṃ bhāti ko nu tvānuśaśāseti ko nu mānuśiṣyād bho itīhāpeva nihnuta ime nūnam īdṛśā anyādṛśā itīhāgnīn abhyūde kiṃ nu somya kila te 'vocann iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Revered sir!' he answered. 'Gentle one, your face shines like one who knows Brahman. Who has taught you?' — 'Who indeed should teach me, sir?' he said, as if denying; and he glanced toward the fires, as if to say: 'these were thus, though now otherwise.' — 'What, gentle one, did they tell you?'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Again knowledge shows in the face. The student's delicate honesty — neither boasting of divine teachers nor lying — and his glance at the fires display perfect discipleship.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The radiant face is grace made visible; the student's modesty leaves the final word with his ācārya.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Truthful even in reticence, the disciple credits the fires while submitting all to the guru's judgment.

verses.dev: इदमिति ह प्रतिजज्ञे लोकान्वाव किल सोम्य तेऽवोचन्नहं तु ते तद्वक्ष्यामि यथा पुष्करपलाश आपो न श्लिष्यन्त एवमेवंविदि पापं कर्म न श्लिष्यत इति ब्रवीतु मे भगवानिति तस्मै होवाच ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: idam iti ha pratijajñe lokān vāva kila somya te 'vocann ahaṃ tu te tad vakṣyāmi yathā puṣkarapalāśa āpo na śliṣyanta evam evaṃvidi pāpaṃ karma na śliṣyata iti bravītu me bhagavān iti tasmai hovāca || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'This,' he confessed (telling what they had taught). 'They told you only the worlds, gentle one; but I will tell you that (knowledge), knowing which, as water does not cling to the lotus leaf, so no evil deed clings to the knower.' — 'Tell me, revered sir.' And he said to him:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara treasures the lotus-leaf image: action leaves no residue on the knower of the Self, for he no longer identifies with the doer. The fires gave worlds; the teacher gives that which frees from all worlds.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The teacher promises the vidyā that renders karma powerless — the soul surrendered to the Lord is untouched by sin, as the lotus leaf by water (the Gītā echoes this).
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: by the Lord's grace won through right knowledge, accrued karma slides off the devotee; the guru now teaches what the fires could not.

verses.dev: य एषोऽक्षिणि पुरुषो दृश्यत एष आत्मेति होवाचैतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्मेति तद्यद्यप्यस्मिन्सर्पिर्वोदकं वा सिञ्चति वर्त्मनी एव गच्छति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: ya eṣo 'kṣiṇi puruṣo dṛśyata eṣa ātmeti hovācaitad amṛtam abhayam etad brahmeti tad yady apy asmin sarpir vodakaṃ vā siñcati vartmanī eva gacchati || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'The Person seen in the eye — this is the Self,' he said; 'this is the immortal, the fearless; this is Brahman. Even if one drops clarified butter or water into the eye, it flows away to the edges (leaving the eye unstained).'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The seer in the eye — the witness of seeing — is the Self, immortal and fearless. As the eye is unstained by what falls on it, the witness is untouched by experience: Śaṅkara's core teaching of the asaṅga (unattached) Self.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Person in the eye is the Lord dwelling within the faculty of sight, immaculate as the eye that nothing can stain; meditation on Him there is the heart of this vidyā.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva identifies the akṣi-puruṣa as Viṣṇu seated in the eye — immortal, fearless, untouched — the object of the disciple's worship, distinct from the soul who sees by His light.

verses.dev: एतं संयद्वाम इत्याचक्षत एतं हि सर्वाणि वामान्यभिसंयन्ति सर्वाण्येनं वामान्यभिसंयन्ति य एवं वेद ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: etaṃ saṃyadvāma ity ācakṣata etaṃ hi sarvāṇi vāmāny abhisaṃyanti sarvāṇy enaṃ vāmāny abhisaṃyanti ya evaṃ veda || 2 ||
verses.trans: They call him Saṃyadvāma (the gatherer of blessings), for all blessings go toward him. All blessings go toward him who knows thus.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: All excellences converge on the Self, the source of every value; the knower shares this convergence.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Every auspicious thing streams toward the Lord in the eye; His meditator becomes a confluence of blessings.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord is the meeting-point of all good; His worshipper receives what flows to Him.

verses.dev: एष उ एव वामनीरेष हि सर्वाणि वामानि नयति सर्वाणि वामानि नयति य एवं वेद ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: eṣa u eva vāmanīr eṣa hi sarvāṇi vāmāni nayati sarvāṇi vāmāni nayati ya evaṃ veda || 3 ||
verses.trans: He is also Vāmanī (the leader of blessings), for he leads all blessings. He who knows thus leads all blessings.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Self not only receives but distributes all good — and so does the one who knows it.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord conducts blessings to all beings; His devotee becomes a channel of that bounty.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Viṣṇu alone dispenses every excellence; the knower shares in His distribution.

verses.dev: एष उ एव भामनीरेष हि सर्वेषु लोकेषु भाति सर्वेषु लोकेषु भाति य एवं वेद ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: eṣa u eva bhāmanīr eṣa hi sarveṣu lokeṣu bhāti sarveṣu lokeṣu bhāti ya evaṃ veda || 4 ||
verses.trans: He is also Bhāmanī (the shining one), for he shines in all worlds. He who knows thus shines in all worlds.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Self is the light of all worlds; its knower is luminous everywhere — the threefold naming completes the meditation on the eye-Person.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's splendour fills the worlds, and His meditator radiates with borrowed glory.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All cosmic light is the Lord's; the devotee reflects it in every world he attains.

verses.dev: अथ यदु चैवास्मिञ्शव्यं कुर्वन्ति यदि च नार्चिषमेवाभिसंभवन्त्यर्चिषोऽहरह्न आपूर्यमाणपक्षमापूर्यमाणपक्षाद्यान्षडुदङ्ङेति मासांस्तान्मासेभ्यः संवत्सरं संवत्सरादादित्यमादित्याच्चन्द्रमसं चन्द्रमसो विद्युतं तत्पुरुषोऽमानवः स एनान्ब्रह्म गमयत्येष देवपथो ब्रह्मपथ एतेन प्रतिपद्यमाना इमं मानवमावर्तं नावर्तन्ते नावर्तन्ते ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: atha yad u caivāsmiñ śavyaṃ kurvanti yadi ca nārciṣam evābhisaṃbhavanty arciṣo 'har ahna āpūryamāṇapakṣam āpūryamāṇapakṣād yān ṣaḍ udaṅṅ eti māsāṃs tān māsebhyaḥ saṃvatsaraṃ saṃvatsarād ādityam ādityāc candramasaṃ candramaso vidyutaṃ tat puruṣo 'mānavaḥ sa enān brahma gamayaty eṣa devapatho brahmapatha etena pratipadyamānā imaṃ mānavam āvartaṃ nāvartante nāvartante || 5 ||
verses.trans: Now, whether or not funeral rites are performed for such men, they pass into the flame; from the flame into the day; from the day into the bright fortnight; from the bright fortnight into the six months when the sun moves north; from the months into the year; from the year into the sun; from the sun into the moon; from the moon into the lightning. There a Person who is not human leads them to Brahman. This is the path of the gods, the path to Brahman. Those who proceed by it do not return to this human whirlpool — they do not return.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The devayāna — the path of light — carries the meditator stage by stage to brahmaloka, whence, says Śaṅkara, there is no return: liberation follows there in due course (krama-mukti). For the fully awakened, however, no path is needed at all; the path belongs to meditators on the qualified Brahman.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Rāmānuja's tradition reads this as the arcirādi-mārga in full: the amānava-puruṣa, the Lord's own messenger, takes the freed soul by the hand to the supreme abode, from which there is no return to saṃsāra — the charter text of Śrīvaiṣṇava eschatology.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the path of light is real and final for the qualified devotee; the non-human Person is the Lord's deputy who conducts the soul to Viṣṇu's world. The double negation — 'they do not return' — guarantees eternal residence with the Lord.

verses.dev: एष ह वै यज्ञो योऽयं पवते एष ह यन्निदं सर्वं पुनाति यदेष यन्निदं सर्वं पुनाति तस्मादेष एव यज्ञस्तस्य मनश्च वाक्च वर्तनी ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: eṣa ha vai yajño yo 'yaṃ pavate eṣa ha yann idaṃ sarvaṃ punāti yad eṣa yann idaṃ sarvaṃ punāti tasmād eṣa eva yajñas tasya manaś ca vāk ca vartanī || 1 ||
verses.trans: That which purifies (the wind that blows) is verily the sacrifice; for moving along, it purifies all this. Because moving along it purifies all this, it is indeed the sacrifice. Its two tracks are mind and speech.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The ever-moving wind is the cosmic sacrifice, and the rite on earth runs on two wheels — mind and speech. Śaṅkara: inner contemplation and outer utterance must move together for the rite to be whole.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The purifying wind is the Lord's breath as cosmic liturgy; the worshipper's mind and tongue are its twin rails — both to be offered to Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Vāyu, the great purifier, embodies the sacrifice under Viṣṇu; mind and speech are the channels by which the rite reaches the Lord.

verses.dev: तयोरन्यतरां मनसा संस्करोति ब्रह्मा वाचा होताध्वर्युरुद्गातान्यतरां स यत्रौपाकृते प्रातरनुवाके पुरा परिधानीयाया ब्रह्मा व्यवदति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tayor anyatarāṃ manasā saṃskaroti brahmā vācā hotādhvaryur udgātānyatarāṃ sa yatraupākṛte prātaranuvāke purā paridhānīyāyā brahmā vyavadati || 2 ||
verses.trans: Of these two tracks, the Brahman priest perfects one with his mind; the Hotṛ, Adhvaryu, and Udgātṛ perfect the other with speech. If, after the morning litany has begun and before the closing verse, the Brahman priest breaks his silence —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Brahman priest's office is silence — the mental track. His untimely speech derails the inner wheel of the rite.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The silent overseer keeps the mind-track for the Lord; speech out of season abandons his post.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each priest's duty is fixed by divine ordinance; the silent guardian must not desert the mental rail.

verses.dev: अन्यतरामेव वर्तनीं संस्करोति हीयतेऽन्यतरा स यथैकपाद्व्रजन्रथो वैकेन चक्रेण वर्तमानो रिष्यत्येवमस्य यज्ञो रिष्यति यज्ञं रिष्यन्तं यजमानोऽनुरिष्यति स इष्ट्वा पापीयान्भवति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: anyatarām eva vartanīṃ saṃskaroti hīyate 'nyatarā sa yathaikapād vrajan ratho vaikena cakreṇa vartamāno riṣyaty evam asya yajño riṣyati yajñaṃ riṣyantaṃ yajamāno 'nuriṣyati sa iṣṭvā pāpīyān bhavati || 3 ||
verses.trans: — then only one track is perfected and the other is lost. As a one-legged man walking, or a chariot running on one wheel, comes to harm, so his sacrifice comes to harm; and when the sacrifice is harmed, the sacrificer is harmed after it. Having sacrificed (thus), he becomes worse.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The one-wheeled chariot is Śaṅkara's emblem of ritual without inwardness: outer performance unsupported by mind injures rather than uplifts.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Worship of the Lord must run on devotion and utterance together; a rite without the mind's offering limps and wounds its performer.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Negligence in the appointed order turns merit to demerit; the Lord receives only the complete offering.

verses.dev: अथ यत्रोपाकृते प्रातरनुवाके न पुरा परिधानीयाया ब्रह्मा व्यवदत्युभे एव वर्तनी संस्कुर्वन्ति न हीयतेऽन्यतरा ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: atha yatraupākṛte prātaranuvāke na purā paridhānīyāyā brahmā vyavadaty ubhe eva vartanī saṃskurvanti na hīyate 'nyatarā || 4 ||
verses.trans: But when, after the morning litany has begun and before the closing verse, the Brahman priest does not break his silence, then both tracks are perfected and neither is lost.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Kept silence keeps the mind-wheel turning; the rite runs whole on both rails.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Discipline of silence preserves the inner offering alongside the outer hymn.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Duty fully kept makes the sacrifice whole before the Lord.

verses.dev: स यथोभयपाद्व्रजन्रथो वोभाभ्यां चक्राभ्यां वर्तमानः प्रतितिष्ठत्येवमस्य यज्ञः प्रतितिष्ठति यज्ञं प्रतितिष्ठन्तं यजमानोऽनुप्रतितिष्ठति स इष्ट्वा श्रेयान्भवति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: sa yathobhayapād vrajan ratho vobhābhyāṃ cakrābhyāṃ vartamānaḥ pratitiṣṭhaty evam asya yajñaḥ pratitiṣṭhati yajñaṃ pratitiṣṭhantaṃ yajamāno 'nupratitiṣṭhati sa iṣṭvā śreyān bhavati || 5 ||
verses.trans: As a man walking on both legs, or a chariot running on both wheels, stands firm, so his sacrifice stands firm; and when the sacrifice stands firm, the sacrificer stands firm after it. Having sacrificed (thus), he becomes better.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Mind and speech together establish the rite — and, beyond ritual, the seeker whose thought and word agree is established in truth.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The two-wheeled rite carries the worshipper steadily toward the Lord's favour.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Complete performance brings firm standing — prosperity here and grace hereafter.

verses.dev: प्रजापतिर्लोकानभ्यतपत्तेषां तप्यमानानां रसान्प्रावृहदग्निं पृथिव्या वायुमन्तरिक्षादादित्यं दिवः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: prajāpatir lokān abhyatapat teṣāṃ tapyamānānāṃ rasān prāvṛhad agniṃ pṛthivyā vāyum antarikṣād ādityaṃ divaḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: Prajāpati brooded over the worlds; from them, thus brooded over, he extracted their essences: fire from the earth, wind from the mid-region, the sun from the sky.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Creation by tapas: the Creator distils the luminous essence of each world — the first step of a triple distillation ending in the vyāhṛtis.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord as Prajāpati draws forth the powers of His own cosmic body for the welfare of the rite.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Viṣṇu through Prajāpati extracts the world-essences — all potency descends from Him by stages.

verses.dev: स एतास्तिस्रो देवता अभ्यतपत्तासां तप्यमानानां रसान्प्रावृहदग्नेरृचो वायोर्यजूंषि सामान्यादित्यात् ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa etās tisro devatā abhyatapat tāsāṃ tapyamānānāṃ rasān prāvṛhad agner ṛco vāyor yajūṃṣi sāmāny ādityāt || 2 ||
verses.trans: He brooded over these three deities; from them, thus brooded over, he extracted their essences: the Ṛg verses from fire, the Yajus formulas from wind, the Sāman chants from the sun.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The three Vedas are the essences of the three lights — scripture itself is distilled cosmos.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's word arises from His own radiant manifestations; Veda and world are one fabric of His glory.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The eternal Vedas are manifested through the deities in order — their authority roots in the Lord.

verses.dev: स एतां त्रयीं विद्यामभ्यतपत्तस्यास्तप्यमानाया रसान्प्रावृहद्भूरित्यृग्भ्यो भुवरिति यजुर्भ्यः स्वरिति सामभ्यः ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa etāṃ trayīṃ vidyām abhyatapat tasyās tapyamānāyā rasān prāvṛhad bhūr ity ṛgbhyo bhuvar iti yajurbhyaḥ svar iti sāmabhyaḥ || 3 ||
verses.trans: He brooded over the threefold knowledge; from it, thus brooded over, he extracted its essences: Bhūḥ from the Ṛg verses, Bhuvaḥ from the Yajus formulas, Svaḥ from the Sāman chants.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The three vyāhṛtis are the Veda's final essence — three syllabic drops holding the whole of revelation, as the Self holds the world.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The sacred utterances are the concentrated power of the Lord's word, His grace in portable form.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The vyāhṛtis carry the Veda's full potency by the Lord's design — medicine prepared in advance for the rite's wounds.

verses.dev: तद्यदृक्तो रिष्येद्भूः स्वाहेति गार्हपत्ये जुहुयादृचामेव तद्रसेनर्चां वीर्येणर्चां यज्ञस्य विरिष्टं संदधाति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tad yad ṛkto riṣyed bhūḥ svāheti gārhapatye juhuyād ṛcām eva tad rasenarcāṃ vīryeṇarcāṃ yajñasya viriṣṭaṃ saṃdadhāti || 4 ||
verses.trans: If the sacrifice is injured on the side of the Ṛg verses, one should offer in the Gārhapatya fire with 'Bhūḥ svāhā!' Thus by the essence and power of the Ṛg verses themselves one mends the Ṛg-side injury of the sacrifice.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Each wound is healed by the essence of its own limb — like cures like at the level of sacred sound.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord has appointed a remedy for every ritual failing; His mercy anticipates human error.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The expiation works by the Veda's inherent power, deployed exactly as ordained.

verses.dev: स यदि यजुष्टो रिष्येद्भुवः स्वाहेति दक्षिणाग्नौ जुहुयाद्यजुषामेव तद्रसेन यजुषां वीर्येण यजुषां यज्ञस्य विरिष्टं संदधाति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: sa yadi yajuṣṭo riṣyed bhuvaḥ svāheti dakṣiṇāgnau juhuyād yajuṣām eva tad rasena yajuṣāṃ vīryeṇa yajuṣāṃ yajñasya viriṣṭaṃ saṃdadhāti || 5 ||
verses.trans: If it is injured on the side of the Yajus formulas, one should offer in the southern fire with 'Bhuvaḥ svāhā!' Thus by the essence and power of the Yajus formulas themselves one mends the Yajus-side injury of the sacrifice.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The second wound, the second balm — the symmetry of the healing doctrine continues.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each fire receives its own remedial utterance, the Lord's order pervading even repair.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Precision in expiation honours the Lord's exact ordinances.

verses.dev: अथ यदि सामतो रिष्येत्स्वः स्वाहेत्याहवनीये जुहुयात्साम्नामेव तद्रसेन साम्नां वीर्येण साम्नां यज्ञस्य विरिष्टं संदधाति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi sāmato riṣyet svaḥ svāhety āhavanīye juhuyāt sāmnām eva tad rasena sāmnāṃ vīryeṇa sāmnāṃ yajñasya viriṣṭaṃ saṃdadhāti || 6 ||
verses.trans: And if it is injured on the side of the Sāman chants, one should offer in the Āhavanīya fire with 'Svaḥ svāhā!' Thus by the essence and power of the Sāman chants themselves one mends the Sāman-side injury of the sacrifice.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The third balm completes the pharmacy of the vyāhṛtis.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Threefold injury, threefold mercy — the Lord leaves no breach without remedy.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All three Vedas' wounds find cure in their distilled essences.

verses.dev: तद्यथा लवणेन सुवर्णं संदध्यात्सुवर्णेन रजतं रजतेन त्रपु त्रपुणा सीसं सीसेन लोहं लोहेन दारु दारु चर्मणा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: tad yathā lavaṇena suvarṇaṃ saṃdadhyāt suvarṇena rajataṃ rajatena trapu trapuṇā sīsaṃ sīsena lohaṃ lohena dāru dāru carmaṇā || 7 ||
verses.trans: As one would join gold with borax-salt, silver with gold, tin with silver, lead with tin, iron with lead, wood with iron, or wood with leather —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The metallurgical simile: every break has its proper solder. So the vyāhṛtis solder the broken rite.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The craftsman's chain of joints images the Lord's graded remedies for every grade of fault.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Gradation even among solders — Madhva sees the hierarchic principle everywhere confirmed.

verses.dev: एवमेषां लोकानामासां देवतानामस्यास्त्रय्या विद्याया वीर्येण यज्ञस्य विरिष्टं संदधाति भेषजकृतो ह वा एष यज्ञो यत्रैवंविद्ब्रह्मा भवति ॥ ८ ॥
verses.iast: evam eṣāṃ lokānām āsāṃ devatānām asyās trayyā vidyāyā vīryeṇa yajñasya viriṣṭaṃ saṃdadhāti bheṣajakṛto ha vā eṣa yajño yatraivaṃvid brahmā bhavati || 8 ||
verses.trans: — even so does one mend the injury of the sacrifice with the power of these worlds, these deities, and the threefold knowledge. That sacrifice, verily, is healed by medicine where the Brahman priest is one who knows thus.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The knowing priest is the rite's physician: knowledge, not mere repetition, heals. Śaṅkara generalises — in all things it is understanding that restores.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: A knower seated as Brahman priest makes the whole rite medicine; the Lord honours knowledge above mechanism.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The rite prospers where its overseer knows the Lord's science of essences.

verses.dev: एष ह वा उदक्प्रवणो यज्ञो यत्रैवंविद्ब्रह्मा भवत्येवंविदं ह वा एषा ब्रह्माणमनुगाथा यतो यत आवर्तते तत्तद्गच्छति ॥ ९ ॥
verses.iast: eṣa ha vā udakpravaṇo yajño yatraivaṃvid brahmā bhavaty evaṃvidaṃ ha vā eṣā brahmāṇam anugāthā yato yata āvartate tat tad gacchati || 9 ||
verses.trans: That sacrifice inclines northward (toward the path of the gods) where the Brahman priest is one who knows thus. About such a knowing Brahman priest there is this song: 'Wherever it turns back, thither it (the rite, guarded) goes (aright).'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: With knowledge at the helm, even the rite's reversals turn to good; the northward slope points it toward light.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The knower's presence orients the whole sacrifice toward the Lord's realm.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Under a knowing guardian every turn of the rite lands true, by the Lord's steering.

verses.dev: मानवो ब्रह्मैवैक ऋत्विक्कुरूनश्वाभिरक्षत्येवंविद्ध वै ब्रह्मा यज्ञं यजमानं सर्वांश्चर्त्विजोऽभिरक्षति तस्मादेवंविदमेव ब्रह्माणं कुर्वीत नानेवंविदं नानेवंविदम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.iast: mānavo brahmaivaika ṛtvik kurūn aśvābhirakṣaty evaṃvid dha vai brahmā yajñaṃ yajamānaṃ sarvāṃś cartvijo 'bhirakṣati tasmād evaṃvidam eva brahmāṇaṃ kurvīta nānevaṃvidaṃ nānevaṃvidam || 10 ||
verses.trans: 'The silent Brahman priest, like a mare protecting (her rider among) the Kurus, is the one priest (who guards the rite).' The knowing Brahman priest protects the sacrifice, the sacrificer, and all the priests. Therefore one should make only such a knower one's Brahman priest — not one who does not know, not one who does not know.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The closing injunction, doubled for emphasis: entrust the rite — and by extension, one's spiritual welfare — only to the one who knows. Knowledge protects all who shelter under it.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The knower-priest is the rite's guardian as the ācārya is the soul's; choose guides who know the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: authority belongs to knowledge alone; the repetition forbids ever installing the ignorant in the seat of the knower. Chapter 4 ends with the supremacy of the knowing guardian.

verses.dev: यो ह वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च ह वै श्रेष्ठश्च भवति प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yo ha vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca ha vai śreṣṭhaś ca bhavati prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca || 1 ||
verses.trans: He who knows the eldest and the best becomes himself the eldest and the best. The breath, verily, is the eldest and the best.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Prāṇa precedes the senses in the embryo (eldest) and excels them in function (best); Śaṅkara reads the vidyā as a step toward the Self, of which prāṇa is the immediate vesture.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The breath's primacy mirrors the Lord's: as prāṇa is first among powers, the Lord is first of all; knowing the true order confers its dignity.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: For Madhva this is a signature text: prāṇa (Mukhya-prāṇa, Vāyu) is the eldest and best of all souls, Viṣṇu's foremost servant; knowing his rank rightly elevates the knower.

verses.dev: यो ह वै वसिष्ठं वेद वसिष्ठो ह स्वानां भवति वाग्वाव वसिष्ठः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: yo ha vai vasiṣṭhaṃ veda vasiṣṭho ha svānāṃ bhavati vāg vāva vasiṣṭhaḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: He who knows the most prosperous becomes the most prosperous among his own. Speech, verily, is the most prosperous.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Speech wins wealth and standing (vasu); each sense now states its excellence before the contest.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Eloquence is the Lord's gift through Vāk; its knower prospers among his kin.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Speech's riches are real but subordinate — the narrative will rank all under prāṇa.

verses.dev: यो ह वै प्रतिष्ठां वेद प्रति ह तिष्ठत्यस्मिंश्च लोकेऽमुष्मिंश्च चक्षुर्वाव प्रतिष्ठा ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: yo ha vai pratiṣṭhāṃ veda prati ha tiṣṭhaty asmiṃś ca loke 'muṣmiṃś ca cakṣur vāva pratiṣṭhā || 3 ||
verses.trans: He who knows the firm basis stands firm in this world and in the other. The eye, verily, is the firm basis.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: By sight one finds footing on even and uneven ground — the eye is the body's surveyor.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Vision steadies the walker; the Lord steadies the soul through it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The eye's stabilising office is delegated, like all offices, from above.

verses.dev: यो ह वै संपदं वेद सं हास्मै कामाः पद्यन्ते दैवाश्च मानुषाश्च श्रोत्रं वाव संपत् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: yo ha vai saṃpadaṃ veda saṃ hāsmai kāmāḥ padyante daivāś ca mānuṣāś ca śrotraṃ vāva saṃpat || 4 ||
verses.trans: He who knows attainment — for him desires are attained, both divine and human. The ear, verily, is attainment.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Through hearing the Veda is gained, and through the Veda all ends — the ear is the gate of attainment.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Śravaṇa — hearing the Lord's word — is the first rung of devotion; hence the ear's claim.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Hearing scripture rightly is the seed of all attainment under the Lord.

verses.dev: यो ह वा आयतनं वेदायतनं ह स्वानां भवति मनो ह वा आयतनम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: yo ha vā āyatanaṃ vedāyatanaṃ ha svānāṃ bhavati mano ha vā āyatanam || 5 ||
verses.trans: He who knows the abode becomes the abode of his own. The mind, verily, is the abode.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: All sense-data come home to the mind; it is the household of experience.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The mind houses the impressions the Lord's instruments gather; its knower shelters his kin.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Mind's housing of the senses is an office held under prāṇa and, above him, the Lord.

verses.dev: अथ ह प्राणा अहंश्रेयसि व्यूदिरेऽहं श्रेयानस्म्यहं श्रेयानस्मीति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: atha ha prāṇā ahaṃśreyasi vyūdire 'haṃ śreyān asmy ahaṃ śreyān asmīti || 6 ||
verses.trans: Now the vital powers disputed about who was superior, each saying: 'I am superior! I am superior!'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The quarrel of the faculties is the parable of egoism: each power, forgetting its source, claims primacy.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Self-assertion among the Lord's servants must be settled by the Father — the appeal to Prajāpati follows.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Rank is not self-claimed but divinely adjudicated, as the story will show.

verses.dev: ते ह प्राणाः प्रजापतिं पितरमेत्योचुर्भगवन्को नः श्रेष्ठ इति तान्होवाच यस्मिन्व उत्क्रान्ते शरीरं पापिष्ठतरमिव दृश्येत स वः श्रेष्ठ इति ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: te ha prāṇāḥ prajāpatiṃ pitaram etyocur bhagavan ko naḥ śreṣṭha iti tān hovāca yasmin va utkrānte śarīraṃ pāpiṣṭhataram iva dṛśyeta sa vaḥ śreṣṭha iti || 7 ||
verses.trans: Those powers went to Prajāpati their father and said: 'Revered sir, who is the best of us?' He said to them: 'He on whose departure the body looks most wretched — he is the best of you.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Creator proposes the test of withdrawal: worth is measured by what collapses in one's absence.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Father's criterion is service rendered, not voice raised — the Lord judges by indispensability.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Prajāpati, under Viṣṇu, sets the test that will reveal Vāyu's supremacy among the powers.

verses.dev: सा ह वागुच्चक्राम सा संवत्सरं प्रोष्य पर्येत्योवाच कथमशकतर्ते मज्जीवितुमिति यथा कला अवदन्तः प्राणन्तः प्राणेन पश्यन्तश्चक्षुषा शृण्वन्तः श्रोत्रेण ध्यायन्तो मनसैवमिति प्रविवेश ह वाक् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.iast: sā ha vāg uccakrāma sā saṃvatsaraṃ proṣya paryetyovāca katham aśakatarte maj jīvitum iti yathā kalā avadantaḥ prāṇantaḥ prāṇena paśyantaś cakṣuṣā śṛṇvantaḥ śrotreṇa dhyāyanto manasaivam iti praviveśa ha vāk || 8 ||
verses.trans: Speech departed. Having stayed away a year, it returned and asked: 'How have you been able to live without me?' They said: 'As the dumb live — not speaking, but breathing with the breath, seeing with the eye, hearing with the ear, thinking with the mind.' So speech re-entered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Life persists without speech — the first claimant's absence proves survivable; the body limps but lives.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each power's leave shows its service partial; only the Lord's chief minister is indispensable.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Speech humbled returns; the ranking of powers proceeds by demonstration.

verses.dev: चक्षुर्होच्चक्राम तत्संवत्सरं प्रोष्य पर्येत्योवाच कथमशकतर्ते मज्जीवितुमिति यथान्धा अपश्यन्तः प्राणन्तः प्राणेन वदन्तो वाचा शृण्वन्तः श्रोत्रेण ध्यायन्तो मनसैवमिति प्रविवेश ह चक्षुः ॥ ९ ॥
verses.iast: cakṣur hoccakrāma tat saṃvatsaraṃ proṣya paryetyovāca katham aśakatarte maj jīvitum iti yathāndhā apaśyantaḥ prāṇantaḥ prāṇena vadanto vācā śṛṇvantaḥ śrotreṇa dhyāyanto manasaivam iti praviveśa ha cakṣuḥ || 9 ||
verses.trans: The eye departed. Having stayed away a year, it returned and asked: 'How have you been able to live without me?' They said: 'As the blind live — not seeing, but breathing with the breath, speaking with speech, hearing with the ear, thinking with the mind.' So the eye re-entered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Sight too proves dispensable; the parable strips each faculty of its boast in turn.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The blind live by the Lord's other gifts; no single sense is the soul's life.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The eye's humbling continues the demonstration of graded worth.

verses.dev: श्रोत्रं होच्चक्राम तत्संवत्सरं प्रोष्य पर्येत्योवाच कथमशकतर्ते मज्जीवितुमिति यथा बधिरा अशृण्वन्तः प्राणन्तः प्राणेन वदन्तो वाचा पश्यन्तश्चक्षुषा ध्यायन्तो मनसैवमिति प्रविवेश ह श्रोत्रम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.iast: śrotraṃ hoccakrāma tat saṃvatsaraṃ proṣya paryetyovāca katham aśakatarte maj jīvitum iti yathā badhirā aśṛṇvantaḥ prāṇantaḥ prāṇena vadanto vācā paśyantaś cakṣuṣā dhyāyanto manasaivam iti praviveśa ha śrotram || 10 ||
verses.trans: The ear departed. Having stayed away a year, it returned and asked: 'How have you been able to live without me?' They said: 'As the deaf live — not hearing, but breathing with the breath, speaking with speech, seeing with the eye, thinking with the mind.' So the ear re-entered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Hearing's absence, too, leaves life standing.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The deaf live on; the ear learns its measure.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Third humbling; the order of worth clarifies.

verses.dev: मनो होच्चक्राम तत्संवत्सरं प्रोष्य पर्येत्योवाच कथमशकतर्ते मज्जीवितुमिति यथा बाला अमनसः प्राणन्तः प्राणेन वदन्तो वाचा पश्यन्तश्चक्षुषा शृण्वन्तः श्रोत्रेणैवमिति प्रविवेश ह मनः ॥ ११ ॥
verses.iast: mano hoccakrāma tat saṃvatsaraṃ proṣya paryetyovāca katham aśakatarte maj jīvitum iti yathā bālā amanasaḥ prāṇantaḥ prāṇena vadanto vācā paśyantaś cakṣuṣā śṛṇvantaḥ śrotreṇaivam iti praviveśa ha manaḥ || 11 ||
verses.trans: The mind departed. Having stayed away a year, it returned and asked: 'How have you been able to live without me?' They said: 'As infants live, mindless — but breathing with the breath, speaking with speech, seeing with the eye, hearing with the ear.' So the mind re-entered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even mind is dispensable to bare life — a radical claim preparing prāṇa's unanswerable case.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The infant's life without formed mind shows where life's root truly lies.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Mind, too, bows; only the chief remains untested.

verses.dev: अथ ह प्राण उच्चिक्रमिषन्स यथा सुहयः पड्वीशशङ्कून्संखिदेदेवमितरान्प्राणान्समखिदत्तं हाभिसमेत्योचुर्भगवन्नेधि त्वं नः श्रेष्ठोऽसि मोत्क्रमीरिति ॥ १२ ॥
verses.iast: atha ha prāṇa uccikramiṣan sa yathā suhayaḥ paḍvīśaśaṅkūn saṃkhided evam itarān prāṇān samakhidat taṃ hābhisametyocur bhagavann edhi tvaṃ naḥ śreṣṭho 'si motkramīr iti || 12 ||
verses.trans: Then, as the breath was about to depart — as a fine horse might tear up the pegs of its tether all together — it tore up the other powers all together. They gathered round it and said: 'Revered sir, remain! You are the best of us — do not depart!'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Prāṇa needs no year's absence: its mere stirring uproots all. Śaṅkara: the powers exist by participation in the one life — the parable of the Self's relation to all faculties.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The tethered-horse image shows utter dependence: the senses are pegged to the breath as all beings are to the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva's central proof-text: at Mukhya-prāṇa's first movement the rest are dragged up — his supremacy among created beings is demonstrated, while he in turn serves Viṣṇu alone.

verses.dev: अथ हैनं वागुवाच यदहं वसिष्ठोऽस्मि त्वं तद्वसिष्ठोऽसीत्यथ हैनं चक्षुरुवाच यदहं प्रतिष्ठास्मि त्वं तत्प्रतिष्ठासीति ॥ १३ ॥
verses.iast: atha hainaṃ vāg uvāca yad ahaṃ vasiṣṭho 'smi tvaṃ tad vasiṣṭho 'sīty atha hainaṃ cakṣur uvāca yad ahaṃ pratiṣṭhāsmi tvaṃ tat pratiṣṭhāsīti || 13 ||
verses.trans: Then speech said to it: 'In that I am the most prosperous, you are the most prosperous.' The eye said to it: 'In that I am the firm basis, you are the firm basis.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Each power returns its boast to the source: whatever excellence they have is prāṇa's lent to them.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The faculties' tribute models the soul's surrender: all our excellences belong to the Sustainer.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The senses formally cede their titles to prāṇa — hierarchy acknowledged is harmony restored.

verses.dev: अथ हैनं श्रोत्रमुवाच यदहं संपदस्मि त्वं तत्संपदसीत्यथ हैनं मन उवाच यदहमायतनमस्मि त्वं तदायतनमसीति ॥ १४ ॥
verses.iast: atha hainaṃ śrotram uvāca yad ahaṃ saṃpad asmi tvaṃ tat saṃpad asīty atha hainaṃ mana uvāca yad aham āyatanam asmi tvaṃ tad āyatanam asīti || 14 ||
verses.trans: The ear said to it: 'In that I am attainment, you are attainment.' The mind said to it: 'In that I am the abode, you are the abode.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The transfer of titles is complete: prosperity, basis, attainment, abode — all are the one life appearing as many functions.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All glories rest finally in the sustaining power, as all souls' glories rest in the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Every office is held in trust from the chief; Madhva sees the cosmic constitution displayed.

verses.dev: न वै वाचो न चक्षूंषि न श्रोत्राणि न मनांसीत्याचक्षते प्राणा इत्येवाचक्षते प्राणो ह्येवैतानि सर्वाणि भवति ॥ १५ ॥
verses.iast: na vai vāco na cakṣūṃṣi na śrotrāṇi na manāṃsīty ācakṣate prāṇā ity evācakṣate prāṇo hy evaitāni sarvāṇi bhavati || 15 ||
verses.trans: Verily, people do not call them 'speeches' or 'eyes' or 'ears' or 'minds'; they call them 'prāṇas' (vital powers), for the breath indeed is all these.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Usage itself testifies: the faculties are named after prāṇa because they are prāṇa diversified. Śaṅkara points beyond: as the senses are prāṇa, so all this is the Self under many names.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The common name declares the common life — one sustainer wearing many functions, as the one Lord ensouls all.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Language enshrines the truth of Vāyu's pervading stewardship of the faculties under the Lord.

verses.dev: स होवाच किं मेऽन्नं भविष्यतीति यत्किंचिदिदमा श्वभ्य आ शकुनिभ्य इति होचुस्तद्वा एतदनस्यान्नमनो ह वै नाम प्रत्यक्षं न ह वा एवंविदि किंचनानन्नं भवतीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: sa hovāca kiṃ me 'nnaṃ bhaviṣyatīti yat kiṃcid idam ā śvabhya ā śakunibhya iti hocus tad vā etad anasyānnam ano ha vai nāma pratyakṣaṃ na ha vā evaṃvidi kiṃcanānannaṃ bhavatīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: It (the breath) said: 'What shall be my food?' They said: 'Whatever there is, down to dogs and birds.' This, then, is the food of 'ana' (the breath); 'ana' is its evident name. For the one who knows thus, nothing whatever is not-food.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: All that lives eats for the universal breath; to the knower nothing is impure at the level of the one life — Śaṅkara cautions this states a meditation's vision, not a licence for conduct.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Every creature's eating feeds the Lord's vital minister; the devotee sees the Lord's sustenance flowing through all.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Prāṇa eats through every mouth as Viṣṇu's agent; the knower honours the universal feeding without breaching dharma.

verses.dev: स होवाच किं मे वासो भविष्यतीत्याप इति होचुस्तस्माद्वा एतदशिष्यन्तः पुरस्ताच्चोपरिष्टाच्चाद्भिः परिदधति लम्भुको ह वासो भवत्यनग्नो ह भवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa hovāca kiṃ me vāso bhaviṣyatīty āpa iti hocus tasmād vā etad aśiṣyantaḥ purastāc copariṣṭāc cādbhiḥ paridadhati lambhuko ha vāso bhavaty anagno ha bhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: It said: 'What shall be my garment?' They said: 'Water.' Therefore, when people are about to eat, they enclose the food before and after with water (by sipping). Thus the breath obtains its garment and is not naked.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The daily sippings (ācamana) around meals clothe the inner breath — ritual practice encodes the vidyā.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The water-garment honours the Lord's life within; tradition's smallest gestures carry His worship.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The custom of sipping is prāṇa's vestment, a perpetual reminder of his presence at every meal.

verses.dev: तद्धैतत्सत्यकामो जाबालो गोश्रुतये वैयाघ्रपद्यायोक्त्वोवाच यद्यप्येनच्छुष्काय स्थाणवे ब्रूयाज्जायेरन्नेवास्मिञ्शाखाः प्ररोहेयुः पलाशानीति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tad dhaitat satyakāmo jābālo gośrutaye vaiyāghrapadyāyoktvovāca yady apy enac chuṣkāya sthāṇave brūyāj jāyerann evāsmiñ śākhāḥ praroheyuḥ palāśānīti || 3 ||
verses.trans: Satyakāma Jābāla, having taught this to Gośruti Vaiyāghrapadya, said: 'Even if one should declare this to a dried-up stump, branches would sprout on it and leaves would spring forth.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Hyperbole of the vidyā's power: the doctrine of life would quicken dead wood — how much more the living soul.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The teaching carries the Lord's vivifying force; no recipient is beyond its greening.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The praise marks the vidyā's potency, granted by the Lord to His doctrine of the chief breath.

verses.dev: अथ यदि महज्जिगमिषेदमावास्यायां दीक्षित्वा पौर्णमास्यां रात्रौ सर्वौषधस्य मन्थं दधिमधुनोरुपमथ्य ज्येष्ठाय श्रेष्ठाय स्वाहेत्यग्नावाज्यस्य हुत्वा मन्थे संपातमवनयेत् ॥ ४ ॥
