verses.iast: atha yadi mahaj jigamiṣed amāvāsyāyāṃ dīkṣitvā paurṇamāsyāṃ rātrau sarvauṣadhasya manthaṃ dadhimadhunor upamathya jyeṣṭhāya śreṣṭhāya svāhety agnāv ājyasya hutvā manthe saṃpātam avanayet || 4 ||
verses.trans: Now, if one desires to attain greatness: having consecrated oneself on the new-moon day, on the night of the full moon let him stir a mash (mantha) of all herbs with curds and honey; let him offer clarified butter in the fire with 'To the eldest, to the best — svāhā!' and pour the residue into the mash.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The mantha rite translates the prāṇa-doctrine into ceremony: greatness is sought from the eldest and best.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The rite petitions the Lord through His chief minister; consecration and offering frame the request.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Worship of prāṇa as eldest and best, with oblations, is the sanctioned path to greatness under the Lord.

verses.dev: वसिष्ठाय स्वाहेत्यग्नावाज्यस्य हुत्वा मन्थे संपातमवनयेत्प्रतिष्ठायै स्वाहेत्यग्नावाज्यस्य हुत्वा मन्थे संपातमवनयेत्संपदे स्वाहेत्यग्नावाज्यस्य हुत्वा मन्थे संपातमवनयेदायतनाय स्वाहेत्यग्नावाज्यस्य हुत्वा मन्थे संपातमवनयेत् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: vasiṣṭhāya svāhety agnāv ājyasya hutvā manthe saṃpātam avanayet pratiṣṭhāyai svāhety agnāv ājyasya hutvā manthe saṃpātam avanayet saṃpade svāhety agnāv ājyasya hutvā manthe saṃpātam avanayed āyatanāya svāhety agnāv ājyasya hutvā manthe saṃpātam avanayet || 5 ||
verses.trans: With 'To the most prosperous — svāhā!' let him offer butter in the fire and pour the residue into the mash. With 'To the firm basis — svāhā!' ... with 'To attainment — svāhā!' ... with 'To the abode — svāhā!' let him offer butter in the fire and pour the residue into the mash.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The five titles won in the contest become the five oblations — narrative crystallised into liturgy.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each excellence is saluted as the Lord's own, gathered into the cup of blessing.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fivefold offering honours prāṇa under his five conquered titles.

verses.dev: अथ प्रतिसृप्याञ्जलौ मन्थमाधाय जपत्यमो नामास्यमा हि ते सर्वमिदं स हि ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठो राजाधिपतिः स मा ज्यैष्ठ्यं श्रैष्ठ्यं राज्यमाधिपत्यं गमयत्वहमेवेदं सर्वमसानीति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: atha pratisṛpyāñjalau mantham ādhāya japaty amo nāmāsy amā hi te sarvam idaṃ sa hi jyeṣṭhaḥ śreṣṭho rājādhipatiḥ sa mā jyaiṣṭhyaṃ śraiṣṭhyaṃ rājyam ādhipatyaṃ gamayatv aham evedaṃ sarvam asānīti || 6 ||
verses.trans: Then, creeping back (to the fire) and holding the mash in his cupped hands, he murmurs: 'Your name is Ama (power), for all this is with you. He is the eldest, the best, the king, the overlord. May he lead me to eldership, to excellence, to kingship, to overlordship. May I be all this!'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The prayer's climax — 'may I be all this' — looks past particular boons to the universal: the meditator aspires to the totality that prāṇa represents and the Self truly is.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The petition addresses the Lord, the true 'eldest and best, king and overlord'; the devotee asks for greatness within His sovereign order.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva reads the titles as Viṣṇu's, mediated by Vāyu; the aspirant seeks the station the Lord may grant, never identity with Him.

verses.dev: अथ खल्वेतयर्चा पच्छ आचामति तत्सवितुर्वृणीमह इत्याचामति वयं देवस्य भोजनमित्याचामति श्रेष्ठं सर्वधातममित्याचामति तुरं भगस्य धीमहीति सर्वं पिबति निर्णिज्य कंसं चमसं वा पश्चादग्नेः संविशति चर्मणि वा स्थण्डिले वा वाचंयमोऽप्रसाहः स यदि स्त्रियं पश्येत्समृद्धं कर्मेति विद्यात् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: atha khalv etayarcā paccha ācāmati tat savitur vṛṇīmaha ity ācāmati vayaṃ devasya bhojanam ity ācāmati śreṣṭhaṃ sarvadhātamam ity ācāmati turaṃ bhagasya dhīmahīti sarvaṃ pibati nirṇijya kaṃsaṃ camasaṃ vā paścād agneḥ saṃviśati carmaṇi vā sthaṇḍile vā vācaṃyamo 'prasāhaḥ sa yadi striyaṃ paśyet samṛddhaṃ karmeti vidyāt || 7 ||
verses.trans: Then he sips the mash foot by foot with this Ṛg verse: 'That (food) of Savitṛ we choose' — he sips; 'the food of the god' — he sips; 'the best and all-sustaining' — he sips; 'we meditate on the swift (bounty) of Bhaga' — and he drinks the whole. Having cleansed the goblet or cup, he lies down behind the fire, on a skin or on the bare ground, restraining speech and self-possessed. If he should see a woman (in his dream), let him know that his rite has prospered.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The verse-by-verse sipping unites mantra and act; the dream-omen attests the rite's ripeness in the depths of the mind.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Sāvitrī-derived verses consecrate the drinking; success is signalled to the surrendered, watchful heart.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Disciplined completion — silence, bare couch, vigil — invites the auspicious sign appointed by the Lord.

verses.dev: तदेष श्लोको यदा कर्मसु काम्येषु स्त्रियं स्वप्नेषु पश्यति समृद्धिं तत्र जानीयात्तस्मिन्स्वप्ननिदर्शने तस्मिन्स्वप्ननिदर्शने ॥ ८ ॥
verses.iast: tad eṣa śloko yadā karmasu kāmyeṣu striyaṃ svapneṣu paśyati samṛddhiṃ tatra jānīyāt tasmin svapnanidarśane tasmin svapnanidarśane || 8 ||
verses.trans: On this there is this verse: 'When, during rites performed for a wish, one sees a woman in dreams, let him know prosperity from that dream-vision — from that dream-vision.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The śloka seals the khaṇḍa; dream is the mind's mirror of the rite's accomplishment. The repetition closes the section.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Even omens are the Lord's gentle assurances to the hopeful performer.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The confirming verse, doubled, certifies the sign's reliability within the Lord's order.

verses.dev: श्वेतकेतुर्हारुणेयः पञ्चालानां समितिमेयाय तं ह प्रवाहणो जैवलिरुवाच कुमारानु त्वाशिषत्पितेत्यनु हि भगव इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: śvetaketur hāruṇeyaḥ pañcālānāṃ samitim eyāya taṃ ha pravāhaṇo jaivalir uvāca kumārānu tvāśiṣat pitety anu hi bhagava iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Śvetaketu Āruṇeya came to the assembly of the Pañcālas. Pravāhaṇa Jaivali said to him: 'Young man, has your father instructed you?' — 'Indeed he has, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The proud young scholar enters a king's court; his confident 'indeed' is about to be tested by questions his book-learning cannot answer.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord arranges instruction through unexpected mouths — here a kṣatriya king will school a brāhmaṇa's son.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Learning without the Lord's deepest doctrines is incomplete, as the dialogue will expose.

verses.dev: वेत्थ यदितोऽधि प्रजाः प्रयन्तीति न भगव इति वेत्थ यथा पुनरावर्तन्त३ इति न भगव इति वेत्थ पथोर्देवयानस्य पितृयाणस्य च व्यावर्तना३ इति न भगव इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: vettha yad ito 'dhi prajāḥ prayantīti na bhagava iti vettha yathā punar āvartanta3 iti na bhagava iti vettha pathor devayānasya pitṛyāṇasya ca vyāvartanā3 iti na bhagava iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Do you know where created beings go from here (at death)?' — 'No, revered sir.' 'Do you know how they return again?' — 'No, revered sir.' 'Do you know where the path of the gods and the path of the fathers part?' — 'No, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Three questions on the soul's journey — departure, return, and the fork of the two paths — none answerable by ritual learning alone.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The king probes the science of the soul's destiny, the special province of those who know the Lord's ways.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Eschatology is revealed knowledge; honest 'I do not know' begins its acquisition.

verses.dev: वेत्थ यथासौ लोको न संपूर्यत३ इति न भगव इति वेत्थ यथा पञ्चम्यामाहुतावापः पुरुषवचसो भवन्तीति नैव भगव इति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: vettha yathāsau loko na saṃpūryata3 iti na bhagava iti vettha yathā pañcamyām āhutāv āpaḥ puruṣavacaso bhavantīti naiva bhagava iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Do you know why yonder world is not filled up (with the many who die)?' — 'No, revered sir.' 'Do you know how, at the fifth oblation, the waters come to speak with a human voice?' — 'No indeed, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fifth question holds the vidyā's key: how subtle 'waters' offered through five fires become a speaking person — the mechanics of rebirth.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The riddle of the waters becoming man frames the Lord's governance of reincarnation.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The five-oblation question encapsulates the whole doctrine of the soul's descent into a body.

verses.dev: अथानु किमनुशिष्टोऽवोचथा यो हीमानि न विद्यात्कथं सोऽनुशिष्टो ब्रुवीतेति स हायस्तः पितुरर्धमेयाय तं होवाचाननुशिष्य वाव किल मा भगवानब्रवीदनु त्वाशिषमिति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: athānu kim anuśiṣṭo 'vocathā yo hīmāni na vidyāt kathaṃ so 'nuśiṣṭo bruvīteti sa hāyastaḥ pitur ardham eyāya taṃ hovācānanuśiṣya vāva kila mā bhagavān abravīd anu tvāśiṣam iti || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'Then why did you say you had been instructed? How could one who does not know these things call himself instructed?' Distressed, he went back to his father's place and said to him: 'Without having instructed me, you said, revered sir, "I have instructed you!"'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Humbling is the door to higher learning; the son's distress is the crack through which new knowledge enters the family.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Wounded pride turns the seeker home — and then beyond home — for what only grace-bearing teachers hold.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Exposure of ignorance is the Lord's kindness, driving father and son to the deeper doctrine.

verses.dev: पञ्च मा राजन्यबन्धुः प्रश्नानप्राक्षीत्तेषां नैकंचनाशकं विवक्तुमिति स होवाच यथा मा त्वं तदैतानवदो यथाहमेषां नैकंचन वेद यद्यहमिमानवेदिष्यं कथं ते नावक्ष्यमिति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: pañca mā rājanyabandhuḥ praśnān aprākṣīt teṣāṃ naikaṃcanāśakaṃ vivaktum iti sa hovāca yathā mā tvaṃ tadaitān avado yathāham eṣāṃ naikaṃcana veda yady aham imān avediṣyaṃ kathaṃ te nāvakṣyam iti || 5 ||
verses.trans: 'That fellow of the princely class asked me five questions, and I could not answer a single one.' The father said: 'As you have stated them to me — I do not know a single one of them. If I had known them, why should I not have told you?'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Gautama's candour matches his son's: the learned father admits the limit of his learning — the honesty that qualifies him to seek.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: No shame attaches to ignorance confessed; the father will model the dignity of becoming a student again.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Truthful acknowledgment of limits is the first step toward the Lord's hidden doctrine.

verses.dev: स ह गौतमो राज्ञोऽर्धमेयाय तस्मै ह प्राप्तायार्हां चकार स ह प्रातः सभाग उदेयाय तं होवाच मानुषस्य भगवन्गौतम वित्तस्य वरं वृणीथा इति स होवाच तवैव राजन्मानुषं वित्तं यामेव कुमारस्यान्ते वाचमभाषथास्तामेव मे ब्रूहीति स ह कृच्छ्री बभूव ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: sa ha gautamo rājño 'rdham eyāya tasmai ha prāptāyārhāṃ cakāra sa ha prātaḥ sabhāga udeyāya taṃ hovāca mānuṣasya bhagavan gautama vittasya varaṃ vṛṇīthā iti sa hovāca tavaiva rājan mānuṣaṃ vittaṃ yām eva kumārasyānte vācam abhāṣathās tām eva me brūhīti sa ha kṛcchrī babhūva || 6 ||
verses.trans: Gautama went to the king's place, and on his arrival the king received him with honour. In the morning he went up to the audience hall, and the king said: 'Revered Gautama, choose a boon of human wealth.' He replied: 'Let human wealth remain yours, O king. Tell me the very speech you spoke in the young man's presence.' The king was troubled.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The brāhmaṇa refuses wealth and begs the word — knowledge outweighs every boon. The king's trouble shows the doctrine's preciousness.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: True seekers barter nothing less than truth; the king hesitates before the guarded treasure of the lineage.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The kṣatriya-held vidyā passes only under constraint — the Lord's secret travels by appointed channels.

verses.dev: तं ह चिरं वसेत्याज्ञापयांचकार तं होवाच यथा मा त्वं गौतमावदो यथेयं न प्राक्त्वत्तः पुरा विद्या ब्राह्मणान्गच्छति तस्मादु सर्वेषु लोकेषु क्षत्रस्यैव प्रशासनमभूदिति तस्मै होवाच ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ ha ciraṃ vasety ājñāpayāṃ cakāra taṃ hovāca yathā mā tvaṃ gautamāvado yatheyaṃ na prāk tvattaḥ purā vidyā brāhmaṇān gacchati tasmād u sarveṣu lokeṣu kṣatrasyaiva praśāsanam abhūd iti tasmai hovāca || 7 ||
verses.trans: He commanded him: 'Stay with me a long time.' Then he said: 'As to what you have told me, Gautama — this knowledge has never before you gone to brāhmaṇas; therefore in all the worlds its teaching belonged to the kṣatriya class alone.' Then he said to him:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara notes the historical remark: this royal science of the soul's journey now crosses to the brāhmaṇas through Gautama's humility — knowledge honours the seeker, not the caste.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The kings held this vidyā in trust; its transmission to Gautama is the Lord widening the river of grace.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Providence appointed kṣatriya custody until this hour; now the worthy brāhmaṇa receives it for all.

verses.dev: असौ वाव लोको गौतमाग्निस्तस्यादित्य एव समिद्रश्मयो धूमोऽहरर्चिश्चन्द्रमा अङ्गारा नक्षत्राणि विस्फुलिङ्गाः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: asau vāva loko gautamāgnis tasyāditya eva samid raśmayo dhūmo 'har arciś candramā aṅgārā nakṣatrāṇi visphuliṅgāḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Yonder world (heaven), O Gautama, is a fire: the sun is its fuel, the rays its smoke, the day its flame, the moon its embers, the stars its sparks.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The first of five cosmic altars: heaven itself imagined as the sacrificial fire into which the gods offer — the universe as one continuous liturgy.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Creation is the Lord's yajña; each region is His altar, tended by His gods.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The cosmic fires are the Lord's instruments for the soul's descent — stages He alone controls.

verses.dev: तस्मिन्नेतस्मिन्नग्नौ देवाः श्रद्धां जुह्वति तस्या आहुतेः सोमो राजा संभवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasminn etasminn agnau devāḥ śraddhāṃ juhvati tasyā āhuteḥ somo rājā saṃbhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: In this fire the gods offer faith (śraddhā); from that oblation King Soma arises.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: 'Faith' here is the subtle water-body of the departed offered into heaven; it condenses as Soma, the lunar substance of the soul's sojourn.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The soul wrapped in its subtle elements is the gods' oblation; the Lord receives it into the lunar realm.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The first oblation begins the soul's guided descent — śraddhā transformed to Soma by divine agency.

verses.dev: पर्जन्यो वाव गौतमाग्निस्तस्य वायुरेव समिदभ्रं धूमो विद्युदर्चिरशनिरङ्गारा ह्रादुनयो विस्फुलिङ्गाः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: parjanyo vāva gautamāgnis tasya vāyur eva samid abhraṃ dhūmo vidyud arcir aśanir aṅgārā hrādunayo visphuliṅgāḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'The rain-god Parjanya, O Gautama, is a fire: the wind is its fuel, the cloud its smoke, the lightning its flame, the thunderbolt its embers, the hailstones its sparks.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The second altar: the storm. The soul's substance moves from moon to cloud in the unbroken chain of offerings.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's rain-altar receives the lunar oblation; His weather is liturgy.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each transformation is an offering made by gods at Viṣṇu's behest.

verses.dev: तस्मिन्नेतस्मिन्नग्नौ देवाः सोमं राजानं जुह्वति तस्या आहुतेर्वर्षं संभवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasminn etasminn agnau devāḥ somaṃ rājānaṃ juhvati tasyā āhuter varṣaṃ saṃbhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: In this fire the gods offer King Soma; from that oblation rain arises.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Soma poured into the storm becomes rain — the soul's vehicle descends another step toward earth.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The lunar soul-substance is released as rain by the Lord's storm-priests.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The second oblation: divine hands pass the soul from moon to rain.

verses.dev: पृथिवी वाव गौतमाग्निस्तस्याः संवत्सर एव समिदाकाशो धूमो रात्रिरर्चिर्दिशोऽङ्गारा अवान्तरदिशो विस्फुलिङ्गाः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: pṛthivī vāva gautamāgnis tasyāḥ saṃvatsara eva samid ākāśo dhūmo rātrir arcir diśo 'ṅgārā avāntaradiśo visphuliṅgāḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'The earth, O Gautama, is a fire: the year is its fuel, space its smoke, the night its flame, the quarters its embers, the intermediate quarters its sparks.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The third altar: earth, fed by the year. The rain-borne soul is now offered into the soil of time and place.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Earth, the Lord's footstool, is also His hearth; the seasons stoke it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The earthly altar receives the rain-oblation in its appointed season.

verses.dev: तस्मिन्नेतस्मिन्नग्नौ देवा वर्षं जुह्वति तस्या आहुतेरन्नं संभवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasminn etasminn agnau devā varṣaṃ juhvati tasyā āhuter annaṃ saṃbhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: In this fire the gods offer rain; from that oblation food arises.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Rain offered to earth becomes food — the soul's substance now grows in the grain.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord clothes the descending soul in the harvest.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Third oblation: rain to food, by divine cultivation.

verses.dev: पुरुषो वाव गौतमाग्निस्तस्य वागेव समित्प्राणो धूमो जिह्वार्चिश्चक्षुरङ्गाराः श्रोत्रं विस्फुलिङ्गाः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: puruṣo vāva gautamāgnis tasya vāg eva samit prāṇo dhūmo jihvārciś cakṣur aṅgārāḥ śrotraṃ visphuliṅgāḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Man, O Gautama, is a fire: speech is his fuel, breath his smoke, the tongue his flame, the eye his embers, the ear his sparks.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fourth altar is the eater himself: man as fire consuming food, his faculties the parts of the blaze.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's altar now wears a human form; eating is oblation.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The soul-bearing food enters man, the fourth fire in the Lord's sequence.

verses.dev: तस्मिन्नेतस्मिन्नग्नौ देवा अन्नं जुह्वति तस्या आहुते रेतः संभवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasminn etasminn agnau devā annaṃ juhvati tasyā āhute retaḥ saṃbhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: In this fire the gods offer food; from that oblation seed arises.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Food refined to seed: the soul's vehicle approaches its final transformation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Within man the Lord's alchemy prepares the seed of new embodiment.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Fourth oblation: food to seed, under the indwelling Lord's direction.

verses.dev: योषा वाव गौतमाग्निस्तस्या उपस्थ एव समिद्यदुपमन्त्रयते स धूमो योनिरर्चिर्यदन्तः करोति तेऽङ्गारा अभिनन्दा विस्फुलिङ्गाः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yoṣā vāva gautamāgnis tasyā upastha eva samid yad upamantrayate sa dhūmo yonir arcir yad antaḥ karoti te 'ṅgārā abhinandā visphuliṅgāḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Woman, O Gautama, is a fire (her aspects are its fuel, smoke, flame, embers, and sparks).

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fifth altar completes the chain: generation itself is sacrificial, no profane act but a station of the cosmic rite.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord sanctifies procreation as the last altar of the soul's descent.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fifth fire receives the fourth's oblation as ordained from the beginning.

verses.dev: तस्मिन्नेतस्मिन्नग्नौ देवा रेतो जुह्वति तस्या आहुतेर्गर्भः संभवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasminn etasminn agnau devā reto juhvati tasyā āhuter garbhaḥ saṃbhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: In this fire the gods offer seed; from that oblation the embryo arises.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fifth oblation: seed becomes embryo — the 'waters' first offered as faith in heaven now stand on the threshold of human speech.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The riddle resolves: through five offerings the Lord shepherds the subtle waters into a person.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The soul's vehicle, five times offered, is shaped into the womb-borne body by divine priests.

verses.dev: इति तु पञ्चम्यामाहुतावापः पुरुषवचसो भवन्तीति स उल्बावृतो गर्भो दश वा नव वा मासानन्तः शयित्वा यावद्वाथ जायते ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: iti tu pañcamyām āhutāv āpaḥ puruṣavacaso bhavantīti sa ulbāvṛto garbho daśa vā nava vā māsān antaḥ śayitvā yāvad vātha jāyate || 1 ||
verses.trans: Thus it is that at the fifth oblation the waters come to speak with a human voice. The embryo, wrapped in its membrane, having lain inside for ten or nine months or thereabouts, is then born.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The king's riddle is answered: the subtle 'waters' have passed through heaven, storm, earth, man, and woman to emerge as a speaking child. Śaṅkara: such is saṃsāra's machinery — seen clearly, it breeds the dispassion that seeks the unborn Self.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The five-staged descent shows the Lord's meticulous governance of rebirth; no step is chance, all is His ordination.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The soul's embodiment follows the Lord's exact sequence of oblations — birth is divine administration, not accident.

verses.dev: स जातो यावदायुषं जीवति तं प्रेतं दिष्टमितोऽग्नय एव हरन्ति यत एवेतो यतः संभूतो भवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa jāto yāvad āyuṣaṃ jīvati taṃ pretaṃ diṣṭam ito 'gnaya eva haranti yata eveto yataḥ saṃbhūto bhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: Born, he lives whatever his length of life may be. When he has departed, they carry him hence to the appointed place — to the fire itself from which he came, from which he arose.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: From fire to fire: the body, born of the five fires, returns at death to fire. The cycle closes — and the next khaṇḍa will open the two roads out of it.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The funeral fire receives back the Lord's loan; the soul now takes one of the two paths He has laid.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Death returns the elements to their altar; the soul proceeds as the Lord's justice and the soul's nature determine.

verses.dev: तद्य इत्थं विदुर्ये चेमेऽरण्ये श्रद्धा तप इत्युपासते तेऽर्चिषमभिसंभवन्त्यर्चिषोऽहरह्न आपूर्यमाणपक्षमापूर्यमाणपक्षाद्यान्षडुदङ्ङेति मासांस्तान् ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: tad ya itthaṃ vidur ye ceme 'raṇye śraddhā tapa ity upāsate te 'rciṣam abhisaṃbhavanty arciṣo 'har ahna āpūryamāṇapakṣam āpūryamāṇapakṣād yān ṣaḍ udaṅṅ eti māsāṃs tān || 1 ||
verses.trans: Now, those who know this thus, and those who in the forest meditate with faith and austerity, pass into the flame; from the flame into the day; from the day into the bright fortnight; from the bright fortnight into the six months when the sun moves north —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The bright road opens for knowers of the five fires and forest meditators: stage by luminous stage toward Brahman. Śaṅkara reads the stations as escorting deities, not mere times.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The arcirādi path — flame, day, bright fortnight, northern sun — is the soul's royal road to the Lord, attended at each stage by His escorts.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The path of light is reserved for qualified knowers; each station is a divine officer conveying the soul upward.

verses.dev: मासेभ्यः संवत्सरं संवत्सरादादित्यमादित्याच्चन्द्रमसं चन्द्रमसो विद्युतं तत्पुरुषोऽमानवः स एनान्ब्रह्म गमयत्येष देवयानः पन्था इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: māsebhyaḥ saṃvatsaraṃ saṃvatsarād ādityam ādityāc candramasaṃ candramaso vidyutaṃ tat puruṣo 'mānavaḥ sa enān brahma gamayaty eṣa devayānaḥ panthā iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: — from the months into the year; from the year into the sun; from the sun into the moon; from the moon into the lightning. There a Person who is not human leads them on to Brahman. This is the path of the gods (devayāna).

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The non-human Person conducts the soul to the conditioned Brahman (brahmaloka), whence liberation follows with the final knowledge — the doctrine of krama-mukti.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The amānava-puruṣa, commissioned by the Lord, takes the soul by the hand into the supreme abode — the consummation of surrender, never to return.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the soul is led to Viṣṇu's realm according to its intrinsic worth; the path of the gods is final beatitude for the devoted knower.

verses.dev: अथ य इमे ग्राम इष्टापूर्ते दत्तमित्युपासते ते धूममभिसंभवन्ति धूमाद्रात्रिं रात्रेरपरपक्षमपरपक्षाद्यान्षड्दक्षिणैति मासांस्तान्नैते संवत्सरमभिप्राप्नुवन्ति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: atha ya ime grāma iṣṭāpūrte dattam ity upāsate te dhūmam abhisaṃbhavanti dhūmād rātriṃ rātrer aparapakṣam aparapakṣād yān ṣaḍ dakṣiṇaiti māsāṃs tān naite saṃvatsaram abhiprāpnuvanti || 3 ||
verses.trans: But those who, living in the village, revere sacrifice, public works, and almsgiving pass into the smoke; from the smoke into the night; from the night into the dark fortnight; from the dark fortnight into the six months when the sun moves south. They do not reach the year.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The smoky road of the ritualists: works without knowledge climb only partway. Missing 'the year' marks the path's limit — it cannot pass time's circle.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Meritorious works win the world of the fathers, a real but returnable reward; the Lord honours the deed but reserves Himself for the devoted knower.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The southern path is the just wage of pious works; its travellers remain within the revolving order.

verses.dev: मासेभ्यः पितृलोकं पितृलोकादाकाशमाकाशाच्चन्द्रमसमेष सोमो राजा तद्देवानामन्नं तं देवा भक्षयन्ति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: māsebhyaḥ pitṛlokaṃ pitṛlokād ākāśam ākāśāc candramasam eṣa somo rājā tad devānām annaṃ taṃ devā bhakṣayanti || 4 ||
verses.trans: From the months into the world of the fathers; from the world of the fathers into space; from space into the moon. That is King Soma. That is the food of the gods; the gods feed on it.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The merit-bearing soul becomes lunar substance on which the gods 'feed' — i.e., it serves and shares their world while its merit lasts.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Lunar sojourn is real enjoyment in the gods' company, measured to the soul's account of works.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Being 'eaten by the gods' means serving them as an instrument of their joy — temporary elevation, not freedom.

verses.dev: तस्मिन्यावत्संपातमुषित्वाथैतमेवाध्वानं पुनर्निवर्तन्ते यथेतमाकाशमाकाशाद्वायुं वायुर्भूत्वा धूमो भवति धूमो भूत्वाभ्रं भवति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: tasmin yāvat saṃpātam uṣitvāthaitam evādhvānaṃ punar nivartante yathetam ākāśam ākāśād vāyuṃ vāyur bhūtvā dhūmo bhavati dhūmo bhūtvābhraṃ bhavati || 5 ||
verses.trans: Having dwelt there until their works are exhausted, they return again by the way they came: into space; from space into the wind. Having become wind, one becomes smoke; having become smoke, one becomes mist —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Merit spends like a purse; the descent retraces the ascent. Śaṅkara: herein lies the great defect of works — their fruits end.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The return journey shows the perishability of all ends but the Lord; the soul drifts down through the elements it once climbed.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Exhausted merit triggers lawful descent — the Lord's accounting is exact.

verses.dev: अभ्रं भूत्वा मेघो भवति मेघो भूत्वा प्रवर्षति त इह व्रीहियवा ओषधिवनस्पतयस्तिलमाषा इति जायन्तेऽतो वै खलु दुर्निष्प्रपतरं यो यो ह्यन्नमत्ति यो रेतः सिञ्चति तद्भूय एव भवति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: abhraṃ bhūtvā megho bhavati megho bhūtvā pravarṣati ta iha vrīhiyavā oṣadhivanaspatayas tilamāṣā iti jāyante 'to vai khalu durniṣprapataraṃ yo yo hy annam atti yo retaḥ siñcati tad bhūya eva bhavati || 6 ||
verses.trans: — having become mist, one becomes cloud; having become cloud, one rains down. Here they are born as rice and barley, herbs and trees, sesame and beans. From this state, truly, the escape is most difficult: only when someone eats him as food and emits him as seed does he develop further.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The bottleneck of rebirth: lodged in plants, the soul waits upon another's eating — Śaṅkara presses the horror of dependence to kindle renunciation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The painful narrows of the return reveal saṃsāra's bondage, from which only the Lord's path of light finally frees.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The difficult passage is the strict mechanics of karma under divine supervision — sobering the wise toward devotion.

verses.dev: तद्य इह रमणीयचरणा अभ्याशो ह यत्ते रमणीयां योनिमापद्येरन्ब्राह्मणयोनिं वा क्षत्रिययोनिं वा वैश्ययोनिं वाथ य इह कपूयचरणा अभ्याशो ह यत्ते कपूयां योनिमापद्येरन्श्वयोनिं वा सूकरयोनिं वा चण्डालयोनिं वा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: tad ya iha ramaṇīyacaraṇā abhyāśo ha yat te ramaṇīyāṃ yonim āpadyeran brāhmaṇayoniṃ vā kṣatriyayoniṃ vā vaiśyayoniṃ vātha ya iha kapūyacaraṇā abhyāśo ha yat te kapūyāṃ yonim āpadyeran śvayoniṃ vā sūkarayoniṃ vā caṇḍālayoniṃ vā || 7 ||
verses.trans: Those whose conduct here has been pleasing may expect to enter a pleasing womb — of a brāhmaṇa, a kṣatriya, or a vaiśya. But those whose conduct here has been foul may expect to enter a foul womb — of a dog, a pig, or an outcaste.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Conduct (caraṇa) steers the re-entry: ethics writes the address of the next birth. Śaṅkara: hence the urgency of right living even for the seeker of knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord, impartial registrar of conduct, assigns wombs according to deeds — His justice underwrites morality.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Karma's fruit is allotted by the Lord with perfect equity; conduct here is destiny's seed.

verses.dev: अथैतयोः पथोर्न कतरेणचन तानीमानि क्षुद्राण्यसकृदावर्तीनि भूतानि भवन्ति जायस्व म्रियस्वेत्येतत्तृतीयं स्थानं तेनासौ लोको न संपूर्यते तस्माज्जुगुप्सेत तदेष श्लोकः ॥ ८ ॥
verses.iast: athaitayoḥ pathor na katareṇacana tānīmāni kṣudrāṇy asakṛdāvartīni bhūtāni bhavanti jāyasva mriyasvety etat tṛtīyaṃ sthānaṃ tenāsau loko na saṃpūryate tasmāj jugupseta tad eṣa ślokaḥ || 8 ||
verses.trans: But those who go by neither of these two paths become the small creatures that revolve again and again, of whom it is said: 'Be born and die.' This is the third state. Therefore yonder world is not filled up. Hence one should be disgusted (with this round). On this there is this verse:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The third state — the swarm of briefly-living creatures — answers the king's fourth question: heaven never fills because so many circle below. 'Therefore let one recoil': scripture itself prescribes vairāgya.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The ceaseless churn of small births moves the compassionate Lord to call souls to His path; disgust with saṃsāra is grace beginning.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The third station shows karma's machinery at its bleakest; aversion to it turns the soul toward the Lord.

verses.dev: स्तेनो हिरण्यस्य सुरां पिबंश्च गुरोस्तल्पमावसन्ब्रह्महा चैते पतन्ति चत्वारः पञ्चमश्चाचरंस्तैरिति ॥ ९ ॥
verses.iast: steno hiraṇyasya surāṃ pibaṃś ca guros talpam āvasan brahmahā caite patanti catvāraḥ pañcamaś cācaraṃs tair iti || 9 ||
verses.trans: 'The thief of gold, the drinker of liquor, the defiler of his teacher's bed, the slayer of a brāhmaṇa — these four fall; and fifth, he who consorts with them.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The śloka names the great falls; association shares their fate. Moral gravity holds even within the metaphysics of the paths.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Grave sins bar both bright and smoky roads; the verse fences the path of return.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The five great sinners plunge by the Lord's justice; company in sin is complicity.

verses.dev: अथ ह य एतानेवं पञ्चाग्नीन्वेद न सह तैरप्याचरन्पाप्मना लिप्यते शुद्धः पूतः पुण्यलोको भवति य एवं वेद य एवं वेद ॥ १० ॥
verses.iast: atha ha ya etān evaṃ pañcāgnīn veda na saha tair apy ācaran pāpmanā lipyate śuddhaḥ pūtaḥ puṇyaloko bhavati ya evaṃ veda ya evaṃ veda || 10 ||
verses.trans: But he who knows these five fires thus is not stained by evil even while living among such people; he becomes clean and pure and wins the world of the blessed — he who knows thus, he who knows thus.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Knowledge purifies beyond circumstance: the knower of the five fires, seeing the whole machinery of saṃsāra, stands inwardly free of it. The doubled close seals the Pañcāgni-vidyā.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Understanding the Lord's governance of birth and death itself sanctifies; the knower lives unstained, destined for the blessed realm.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Right knowledge of the Lord's cosmic administration shields the soul from surrounding sin and earns the pure world He grants.

verses.dev: प्राचीनशाल औपमन्यवः सत्ययज्ञः पौलुषिरिन्द्रद्युम्नो भाल्लवेयो जनः शार्कराक्ष्यो बुडिल आश्वतराश्विस्ते हैते महाशाला महाश्रोत्रियाः समेत्य मीमांसां चक्रुः को न आत्मा किं ब्रह्मेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: prācīnaśāla aupamanyavaḥ satyayajñaḥ pauluṣir indradyumno bhāllaveyo janaḥ śārkarākṣyo buḍila āśvatarāśvis te haite mahāśālā mahāśrotriyāḥ sametya mīmāṃsāṃ cakruḥ ko na ātmā kiṃ brahmeti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Prācīnaśāla Aupamanyava, Satyayajña Pauluṣi, Indradyumna Bhāllaveya, Jana Śārkarākṣya, and Buḍila Āśvatarāśvi — these great householders and great Vedic scholars came together and deliberated: 'What is our Self? What is Brahman?'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The two questions — 'what is the Self, what is Brahman?' — are really one; the vidyā will answer by uniting the inner Self with the universal (Vaiśvānara).
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Earnest joint inquiry by the learned is itself worship; the Lord will answer them through a king.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Even great scholars must seek further; the question of Self and Brahman is settled only by right tradition.

verses.dev: ते ह संपादयांचक्रुरुद्दालको वै भगवन्तोऽयमारुणिः संप्रतीममात्मानं वैश्वानरमध्येति तं हन्ताभ्यागच्छामेति तं हाभ्याजग्मुः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: te ha saṃpādayāṃ cakrur uddālako vai bhagavanto 'yam āruṇiḥ saṃpratīmam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram adhyeti taṃ hantābhyāgacchāmeti taṃ hābhyājagmuḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: They resolved: 'Sirs, Uddālaka Āruṇi here is at present studying this Vaiśvānara Self. Come, let us go to him.' And they went to him.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: They seek the reputed knower of the Universal Self — the Self 'common to all men' (viśva-nara).
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Vaiśvānara is the Lord as indweller of all; the search for His knower begins.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Vaiśvānara sought is Viṣṇu in His universal form; teachers are weighed before being approached.

verses.dev: स ह संपादयांचकार प्रक्ष्यन्ति मामिमे महाशाला महाश्रोत्रियास्तेभ्यो न सर्वमिव प्रतिपत्स्ये हन्ताहमन्यमभ्यनुशासानीति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ha saṃpādayāṃ cakāra prakṣyanti mām ime mahāśālā mahāśrotriyās tebhyo na sarvam iva pratipatsye hantāham anyam abhyanuśāsānīti || 3 ||
verses.trans: He reflected: 'These great householders and great scholars will question me, and I shall not be able to tell them everything. Come, I will direct them to another.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Uddālaka's honesty about his limits is exemplary; the would-be teacher becomes a fellow student — pride has no place near this knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Humility in the learned opens the road to the king who holds the full vidyā.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Admitting partial knowledge honours the truth; the Lord guides seekers through honest guides.

verses.dev: तान्होवाचाश्वपतिर्वै भगवन्तोऽयं कैकेयः संप्रतीममात्मानं वैश्वानरमध्येति तं हन्ताभ्यागच्छामेति तं हाभ्याजग्मुः ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tān hovācāśvapatir vai bhagavanto 'yaṃ kaikeyaḥ saṃpratīmam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram adhyeti taṃ hantābhyāgacchāmeti taṃ hābhyājagmuḥ || 4 ||
verses.trans: He said to them: 'Sirs, Aśvapati Kaikeya is at present studying this Vaiśvānara Self. Come, let us go to him.' And they went to him.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Again a kṣatriya king holds the higher doctrine; the brāhmaṇas, led by Uddālaka himself, go as students.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord lodges His vidyā where He wills; rank yields to knowledge.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The six now approach the appointed custodian of the universal-form meditation.

verses.dev: तेभ्यो ह प्राप्तेभ्यः पृथगर्हाणि कारयांचकार स ह प्रातः संजिहान उवाच न मे स्तेनो जनपदे न कदर्यो न मद्यपो नानाहिताग्निर्नाविद्वान्न स्वैरी स्वैरिणी कुतो यक्ष्यमाणो वै भगवन्तोऽहमस्मि यावदेकैकस्मा ऋत्विजे धनं दास्यामि तावद्भगवद्भ्यो दास्यामि वसन्तु भगवन्त इति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: tebhyo ha prāptebhyaḥ pṛthag arhāṇi kārayāṃ cakāra sa ha prātaḥ saṃjihāna uvāca na me steno janapade na kadaryo na madyapo nānāhitāgnir nāvidvān na svairī svairiṇī kuto yakṣyamāṇo vai bhagavanto 'ham asmi yāvad ekaikasmā ṛtvije dhanaṃ dāsyāmi tāvad bhagavadbhyo dāsyāmi vasantu bhagavanta iti || 5 ||
verses.trans: When they arrived he had honours shown to each separately. Rising the next morning he said: 'In my kingdom there is no thief, no miser, no drunkard, no one without the sacred fire, no one ignorant, no licentious man — how then a licentious woman? I am about to perform a sacrifice, sirs; as much wealth as I shall give to each priest, so much shall I give to you. Stay, revered sirs.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The king's account of his realm is his credential: a kingdom mirrors its ruler's inner order. He offers wealth first, as duty requires of a host.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Righteous governance flows from meditation on the universal Lord; the king's virtues authenticate his vidyā.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The flawless kingdom witnesses the worth of its knower-king, fit custodian of the Lord's doctrine.

verses.dev: ते होचुर्येन हैवार्थेन पुरुषश्चरेत्तं हैव वदेदात्मानमेवेमं वैश्वानरं संप्रत्यध्येषि तमेव नो ब्रूहीति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: te hocur yena haivārthena puruṣaś caret taṃ haiva vaded ātmānam evemaṃ vaiśvānaraṃ saṃpraty adhyeṣi tam eva no brūhīti || 6 ||
verses.trans: They said: 'A man should speak only of the purpose for which he has come. You are at present studying the Vaiśvānara Self; tell us of that.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: They decline riches and ask for the Self — the only wealth seekers of this kind will carry away.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Single-minded petition: not the king's gold but the Lord's knowledge.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: True seekers state their object plainly; the vidyā is the boon.

verses.dev: तान्होवाच प्रातर्वः प्रतिवक्तास्मीति ते ह समित्पाणयः पूर्वाह्णे प्रतिचक्रमिरे तान्होवाचानुपनीयैव ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: tān hovāca prātar vaḥ prativaktāsmīti te ha samitpāṇayaḥ pūrvāhṇe praticakramire tān hovācānupanīyaiva || 7 ||
verses.trans: He said to them: 'I shall answer you in the morning.' In the forenoon they returned to him with fuel in their hands (as students approach a teacher). Without even initiating them, he spoke:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Great scholars carry the student's fuel — the eternal protocol of discipleship. The king, honouring their ripeness, waives the formal initiation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Their humility completes their qualification; grace dispenses with ceremony for the ready.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fuel in hand seals their submission; the teaching may now be given.

verses.dev: औपमन्यव कं त्वमात्मानमुपास्स इति दिवमेव भगवो राजन्निति होवाचैष वै सुतेजा आत्मा वैश्वानरो यं त्वमात्मानमुपास्से तस्मात्तव सुतं प्रसुतमासुतं कुले दृश्यते ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: aupamanyava kaṃ tvam ātmānam upāssa iti divam eva bhagavo rājann iti hovācaiṣa vai sutejā ātmā vaiśvānaro yaṃ tvam ātmānam upāsse tasmāt tava sutaṃ prasutam āsutaṃ kule dṛśyate || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Aupamanyava, what do you meditate on as the Self?' — 'Heaven, revered king.' He said: 'What you meditate on is the Vaiśvānara Self called the Brilliant (Sutejas); therefore in your family the soma pressings — pressed, more-pressed, and after-pressed — are seen.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Heaven is only the head of the Universal Self. Each sage has mistaken a limb for the whole — the vidyā corrects part-worship into whole-vision.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Heaven is the crown of the Lord's cosmic body; meditation on it yields brilliance but remains partial.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord's head is the bright heaven; worship of a member brings its fruit, not the Lord entire.

verses.dev: अत्स्यन्नं पश्यसि प्रियमत्त्यन्नं पश्यति प्रियं भवत्यस्य ब्रह्मवर्चसं कुले य एतमेवमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते मूर्धा त्वेष आत्मन इति होवाच मूर्धा ते व्यपतिष्यद्यन्मां नागमिष्य इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atsy annaṃ paśyasi priyam atty annaṃ paśyati priyaṃ bhavaty asya brahmavarcasaṃ kule ya etam evam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram upāste mūrdhā tv eṣa ātmana iti hovāca mūrdhā te vyapatiṣyad yan māṃ nāgamiṣya iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'You eat food and see what is dear; he who meditates on the Vaiśvānara Self thus eats food, sees what is dear, and the lustre of sacred knowledge is in his family. But this is only the head of the Self,' he said, 'and your head would have fallen off had you not come to me.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Part-meditation carries part-danger: to take the head for the whole imperils the head. The warning drives home the necessity of integral knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Worship of a limb as the whole Lord courts ruin; grace brought the sage to correction in time.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Error about the Lord's form endangers its corresponding member; right tradition saves.

verses.dev: अथ होवाच सत्ययज्ञं पौलुषिं प्राचीनयोग्य कं त्वमात्मानमुपास्स इत्यादित्यमेव भगवो राजन्निति होवाचैष वै विश्वरूप आत्मा वैश्वानरो यं त्वमात्मानमुपास्से तस्मात्तव बहु विश्वरूपं कुले दृश्यते ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hovāca satyayajñaṃ pauluṣiṃ prācīnayogya kaṃ tvam ātmānam upāssa ity ādityam eva bhagavo rājann iti hovācaiṣa vai viśvarūpa ātmā vaiśvānaro yaṃ tvam ātmānam upāsse tasmāt tava bahu viśvarūpaṃ kule dṛśyate || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then he said to Satyayajña Pauluṣi: 'Prācīnayogya, what do you meditate on as the Self?' — 'The sun, revered king.' He said: 'What you meditate on is the Vaiśvānara Self called the Many-formed (Viśvarūpa); therefore much that is manifold is seen in your family.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The sun is the eye of the Universal Self — another limb honoured as the whole.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The sun's many-formed glory is the Lord's eye; rich but partial.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord's eye, worshipped alone, gives manifold fruit, not the vision of Him.

verses.dev: प्रवृत्तोऽश्वतरीरथो दासीनिष्कोऽत्स्यन्नं पश्यसि प्रियमत्त्यन्नं पश्यति प्रियं भवत्यस्य ब्रह्मवर्चसं कुले य एतमेवमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते चक्षुष्ट्वेतदात्मन इति होवाचान्धोऽभविष्यो यन्मां नागमिष्य इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: pravṛtto 'śvatarīratho dāsīniṣko 'tsy annaṃ paśyasi priyam atty annaṃ paśyati priyaṃ bhavaty asya brahmavarcasaṃ kule ya etam evam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram upāste cakṣuṣ ṭv etad ātmana iti hovācāndho 'bhaviṣyo yan māṃ nāgamiṣya iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'You have a mule-chariot, a maidservant, a gold ornament; you eat food and see what is dear — such fruits has this meditation. But this is only the eye of the Self,' he said, 'and you would have gone blind had you not come to me.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Eye mistaken for the whole risks blindness; each error endangers its own organ.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Partial worship, partial peril; the king's correction is the Lord's rescue.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Fruits flow, yet truncated doctrine maims; the warning is mercy.

verses.dev: अथ होवाचेन्द्रद्युम्नं भाल्लवेयं वैयाघ्रपद्य कं त्वमात्मानमुपास्स इति वायुमेव भगवो राजन्निति होवाचैष वै पृथग्वर्त्मात्मा वैश्वानरो यं त्वमात्मानमुपास्से तस्मात्त्वां पृथग्बलय आयन्ति पृथग्रथश्रेणयोऽनुयन्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hovācendradyumnaṃ bhāllaveyaṃ vaiyāghrapadya kaṃ tvam ātmānam upāssa iti vāyum eva bhagavo rājann iti hovācaiṣa vai pṛthagvartmātmā vaiśvānaro yaṃ tvam ātmānam upāsse tasmāt tvāṃ pṛthag balaya āyanti pṛthag rathaśreṇayo 'nuyanti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then he said to Indradyumna Bhāllaveya: 'Vaiyāghrapadya, what do you meditate on as the Self?' — 'The wind, revered king.' He said: 'What you meditate on is the Vaiśvānara Self called the One of Diverse Paths (Pṛthagvartman); therefore offerings come to you from diverse sides and rows of chariots follow you diversely.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Wind is the breath of the Universal Self; its worshipper draws tribute from every quarter — yet holds only a limb.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's breath blows on many roads; its honour brings many-sided gain.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Vāyu, exalted as he is, is here the Lord's breath-member, not the whole to be worshipped alone.

verses.dev: अत्स्यन्नं पश्यसि प्रियमत्त्यन्नं पश्यति प्रियं भवत्यस्य ब्रह्मवर्चसं कुले य एतमेवमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते प्राणस्त्वेष आत्मन इति होवाच प्राणस्त उदक्रमिष्यद्यन्मां नागमिष्य इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atsy annaṃ paśyasi priyam atty annaṃ paśyati priyaṃ bhavaty asya brahmavarcasaṃ kule ya etam evam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram upāste prāṇas tv eṣa ātmana iti hovāca prāṇas ta udakramiṣyad yan māṃ nāgamiṣya iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'You eat food and see what is dear — such fruits has this meditation. But this is only the breath of the Self,' he said, 'and your breath would have departed had you not come to me.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Breath taken for the whole imperils breath itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The correction saves the sage's very life-air.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Partial doctrine, mortal risk; the king's word restores order.

verses.dev: अथ होवाच जनं शार्कराक्ष्य कं त्वमात्मानमुपास्स इत्याकाशमेव भगवो राजन्निति होवाचैष वै बहुल आत्मा वैश्वानरो यं त्वमात्मानमुपास्से तस्मात्त्वं बहुलोऽसि प्रजया च धनेन च ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hovāca janaṃ śārkarākṣya kaṃ tvam ātmānam upāssa ity ākāśam eva bhagavo rājann iti hovācaiṣa vai bahula ātmā vaiśvānaro yaṃ tvam ātmānam upāsse tasmāt tvaṃ bahulo 'si prajayā ca dhanena ca || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then he said to Jana: 'Śārkarākṣya, what do you meditate on as the Self?' — 'Space, revered king.' He said: 'What you meditate on is the Vaiśvānara Self called the Abundant (Bahula); therefore you abound in offspring and wealth.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Space is the ample trunk of the Universal Self; abundance is its fruit, partiality its flaw.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's spacious middle blesses with plenty those who honour it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The trunk-member gives abundance; the whole Lord gives Himself.

verses.dev: अत्स्यन्नं पश्यसि प्रियमत्त्यन्नं पश्यति प्रियं भवत्यस्य ब्रह्मवर्चसं कुले य एतमेवमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते संदेहस्त्वेष आत्मन इति होवाच संदेहस्ते व्यशीर्यद्यन्मां नागमिष्य इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atsy annaṃ paśyasi priyam atty annaṃ paśyati priyaṃ bhavaty asya brahmavarcasaṃ kule ya etam evam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram upāste saṃdehas tv eṣa ātmana iti hovāca saṃdehas te vyaśīryad yan māṃ nāgamiṣya iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'You eat food and see what is dear — such fruits has this meditation. But this is only the trunk of the Self,' he said, 'and your trunk would have crumbled had you not come to me.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The body's middle answers for the meditation's middle-sized error.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Timely correction preserves the worshipper whole.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each limb-error endangers its limb; the pattern teaches precision in doctrine.

verses.dev: अथ होवाच बुडिलमाश्वतराश्विं वैयाघ्रपद्य कं त्वमात्मानमुपास्स इत्यप एव भगवो राजन्निति होवाचैष वै रयिरात्मा वैश्वानरो यं त्वमात्मानमुपास्से तस्मात्त्वं रयिमान्पुष्टिमानसि ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hovāca buḍilam āśvatarāśviṃ vaiyāghrapadya kaṃ tvam ātmānam upāssa ity apa eva bhagavo rājann iti hovācaiṣa vai rayir ātmā vaiśvānaro yaṃ tvam ātmānam upāsse tasmāt tvaṃ rayimān puṣṭimān asi || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then he said to Buḍila Āśvatarāśvi: 'Vaiyāghrapadya, what do you meditate on as the Self?' — 'Water, revered king.' He said: 'What you meditate on is the Vaiśvānara Self called Wealth (Rayi); therefore you are wealthy and flourishing.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Water, the world's wealth, is but the lower vessel of the Universal Self.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's waters enrich their worshipper, yet are not His fullness.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The water-member yields prosperity under the Lord's dispensation.

verses.dev: अत्स्यन्नं पश्यसि प्रियमत्त्यन्नं पश्यति प्रियं भवत्यस्य ब्रह्मवर्चसं कुले य एतमेवमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते बस्तिस्त्वेष आत्मन इति होवाच बस्तिस्ते व्यभेत्स्यद्यन्मां नागमिष्य इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atsy annaṃ paśyasi priyam atty annaṃ paśyati priyaṃ bhavaty asya brahmavarcasaṃ kule ya etam evam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram upāste bastis tv eṣa ātmana iti hovāca bastis te vyabhetsyad yan māṃ nāgamiṣya iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'You eat food and see what is dear — such fruits has this meditation. But this is only the bladder of the Self,' he said, 'and your bladder would have burst had you not come to me.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even the humblest organ has its cosmic counterpart; no part may pose as the whole.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's body includes the lowly; error about any member harms it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Correction descends to the least limb — completeness matters everywhere.

verses.dev: अथ होवाचोद्दालकमारुणिं गौतम कं त्वमात्मानमुपास्स इति पृथिवीमेव भगवो राजन्निति होवाचैष वै प्रतिष्ठात्मा वैश्वानरो यं त्वमात्मानमुपास्से तस्मात्त्वं प्रतिष्ठितोऽसि प्रजया च पशुभिश्च ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha hovācoddālakam āruṇiṃ gautama kaṃ tvam ātmānam upāssa iti pṛthivīm eva bhagavo rājann iti hovācaiṣa vai pratiṣṭhātmā vaiśvānaro yaṃ tvam ātmānam upāsse tasmāt tvaṃ pratiṣṭhito 'si prajayā ca paśubhiś ca || 1 ||
verses.trans: Then he said to Uddālaka Āruṇi: 'Gautama, what do you meditate on as the Self?' — 'The earth, revered king.' He said: 'What you meditate on is the Vaiśvānara Self called the Firm Basis (Pratiṣṭhā); therefore you stand firm in offspring and cattle.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even Uddālaka, future teacher of 'Tat tvam asi', here worships only the feet — the greatest may hold but a part until the whole is shown.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Earth, the Lord's footing, steadies its worshipper; the king will now assemble the limbs.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The feet-member grants stability; the integral vision follows.

verses.dev: अत्स्यन्नं पश्यसि प्रियमत्त्यन्नं पश्यति प्रियं भवत्यस्य ब्रह्मवर्चसं कुले य एतमेवमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते पादौ त्वेतावात्मन इति होवाच पादौ ते व्यम्लास्येतां यन्मां नागमिष्य इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atsy annaṃ paśyasi priyam atty annaṃ paśyati priyaṃ bhavaty asya brahmavarcasaṃ kule ya etam evam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram upāste pādau tv etāv ātmana iti hovāca pādau te vyamlāsyetāṃ yan māṃ nāgamiṣya iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'You eat food and see what is dear — such fruits has this meditation. But these are only the feet of the Self,' he said, 'and your feet would have withered had you not come to me.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The last limb named, the last warning given; the stage is set for the whole.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Six members, six corrections — now the Lord's full form will be taught.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The survey of members complete, the integral meditation follows.

verses.dev: तान्होवाचैते वै खलु यूयं पृथगिवेममात्मानं वैश्वानरं विद्वांसोऽन्नमत्थ यस्त्वेतमेवं प्रादेशमात्रमभिविमानमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते स सर्वेषु लोकेषु सर्वेषु भूतेषु सर्वेष्वात्मस्वन्नमत्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: tān hovācaite vai khalu yūyaṃ pṛthag ivemam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaraṃ vidvāṃso 'nnam attha yas tv etam evaṃ prādeśamātram abhivimānam ātmānaṃ vaiśvānaram upāste sa sarveṣu lokeṣu sarveṣu bhūteṣu sarveṣv ātmasv annam atti || 1 ||
verses.trans: He said to them: 'You, knowing this Vaiśvānara Self as if separate (limb by limb), eat your food. But he who meditates on the Vaiśvānara Self as a span in measure (from heaven to earth, or within the body) and as identified with himself — he eats food in all worlds, in all beings, in all selves.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The king's resolution: the Self is to be meditated whole — 'prādeśamātra' (span-measured) as dwelling between crown and chin, 'abhivimāna' as one's own self. The knower of the whole eats in all beings: he is the one eater everywhere — Śaṅkara hears the universal Self announced.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord, though spanning heaven and earth, is meditated within the small measure of the heart as one's own inner Self; such a worshipper shares the Lord's universal enjoyment.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The span-measured Vaiśvānara is Viṣṇu meditated in the heart; the integral worshipper receives sustenance in every world from Him.

verses.dev: तस्य ह वा एतस्यात्मनो वैश्वानरस्य मूर्धैव सुतेजाश्चक्षुर्विश्वरूपः प्राणः पृथग्वर्त्मात्मा संदेहो बहुलो बस्तिरेव रयिः पृथिव्येव पादावुर एव वेदिर्लोमानि बर्हिर्हृदयं गार्हपत्यो मनोऽन्वाहार्यपचन आस्यमाहवनीयः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasya ha vā etasyātmano vaiśvānarasya mūrdhaiva sutejāś cakṣur viśvarūpaḥ prāṇaḥ pṛthagvartmātmā saṃdeho bahulo bastir eva rayiḥ pṛthivy eva pādāv ura eva vedir lomāni barhir hṛdayaṃ gārhapatyo mano 'nvāhāryapacana āsyam āhavanīyaḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: Of this Vaiśvānara Self, the head is the Brilliant (heaven), the eye the Many-formed (sun), the breath the One of Diverse Paths (wind), the trunk the Abundant (space), the bladder Wealth (water), the feet the earth. (In the rite of eating,) the chest is the altar, the hairs the sacred grass, the heart the Gārhapatya fire, the mind the Anvāhāryapacana fire, the mouth the Āhavanīya fire.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The six partial meditations now assemble into one body of the Universal Self, and the eater's own frame becomes the sacrificial ground — the daily meal transfigured into Agnihotra. Knowledge gathers the scattered into the whole.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's cosmic form is composed before their eyes, and the devotee's body is His altar: eating becomes unbroken worship of the indweller.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The full form of the Lord assembled from His members crowns the instruction; the body's fires now serve the inner sacrifice that the next khaṇḍas detail.

verses.dev: तद्यद्भक्तं प्रथममागच्छेत्तद्धोमीयं स यां प्रथमामाहुतिं जुहुयात्तां जुहुयात्प्राणाय स्वाहेति प्राणस्तृप्यति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: tad yad bhaktaṃ prathamam āgacchet tad dhomīyaṃ sa yāṃ prathamām āhutiṃ juhuyāt tāṃ juhuyāt prāṇāya svāheti prāṇas tṛpyati || 1 ||
verses.trans: So whatever food comes first should be an offering. The first oblation one offers, one should offer with 'To prāṇa — svāhā!' Then prāṇa is satisfied.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The meal becomes Agnihotra: the first morsel offered to the out-breath. Eating with this knowledge is worship without altar or priest.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Every meal is the Lord's fire-rite; the first portion honours His vital presence within.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The bodily fire-sacrifice begins with prāṇa, the Lord's chief minister, duly fed first.

verses.dev: प्राणे तृप्यति चक्षुस्तृप्यति चक्षुषि तृप्यत्यादित्यस्तृप्यत्यादित्ये तृप्यति द्यौस्तृप्यति दिवि तृप्यन्त्यां यत्किंच द्यौश्चादित्यश्चाधितिष्ठतस्तत्तृप्यति तस्यानुतृप्तिं तृप्यति प्रजया पशुभिरन्नाद्येन तेजसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेनेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: prāṇe tṛpyati cakṣus tṛpyati cakṣuṣi tṛpyaty ādityas tṛpyaty āditye tṛpyati dyaus tṛpyati divi tṛpyantyāṃ yat kiṃca dyauś cādityaś cādhitiṣṭhatas tat tṛpyati tasyānutṛptiṃ tṛpyati prajayā paśubhir annādyena tejasā brahmavarcaseneti || 2 ||
verses.trans: When prāṇa is satisfied, the eye is satisfied; when the eye is satisfied, the sun is satisfied; when the sun is satisfied, heaven is satisfied; when heaven is satisfied, whatever heaven and the sun preside over is satisfied. Through its satisfaction the eater is satisfied with offspring, cattle, food, splendour, and the lustre of sacred knowledge.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: One morsel ripples outward: breath, eye, sun, heaven, and all beneath them. The microcosm feeds the macrocosm because both are one fabric.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Satisfaction ascends the chain of the Lord's body — the inner offering gladdens the cosmic hierarchy and returns as blessing.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The graded chain of satisfaction shows the Lord's ordered administration: feed His agent within, and the gods above are pleased.

verses.dev: अथ यां द्वितीयां जुहुयात्तां जुहुयाद्व्यानाय स्वाहेति व्यानस्तृप्यति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yāṃ dvitīyāṃ juhuyāt tāṃ juhuyād vyānāya svāheti vyānas tṛpyati || 1 ||
verses.trans: The second oblation one offers, one should offer with 'To vyāna — svāhā!' Then vyāna is satisfied.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The second morsel feeds the pervading breath that knits the body together.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Vyāna, the Lord's binding energy, receives the second portion.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each breath-officer is fed in order, as the rite prescribes.

verses.dev: व्याने तृप्यति श्रोत्रं तृप्यति श्रोत्रे तृप्यति चन्द्रमास्तृप्यति चन्द्रमसि तृप्यति दिशस्तृप्यन्ति दिक्षु तृप्यन्तीषु यत्किंच दिशश्च चन्द्रमाश्चाधितिष्ठन्ति तत्तृप्यति तस्यानुतृप्तिं तृप्यति प्रजया पशुभिरन्नाद्येन तेजसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेनेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: vyāne tṛpyati śrotraṃ tṛpyati śrotre tṛpyati candramās tṛpyati candramasi tṛpyati diśas tṛpyanti dikṣu tṛpyantīṣu yat kiṃca diśaś ca candramāś cādhitiṣṭhanti tat tṛpyati tasyānutṛptiṃ tṛpyati prajayā paśubhir annādyena tejasā brahmavarcaseneti || 2 ||
verses.trans: When vyāna is satisfied, the ear is satisfied; when the ear is satisfied, the moon is satisfied; when the moon is satisfied, the quarters are satisfied; when the quarters are satisfied, whatever the quarters and the moon preside over is satisfied. Through its satisfaction the eater is satisfied with offspring, cattle, food, splendour, and the lustre of sacred knowledge.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The chain runs through ear, moon, and the quarters — a second artery of the one cosmic circulation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The lunar line of the Lord's body is gladdened by the second offering.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The second chain of satisfaction proceeds by the same divine order.

verses.dev: अथ यां तृतीयां जुहुयात्तां जुहुयादपानाय स्वाहेत्यपानस्तृप्यति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yāṃ tṛtīyāṃ juhuyāt tāṃ juhuyād apānāya svāhety apānas tṛpyati || 1 ||
verses.trans: The third oblation one offers, one should offer with 'To apāna — svāhā!' Then apāna is satisfied.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The third morsel to the downward breath, keeper of elimination and rootedness.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Apāna serves the Lord below as prāṇa above; both are honoured.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The downward officer receives his due portion.

verses.dev: अपाने तृप्यति वाक्तृप्यति वाचि तृप्यन्त्यामग्निस्तृप्यत्यग्नौ तृप्यति पृथिवी तृप्यति पृथिव्यां तृप्यन्त्यां यत्किंच पृथिवी चाग्निश्चाधितिष्ठतस्तत्तृप्यति तस्यानुतृप्तिं तृप्यति प्रजया पशुभिरन्नाद्येन तेजसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेनेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: apāne tṛpyati vāk tṛpyati vāci tṛpyantyām agnis tṛpyaty agnau tṛpyati pṛthivī tṛpyati pṛthivyāṃ tṛpyantyāṃ yat kiṃca pṛthivī cāgniś cādhitiṣṭhatas tat tṛpyati tasyānutṛptiṃ tṛpyati prajayā paśubhir annādyena tejasā brahmavarcaseneti || 2 ||
verses.trans: When apāna is satisfied, speech is satisfied; when speech is satisfied, fire is satisfied; when fire is satisfied, the earth is satisfied; when the earth is satisfied, whatever the earth and fire preside over is satisfied. Through its satisfaction the eater is satisfied with offspring, cattle, food, splendour, and the lustre of sacred knowledge.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Speech, fire, earth — the third circuit grounds the offering in the terrestrial order.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The earthly line of the Lord's form receives its share of gladness.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Earth and fire, His servants, are pleased through their inner counterpart.

verses.dev: अथ यां चतुर्थीं जुहुयात्तां जुहुयात्समानाय स्वाहेति समानस्तृप्यति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yāṃ caturthīṃ juhuyāt tāṃ juhuyāt samānāya svāheti samānas tṛpyati || 1 ||
verses.trans: The fourth oblation one offers, one should offer with 'To samāna — svāhā!' Then samāna is satisfied.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The equalising breath, digester at the body's centre, takes the fourth portion.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Samāna distributes the Lord's nourishment evenly; he too is fed.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The central officer of digestion receives his oblation.

verses.dev: समाने तृप्यति मनस्तृप्यति मनसि तृप्यति पर्जन्यस्तृप्यति पर्जन्ये तृप्यति विद्युत्तृप्यति विद्युति तृप्यन्त्यां यत्किंच विद्युच्च पर्जन्यश्चाधितिष्ठतस्तत्तृप्यति तस्यानुतृप्तिं तृप्यति प्रजया पशुभिरन्नाद्येन तेजसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेनेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: samāne tṛpyati manas tṛpyati manasi tṛpyati parjanyas tṛpyati parjanye tṛpyati vidyut tṛpyati vidyuti tṛpyantyāṃ yat kiṃca vidyuc ca parjanyaś cādhitiṣṭhatas tat tṛpyati tasyānutṛptiṃ tṛpyati prajayā paśubhir annādyena tejasā brahmavarcaseneti || 2 ||
verses.trans: When samāna is satisfied, the mind is satisfied; when the mind is satisfied, the rain-god is satisfied; when the rain-god is satisfied, lightning is satisfied; when lightning is satisfied, whatever lightning and the rain-god preside over is satisfied. Through its satisfaction the eater is satisfied with offspring, cattle, food, splendour, and the lustre of sacred knowledge.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Mind, rain, lightning — the subtle circuit of thought and storm gladdened by the fourth morsel.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The mental-meteorological line of the Lord's body is satisfied in turn.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fourth chain ascends to the storm-gods under the Lord.

verses.dev: अथ यां पञ्चमीं जुहुयात्तां जुहुयादुदानाय स्वाहेत्युदानस्तृप्यति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yāṃ pañcamīṃ juhuyāt tāṃ juhuyād udānāya svāhety udānas tṛpyati || 1 ||
verses.trans: The fifth oblation one offers, one should offer with 'To udāna — svāhā!' Then udāna is satisfied.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The ascending breath, which will one day carry the soul upward, receives the final portion.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Udāna, bearer of the soul on its last journey, is honoured at every meal.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fifth officer completes the round of the inner Agnihotra.

verses.dev: उदाने तृप्यति त्वक्तृप्यति त्वचि तृप्यन्त्यां वायुस्तृप्यति वायौ तृप्यत्याकाशस्तृप्यत्याकाशे तृप्यति यत्किंच वायुश्चाकाशश्चाधितिष्ठतस्तत्तृप्यति तस्यानुतृप्तिं तृप्यति प्रजया पशुभिरन्नाद्येन तेजसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेनेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: udāne tṛpyati tvak tṛpyati tvaci tṛpyantyāṃ vāyus tṛpyati vāyau tṛpyaty ākāśas tṛpyaty ākāśe tṛpyati yat kiṃca vāyuś cākāśaś cādhitiṣṭhatas tat tṛpyati tasyānutṛptiṃ tṛpyati prajayā paśubhir annādyena tejasā brahmavarcaseneti || 2 ||
verses.trans: When udāna is satisfied, the skin is satisfied; when the skin is satisfied, the wind is satisfied; when the wind is satisfied, space is satisfied; when space is satisfied, whatever the wind and space preside over is satisfied. Through its satisfaction the eater is satisfied with offspring, cattle, food, splendour, and the lustre of sacred knowledge.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Skin, wind, space — the widest circuit closes the five; one mindful meal has fed the universe.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The fifth chain reaches the Lord's most pervasive members; the whole cosmos shares the devotee's table.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All five chains complete, the entire hierarchy is satisfied through the body's small fire.

verses.dev: स य इदमविद्वानग्निहोत्रं जुहोति यथाङ्गारानपोह्य भस्मनि जुहुयात्तादृक्तत्स्यात् ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya idam avidvān agnihotraṃ juhoti yathāṅgārān apohya bhasmani juhuyāt tādṛk tat syāt || 1 ||
verses.trans: He who offers the Agnihotra without knowing this — that would be as if he removed the live coals and poured his offering on dead ashes.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Ritual minus knowledge is oblation on ash: form without fire. Śaṅkara's recurring verdict — understanding is the live coal of every act.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Worship unanimated by knowledge of the Lord's indwelling misses the living flame of His presence.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Acts without right knowledge of the Lord's order are cold offerings; knowledge kindles them.

verses.dev: अथ य एतदेवं विद्वानग्निहोत्रं जुहोति तस्य सर्वेषु लोकेषु सर्वेषु भूतेषु सर्वेष्वात्मसु हुतं भवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atha ya etad evaṃ vidvān agnihotraṃ juhoti tasya sarveṣu lokeṣu sarveṣu bhūteṣu sarveṣv ātmasu hutaṃ bhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: But he who offers the Agnihotra knowing this — his offering is made in all worlds, in all beings, in all selves.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Knowing the Vaiśvānara, one's single offering lands everywhere — for the knower's self is the Self of all.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Offered to the Lord within, the oblation reaches Him in every world and creature He ensouls.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Through the Lord's pervading administration, the knower's oblation is credited universally.

verses.dev: तद्यथेषीकातूलमग्नौ प्रोतं प्रदूयेतैवं हास्य सर्वे पाप्मानः प्रदूयन्ते य एतदेवं विद्वानग्निहोत्रं जुहोति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tad yatheṣīkātūlam agnau protaṃ pradūyetaivaṃ hāsya sarve pāpmānaḥ pradūyante ya etad evaṃ vidvān agnihotraṃ juhoti || 3 ||
verses.trans: As a tuft of reed-cotton laid on a fire is burnt up, so are all the sins of him burnt up who, knowing this, offers the Agnihotra.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The famous image: sins flare to nothing like cotton on flame in the fire of knowledge-informed action.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's accepted offering consumes the offerer's sins instantly and utterly.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Accumulated sin is incinerated for the knower by the Lord's grace through the rite.

verses.dev: तस्मादु हैवंविद्यद्यपि चण्डालायोच्छिष्टं प्रयच्छेदात्मनि हैवास्य तद्वैश्वानरे हुतं स्यादिति तदेष श्लोकः ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tasmād u haivaṃvid yady api caṇḍālāyocchiṣṭaṃ prayacched ātmani haivāsya tad vaiśvānare hutaṃ syād iti tad eṣa ślokaḥ || 4 ||
verses.trans: Therefore, even if one who knows this should give his leavings to an outcaste, it would be offered in that very Vaiśvānara Self (in him too). On this there is this verse:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The radical consequence: the one Self dwells in the outcaste also; given with knowledge, even leavings are oblation to the Universal. Śaṅkara: the vidyā dissolves the walls the world builds.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord indwells every body; what is given to any of His dwellings reaches Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord present in all receives what is given anywhere with knowledge of Him.

verses.dev: यथेह क्षुधिता बाला मातरं पर्युपासत एवं सर्वाणि भूतान्यग्निहोत्रमुपासत इत्यग्निहोत्रमुपासत इति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: yatheha kṣudhitā bālā mātaraṃ paryupāsata evaṃ sarvāṇi bhūtāny agnihotram upāsata ity agnihotram upāsata iti || 5 ||
verses.trans: 'As hungry children here sit round their mother, so do all beings sit round the Agnihotra — sit round the Agnihotra.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The closing śloka: the whole creation waits like children on the knower's inner sacrifice — the universe nourished from one wise meal. The repetition ends Chapter 5.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All beings cluster as children at the mother's lap around the worship of the indwelling Lord — the image of His universal motherhood.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Creation depends on the rite of the wise as children on the mother; the Lord feeds the world through His knowers.

verses.dev: श्वेतकेतुर्हारुणेय आस तं ह पितोवाच श्वेतकेतो वस ब्रह्मचर्यं न वै सोम्यास्मत्कुलीनोऽननूच्य ब्रह्मबन्धुरिव भवतीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: śvetaketur hāruṇeya āsa taṃ ha pitovāca śvetaketo vasa brahmacaryaṃ na vai somyāsmatkulīno 'nanūcya brahmabandhur iva bhavatīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: There was Śvetaketu Āruṇeya. His father said to him: 'Śvetaketu, live the life of a student of sacred knowledge. No one of our family, gentle one, remains unlearned, a brāhmaṇa only by kinship.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The opening of the Sad-vidyā, crown of the Upaniṣad. The father's charge — not to be a brāhmaṇa in name only — sets the theme: reality over label, being over designation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The family's tradition of learning is the channel of grace; the father sends the son to be formed in it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Birth without learning is an empty title; the boy is dispatched to earn the substance of his name.

verses.dev: स ह द्वादशवर्ष उपेत्य चतुर्विंशतिवर्षः सर्वान्वेदानधीत्य महामना अनूचानमानी स्तब्ध एयाय तं ह पितोवाच ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa ha dvādaśavarṣa upetya caturviṃśativarṣaḥ sarvān vedān adhītya mahāmanā anūcānamānī stabdha eyāya taṃ ha pitovāca || 2 ||
verses.trans: Having gone at the age of twelve, he returned at twenty-four, having studied all the Vedas — conceited, thinking himself learned, and stiff with pride. His father said to him:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Twelve years of Veda yielded pride, not wisdom — text-learning without knowledge of the Self inflates. The father will now deflate and then truly teach.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Mastery of words without the Lord they signify breeds stiffness; the cure is the doctrine of the One.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Learning is completed, humility is not; the father begins the higher course.

verses.dev: श्वेतकेतो यन्नु सोम्येदं महामना अनूचानमानी स्तब्धोऽस्युत तमादेशमप्राक्ष्यो येनाश्रुतं श्रुतं भवत्यमतं मतमविज्ञातं विज्ञातमिति कथं नु भगवः स आदेशो भवतीति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: śvetaketo yan nu somyedaṃ mahāmanā anūcānamānī stabdho 'sy uta tam ādeśam aprākṣyo yenāśrutaṃ śrutaṃ bhavaty amataṃ matam avijñātaṃ vijñātam iti kathaṃ nu bhagavaḥ sa ādeśo bhavatīti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Śvetaketu, since you are now so conceited, thinking yourself learned, and stiff — did you ask for that teaching by which the unheard becomes heard, the unthought thought, the unknown known?' — 'How, revered sir, can there be such a teaching?'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The question of questions: what single knowledge reveals all? Śaṅkara — only if all is in essence one thing can knowing it reveal everything; the question itself implies non-duality.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: To know the cause is to know all effects: knowing the Lord, the soul knows all that is His body — Rāmānuja reads the promise through the cause-effect relation of Brahman and world.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: knowing the Lord, the chief of all, one knows all in principle — as knowing the king one knows the kingdom's order; identity of all things is not implied.

verses.dev: यथा सोम्यैकेन मृत्पिण्डेन सर्वं मृन्मयं विज्ञातं स्याद्वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं मृत्तिकेत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: yathā somyaikena mṛtpiṇḍena sarvaṃ mṛnmayaṃ vijñātaṃ syād vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ mṛttikety eva satyam || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'Just as, gentle one, by one lump of clay all that is made of clay would be known — the modification is a mere name arising from speech, while the truth is just "clay" —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The charter text of vivarta: pots and jars are 'names arising from speech' (vācārambhaṇa); clay alone is real. So the world of effects is name-and-form upon the one Being — knowing It, all is known. Śaṅkara builds his entire doctrine of the non-difference of effect and cause here.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Rāmānuja: the effect is the cause in a new state — clay really becomes pot. 'Name' designates the new configuration, not an illusion. So the world is a real transformation of Brahman's body, and knowing Brahman one knows His every state.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the example teaches that effects depend wholly on their material; knowledge of the independent cause (Viṣṇu) gives knowledge of all dependents — dependence, not identity, is the point.

verses.dev: यथा सोम्यैकेन लोहमणिना सर्वं लोहमयं विज्ञातं स्याद्वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं लोहमित्येव सत्यम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: yathā somyaikena lohamaṇinā sarvaṃ lohamayaṃ vijñātaṃ syād vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ loham ity eva satyam || 5 ||
verses.trans: 'just as by one nugget of gold all that is made of gold would be known — the modification is a mere name arising from speech, while the truth is just "gold" —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Ornaments differ in name and use; gold alone persists through them all — the second witness to the sole reality of the substrate.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Gold truly assumes the bracelet's form; the underlying substance unifies real modifications.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All golden things are known through gold as dependents through their source.

verses.dev: यथा सोम्यैकेन नखनिकृन्तनेन सर्वं कार्ष्णायसं विज्ञातं स्याद्वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं कृष्णायसमित्येव सत्यमेवं सोम्य स आदेशो भवतीति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: yathā somyaikena nakhanikṛntanena sarvaṃ kārṣṇāyasaṃ vijñātaṃ syād vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ kṛṣṇāyasam ity eva satyam evaṃ somya sa ādeśo bhavatīti || 6 ||
verses.trans: 'and just as by one nail-cutter all that is made of iron would be known — the modification is a mere name arising from speech, while the truth is just "iron" — even so, gentle one, is that teaching.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Clay, gold, iron: three substrates, one lesson — difference belongs to speech, being to the substrate. The 'teaching' is thus the knowledge of Being itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Three examples of real causal continuity prepare the doctrine of Brahman as the world's material and efficient cause.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The examples illustrate knowledge-by-the-chief; the teaching proper will name the One on whom all depends.

verses.dev: न वै नूनं भगवन्तस्त एतदवेदिषुर्यद्ध्येतदवेदिष्यन्कथं मे नावक्ष्यन्निति भगवांस्त्वेव मे तद्ब्रवीत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: na vai nūnaṃ bhagavantas ta etad avediṣur yad dhy etad avediṣyan kathaṃ me nāvakṣyann iti bhagavāṃs tv eva me tad bravītv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 7 ||
verses.trans: 'Surely my revered teachers did not know this; for had they known it, why would they not have told me? So may you, revered sir, tell it to me.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Pride dissolves into discipleship at home: the son asks his father for what twelve years abroad never gave.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The humbled son becomes the fit vessel; the father consents as the Lord's instrument.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Right asking precedes right teaching; the great instruction begins.

verses.dev: सदेव सोम्येदमग्र आसीदेकमेवाद्वितीयम् । तद्धैक आहुरसदेवेदमग्र आसीदेकमेवाद्वितीयं तस्मादसतः सज्जायत ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: sad eva somyedam agra āsīd ekam evādvitīyam | tad dhaika āhur asad evedam agra āsīd ekam evādvitīyaṃ tasmād asataḥ saj jāyata || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'In the beginning, gentle one, this was Being alone — one only, without a second. Some say that in the beginning this was Non-being alone, one only, without a second, and that from Non-being Being was born.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The mahāvākya-context opens: SAT — pure Being, one without a second (advitīya: no second of any kind, like or unlike or internal). Śaṅkara takes this as the definitive statement of non-dual Brahman, refuting the nihilist alternative cited next.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Being 'one without a second' means Brahman with souls and matter in subtle, name-form-free condition — no rival principle existed, but His body was ever with Him. The Lord alone, thus qualified, was.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: 'Being alone' affirms Viṣṇu's sole independence before creation; 'without a second' denies any equal or superior, not the existence of dependent realities in latent form.

verses.dev: कुतस्तु खलु सोम्यैवं स्यादिति होवाच कथमसतः सज्जायेतेति । सत्त्वेव सोम्येदमग्र आसीदेकमेवाद्वितीयम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: kutas tu khalu somyaivaṃ syād iti hovāca katham asataḥ saj jāyeteti | sat tv eva somyedam agra āsīd ekam evādvitīyam || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'But how, indeed, gentle one, could it be so?' he said. 'How could Being be born from Non-being? No — in the beginning this was Being alone, one only, without a second.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Ex nihilo is unthinkable: from nothing, nothing comes. Being is beginningless — the rational backbone of Vedānta's satkāryavāda.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The world's source must itself exist; the Lord, ever-existent, is that source.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Non-being produces nothing; the eternal Lord precedes and produces all.

verses.dev: तदैक्षत बहु स्यां प्रजायेयेति तत्तेजोऽसृजत तत्तेज ऐक्षत बहु स्यां प्रजायेयेति तदपोऽसृजत । तस्माद्यत्र क्वच शोचति स्वेदते वा पुरुषस्तेजस एव तदध्यापो जायन्ते ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tad aikṣata bahu syāṃ prajāyeyeti tat tejo 'sṛjata tat teja aikṣata bahu syāṃ prajāyeyeti tad apo 'sṛjata | tasmād yatra kvaca śocati svedate vā puruṣas tejasa eva tad adhy āpo jāyante || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'It thought (aikṣata): "May I be many; may I procreate." It created fire. That fire thought: "May I be many; may I procreate." It created water. Therefore whenever a person grieves or sweats, water is born from that heat.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: 'It saw/thought' — Being is conscious, refuting the Sāṅkhya's unconscious prime matter. Creation proceeds by the One's own will to be many: the world is Brahman appearing as plurality, not a second thing.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's resolve 'may I be many' is His real self-diversification into the cosmos through His body — divine will, not impersonal evolution.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The conscious Lord wills creation; fire and water arise as His dependent productions in ordered sequence.

verses.dev: ता आप ऐक्षन्त बह्व्यः स्याम प्रजायेमहीति ता अन्नमसृजन्त तस्माद्यत्र क्व च वर्षति तदेव भूयिष्ठमन्नं भवत्यद्भ्य एव तदध्यन्नाद्यं जायते ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tā āpa aikṣanta bahvyaḥ syāma prajāyemahīti tā annam asṛjanta tasmād yatra kva ca varṣati tad eva bhūyiṣṭham annaṃ bhavaty adbhya eva tad adhy annādyaṃ jāyate || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'Those waters thought: "May we be many; may we procreate." They created food (earth). Therefore wherever it rains, abundant food grows; from water indeed edible food is born.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Fire, water, food (earth): the primal triad from the One — each stage 'thinking', for the whole chain is consciousness diversifying.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each element wills under the Lord's indwelling impulse; the chain of creation is His continuous act.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The elements' 'thinking' is the Lord thinking within them; production is His alone.

verses.dev: तेषां खल्वेषां भूतानां त्रीण्येव बीजानि भवन्त्याण्डजं जीवजमुद्भिज्जमिति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: teṣāṃ khalv eṣāṃ bhūtānāṃ trīṇy eva bījāni bhavanty āṇḍajaṃ jīvajam udbhijjam iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Of these beings there are just three origins: born from the egg, born from the living, and born from the sprout.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: All creatures classed by three modes of birth — the canvas on which name-and-form will be painted.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's creatures in three families, all His body's diversifications.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The threefold taxonomy displays the Lord's ordered zoology.

verses.dev: सेयं देवतैक्षत हन्ताहमिमास्तिस्रो देवता अनेन जीवेनात्मनानुप्रविश्य नामरूपे व्याकरवाणीति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: seyaṃ devataikṣata hantāham imās tisro devatā anena jīvenātmanānupraviśya nāmarūpe vyākaravāṇīti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'That deity (Being) thought: "Come, let me enter these three deities (fire, water, food) with this living self (jīva) and unfold name and form."

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The pivotal verse: Being enters its creation 'as this living self' — the jīva is Being itself, reflected in its products. Name-and-form (nāmarūpa) are unfolded upon the One; their unfolding is the world.
