commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord enters with the jīva as His mode: He is the soul of the soul, indwelling every name and form as inner ruler — the seed of Rāmānuja's śarīra-śarīri doctrine.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the Lord enters accompanied by the eternally distinct jīva; He differentiates names and forms while remaining the inner controller, never identical with the soul He directs.

verses.dev: तासां त्रिवृतं त्रिवृतमेकैकां करवाणीति सेयं देवतेमास्तिस्रो देवता अनेनैव जीवेनात्मनानुप्रविश्य नामरूपे व्याकरोत् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tāsāṃ trivṛtaṃ trivṛtam ekaikāṃ karavāṇīti seyaṃ devatemās tisro devatā anenaiva jīvenātmanānupraviśya nāmarūpe vyākarot || 3 ||
verses.trans: '"Let me make each of them threefold." That deity, entering these three deities with this living self, unfolded name and form.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Trivṛtkaraṇa — each element compounded with the others — explains the mixed world we perceive; purity belongs to the root, mixture to the branches.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord weaves the elements together as a weaver plies threads — His craftsmanship in every compound.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The threefolding is the Lord's deliberate blending, ordered down to the least mixture.

verses.dev: तासां त्रिवृतं त्रिवृतमेकैकामकरोद्यथा तु खलु सोम्येमास्तिस्रो देवतास्त्रिवृत्त्रिवृदेकैका भवति तन्मे विजानीहीति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tāsāṃ trivṛtaṃ trivṛtam ekaikām akarod yathā tu khalu somyemās tisro devatās trivṛt trivṛd ekaikā bhavati tan me vijānīhīti || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'He made each of them threefold. Now learn from me, gentle one, how each of these three deities becomes threefold.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The father promises the analysis that follows — seeing the three strands in every appearance is training in reduction to the One.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Discernment of the components honours the Composer.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Analysis of the blend glorifies the Blender.

verses.dev: यदग्ने रोहितं रूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं तदपां यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागादग्नेरग्नित्वं वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yad agne rohitaṃ rūpaṃ tejasas tad rūpaṃ yac chuklaṃ tad apāṃ yat kṛṣṇaṃ tad annasyāpāgād agner agnitvaṃ vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇīty eva satyam || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'The red colour of fire is the colour of the primal fire; its white, of water; its black, of food. Thus the fire-ness of fire has vanished: the modification is a mere name arising from speech — the truth is just "the three colours".

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Analysis dissolves 'fire' into its three constituent colours: the composite was only a name. Śaṅkara: so every object, analysed, surrenders its claimed identity — until only Being remains.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Resolution into components shows each thing's dependence on its materials, all states of the Lord's body.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Composite names dissolve into real constituents; the analysis honours the true gradation of causes under the Lord.

verses.dev: यदादित्यस्य रोहितं रूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं तदपां यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागादादित्यादादित्यत्वं वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: yad ādityasya rohitaṃ rūpaṃ tejasas tad rūpaṃ yac chuklaṃ tad apāṃ yat kṛṣṇaṃ tad annasyāpāgād ādityād ādityatvaṃ vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇīty eva satyam || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'The red colour of the sun is the colour of the primal fire; its white, of water; its black, of food. Thus the sun-ness of the sun has vanished: the modification is a mere name arising from speech — the truth is just "the three colours".

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even the sun, mightiest of appearances, dissolves under analysis — no name withstands the reduction to constituents.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The sun's composition glorifies the Lord whose elements compose it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The great luminary too is a dependent compound under the Independent.

verses.dev: यच्चन्द्रमसो रोहितं रूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं तदपां यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागाच्चन्द्राच्चन्द्रत्वं वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: yac candramaso rohitaṃ rūpaṃ tejasas tad rūpaṃ yac chuklaṃ tad apāṃ yat kṛṣṇaṃ tad annasyāpāgāc candrāc candratvaṃ vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇīty eva satyam || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'The red colour of the moon is the colour of the primal fire; its white, of water; its black, of food. Thus the moon-ness of the moon has vanished: the modification is a mere name arising from speech — the truth is just "the three colours".

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Third dissolution: the moon yields its name to its strands.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The moon's threefold weave is the Lord's loom-work.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Another composite resolved into its divinely ordered parts.

verses.dev: यद्विद्युतो रोहितं रूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं तदपां यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागाद्विद्युतो विद्युत्त्वं वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: yad vidyuto rohitaṃ rūpaṃ tejasas tad rūpaṃ yac chuklaṃ tad apāṃ yat kṛṣṇaṃ tad annasyāpāgād vidyuto vidyuttvaṃ vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇīty eva satyam || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'The red colour of lightning is the colour of the primal fire; its white, of water; its black, of food. Thus the lightning-ness of lightning has vanished: the modification is a mere name arising from speech — the truth is just "the three colours".

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Fire, sun, moon, lightning — the world's four great lights all reduce to the same three strands: the lesson generalises to everything nameable.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All radiances are woven of the Lord's three primal threads.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The four lights exemplify universal dependence on the primal elements and their Maker.

verses.dev: एतद्ध स्म वै तद्विद्वांस आहुः पूर्वे महाशाला महाश्रोत्रिया न नोऽद्य कश्चनाश्रुतममतमविज्ञातमुदाहरिष्यतीति ह्येभ्यो विदांचक्रुः ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: etad dha sma vai tad vidvāṃsa āhuḥ pūrve mahāśālā mahāśrotriyā na no 'dya kaścanāśrutam amatam avijñātam udāhariṣyatīti hy ebhyo vidāṃ cakruḥ || 5 ||
verses.trans: 'Knowing this, the great householders and great scholars of old said: "No one now can mention to us anything unheard, unthought, or unknown" — for from these (three forms) they knew everything.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The ancients' boast fulfils the opening promise: grasping the strands, nothing remained foreign — knowledge of the root is knowledge of all.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Knowing the Lord's primal triad, the wise could place every phenomenon within His order.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Comprehension of the principal causes yields comprehension of their endless effects.

verses.dev: यदु रोहितमिवाभूदिति तेजसस्तद्रूपमिति तद्विदांचक्रुर्यदु शुक्लमिवाभूदित्यपां रूपमिति तद्विदांचक्रुर्यदु कृष्णमिवाभूदित्यन्नस्य रूपमिति तद्विदांचक्रुः ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: yad u rohitam ivābhūd iti tejasas tad rūpam iti tad vidāṃ cakrur yad u śuklam ivābhūd ity apāṃ rūpam iti tad vidāṃ cakrur yad u kṛṣṇam ivābhūd ity annasya rūpam iti tad vidāṃ cakruḥ || 6 ||
verses.trans: 'Whatever appeared reddish they knew as the colour of fire; whatever appeared whitish they knew as the colour of water; whatever appeared blackish they knew as the colour of food.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The trained eye reads every appearance back to its strands — the habit of reduction that ends in the One.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Discernment traces each hue to the Lord's element behind it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Right classification of appearances follows right knowledge of causes.

verses.dev: यदविज्ञातमिवाभूदित्येतासामेव देवतानां समास इति तद्विदांचक्रुर्यथा तु खलु सोम्येमास्तिस्रो देवताः पुरुषं प्राप्य त्रिवृत्त्रिवृदेकैका भवति तन्मे विजानीहीति ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: yad avijñātam ivābhūd ity etāsām eva devatānāṃ samāsa iti tad vidāṃ cakrur yathā tu khalu somyemās tisro devatāḥ puruṣaṃ prāpya trivṛt trivṛd ekaikā bhavati tan me vijānīhīti || 7 ||
verses.trans: 'And whatever seemed unknown they knew as a combination of these same deities. Now learn from me, gentle one, how each of these three deities, on reaching man, becomes threefold.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even the unclassifiable is only a denser mixture. The teaching now turns inward: the same strands compose the eater of food — the analysis approaches the Self.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: From cosmos to body: the Lord's three threads will now be traced within the person.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The inquiry moves from world to man, both fabrics of the Lord's weaving.

verses.dev: अन्नमशितं त्रेधा विधीयते तस्य यः स्थविष्ठो धातुस्तत्पुरीषं भवति यो मध्यमस्तन्मांसं योऽणिष्ठस्तन्मनः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: annam aśitaṃ tredhā vidhīyate tasya yaḥ sthaviṣṭho dhātus tat purīṣaṃ bhavati yo madhyamas tan māṃsaṃ yo 'ṇiṣṭhas tan manaḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Food, when eaten, is divided three ways: its coarsest part becomes faeces, its middle part flesh, its subtlest part mind.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Physiology serves metaphysics: the mind is the finest distillate of food — material, therefore not the Self. Śaṅkara uses this to detach the disciple from identifying with the mind.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's alchemy refines the meal into the instrument of thought; mind is His crafted organ for the soul's use.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Mind's material origin proves its dependence; only the Lord and the soul are non-material here, and they are eternally distinct.

verses.dev: आपः पीतास्त्रेधा विधीयन्ते तासां यः स्थविष्ठो धातुस्तन्मूत्रं भवति यो मध्यमस्तल्लोहितं योऽणिष्ठः स प्राणः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: āpaḥ pītās tredhā vidhīyante tāsāṃ yaḥ sthaviṣṭho dhātus tan mūtraṃ bhavati yo madhyamas tal lohitaṃ yo 'ṇiṣṭhaḥ sa prāṇaḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Water, when drunk, is divided three ways: its coarsest part becomes urine, its middle part blood, its subtlest part breath.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even the breath, so near the Self, is water refined — vesture, not wearer.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord turns water into the body's life-current; every sip sustains His gift of breath.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Breath's bodily aspect derives from water; its presiding deity serves the Lord.

verses.dev: तेजोऽशितं त्रेधा विधीयते तस्य यः स्थविष्ठो धातुस्तदस्थि भवति यो मध्यमः स मज्जा योऽणिष्ठः सा वाक् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tejo 'śitaṃ tredhā vidhīyate tasya yaḥ sthaviṣṭho dhātus tad asthi bhavati yo madhyamaḥ sa majjā yo 'ṇiṣṭhaḥ sā vāk || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Fire (in oil, butter, and the like), when consumed, is divided three ways: its coarsest part becomes bone, its middle part marrow, its subtlest part speech.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Speech, the organ of the Veda itself, is refined fire — the third demonstration that all instruments are elemental.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord refines fire into the tongue's power; speech is His luminous gift.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Speech's fiery substance shows its created, dependent character.

verses.dev: अन्नमयं हि सोम्य मन आपोमयः प्राणस्तेजोमयी वागिति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: annamayaṃ hi somya mana āpomayaḥ prāṇas tejomayī vāg iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'For the mind, gentle one, is made of food, the breath of water, speech of fire.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The summary triad — mind of food, breath of water, speech of fire — and the son's hunger for more: each round of teaching deepens toward the Self that none of these are.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The disciple's repeated petition models śravaṇa: hearing again and again until the truth settles.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The progressive method honours the student's growth stage by stage.

verses.dev: दध्नः सोम्य मथ्यमानस्य योऽणिमा स उर्ध्वः समुदीषति तत्सर्पिर्भवति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: dadhnaḥ somya mathyamānasya yo 'ṇimā sa urdhvaḥ samudīṣati tat sarpir bhavati || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'When curds are churned, gentle one, their subtle part rises upward and becomes butter.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The homely churn explains the body's refinery: the subtle ever rises from the gross.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's processes in kitchen and body are one law of refinement.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The illustration grounds the doctrine in observable order.

verses.dev: एवमेव खलु सोम्यान्नस्याश्यमानस्य योऽणिमा स ऊर्ध्वः समुदीषति तन्मनो भवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: evam eva khalu somyānnasyāśyamānasya yo 'ṇimā sa ūrdhvaḥ samudīṣati tan mano bhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Even so, gentle one, when food is eaten, its subtle part rises upward and becomes mind.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Mind is the butter of food — daily churned in the body's vessel.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Thought is food's cream, skimmed by the indwelling Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The mind's hourly dependence on food is plain demonstration.

verses.dev: अपां सोम्य पीयमानानां योऽणिमा स ऊर्ध्वः समुदीषति स प्राणो भवति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: apāṃ somya pīyamānānāṃ yo 'ṇimā sa ūrdhvaḥ samudīṣati sa prāṇo bhavati || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'When water is drunk, gentle one, its subtle part rises upward and becomes breath.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Breath is water's essence ascending — the churn turns in every draught.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Life rises from the cup by the Lord's quiet refining.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The breath is replenished from water as ordained.

verses.dev: तेजसः सोम्याश्यमानस्य योऽणिमा स ऊर्ध्वः समुदीषति सा वाग्भवति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tejasaḥ somyāśyamānasya yo 'ṇimā sa ūrdhvaḥ samudīṣati sā vāg bhavati || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'When fire (in rich food) is consumed, gentle one, its subtle part rises upward and becomes speech.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Speech is fire's finest flame, rising from the eaten spark.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The tongue's light is kindled daily from consumed fire.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Speech burns by borrowed fire, never self-sustained.

verses.dev: अन्नमयं हि सोम्य मन आपोमयः प्राणस्तेजोमयी वागिति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: annamayaṃ hi somya mana āpomayaḥ prāṇas tejomayī vāg iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 5 ||
verses.trans: 'For the mind, gentle one, is made of food, the breath of water, speech of fire.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The refrain returns; now the father will prove it by experiment — the fast of the next khaṇḍa.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Doctrine will be sealed by demonstration, the surest pedagogy.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: From analogy to test: the teaching submits itself to verification.

verses.dev: षोडशकलः सोम्य पुरुषः पञ्चदशाहानि माशीः काममपः पिबापोमयः प्राणो नपिबतो विच्छेत्स्यत इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: ṣoḍaśakalaḥ somya puruṣaḥ pañcadaśāhāni māśīḥ kāmam apaḥ pibāpomayaḥ prāṇo napibato vicchetsyata iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Man, gentle one, consists of sixteen parts. Do not eat for fifteen days, but drink water as you please — for the breath is made of water and will not be cut off in one who drinks.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The experiment: starve the food-made mind while preserving the water-made breath. The sixteen parts will dwindle to one.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The father risks his son's comfort for truth's demonstration — instruction by lived proof.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The test isolates variables with a scientist's care: water continues, food ceases.

verses.dev: स ह पञ्चदशाहानि नाशाथ हैनमुपससाद किं ब्रवीमि भो इत्यृचः सोम्य यजूंषि सामानीति स होवाच न वै मा प्रतिभान्ति भो इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa ha pañcadaśāhāni nāśātha hainam upasasāda kiṃ bravīmi bho ity ṛcaḥ somya yajūṃṣi sāmānīti sa hovāca na vai mā pratibhānti bho iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: He did not eat for fifteen days. Then he approached him: 'What shall I recite, sir?' — 'The Ṛg verses, the Yajus formulas, the Sāman chants, gentle one.' He said: 'They do not come to me, sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Vedas themselves vanish from the starved mind — memory, the mind's treasury, is food-funded. Twelve years of study gone dim in fifteen days.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Even sacred learning rides on the body the Lord nourishes; the proud scholar feels his dependence.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The demonstration humbles: without the Lord's material support, learning is inaccessible.

verses.dev: तं होवाच यथा सोम्य महतोऽभ्याहितस्यैकोऽङ्गारः खद्योतमात्रः परिशिष्टः स्यात्तेन ततोऽपि न बहु दहेदेवं सोम्य ते षोडशानां कलानामेका कलातिशिष्टा स्यात्तयैतर्हि वेदान्नानुभवस्यशानाथ मे विज्ञास्यसीति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ hovāca yathā somya mahato 'bhyāhitasyaiko 'ṅgāraḥ khadyotamātraḥ pariśiṣṭaḥ syāt tena tato 'pi na bahu dahed evaṃ somya te ṣoḍaśānāṃ kalānām ekā kalātiśiṣṭā syāt tayaitarhi vedān nānubhavasy aśānātha me vijñāsyasīti || 3 ||
verses.trans: He said to him: 'As from a great kindled fire a single coal the size of a firefly might remain, and by it the fire could not burn much more — even so, gentle one, of your sixteen parts a single part remains, and by it you cannot now experience the Vedas. Eat; then you will understand me.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The firefly-coal: consciousness persists, but its instrument is reduced to an ember. The Self is not destroyed by fasting — only its mental wick has burned low.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The soul endures while its faculties flicker; the Lord preserves the spark for rekindling.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The last part remaining shows the soul distinct from the waning instruments.

verses.dev: स हाशाथ हैनमुपससाद तं ह यत्किंच पप्रच्छ सर्वं ह प्रतिपेदे ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa hāśātha hainam upasasāda taṃ ha yat kiṃca papraccha sarvaṃ ha pratipede || 4 ||
verses.trans: He ate, and then approached him; and whatever he asked, he answered it all.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: One meal relights the libraries of memory — proof complete: mind is made of food.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Nourished again, the faculties serve; the demonstration is sealed in experience.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Restored capacity confirms the doctrine empirically.

verses.dev: तं होवाच यथा सोम्य महतोऽभ्याहितस्यैकमङ्गारं खद्योतमात्रं परिशिष्टं तृणैरुपसमाधाय प्राज्वलयेत्तेन ततोऽपि बहु दहेत् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ hovāca yathā somya mahato 'bhyāhitasyaikam aṅgāraṃ khadyotamātraṃ pariśiṣṭaṃ tṛṇair upasamādhāya prājvalayet tena tato 'pi bahu dahet || 5 ||
verses.trans: He said to him: 'As from a great kindled fire a single coal the size of a firefly, if covered with straw, may be made to blaze up, and by it the fire would then burn much more —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The simile completes its circle: the ember fed with straw blazes; the mind fed with food knows.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord rekindles the soul's instruments from the smallest spark.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Restoration follows nourishment in the Lord's order of causes.

verses.dev: एवं सोम्य ते षोडशानां कलानामेका कलातिशिष्टाभूत्सान्नेनोपसमाहिता प्राज्वाली तयैतर्हि वेदाननुभवस्यन्नमयं हि सोम्य मन आपोमयः प्राणस्तेजोमयी वागिति तद्धास्य विजज्ञाविति विजज्ञाविति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: evaṃ somya te ṣoḍaśānāṃ kalānām ekā kalātiśiṣṭābhūt sānnenopasamāhitā prājvālī tayaitarhi vedān anubhavasy annamayaṃ hi somya mana āpomayaḥ prāṇas tejomayī vāg iti tad dhāsya vijajñāv iti vijajñāv iti || 6 ||
verses.trans: '— even so, gentle one, of your sixteen parts a single part remained; covered with food it blazed up, and by it you now experience the Vedas. For the mind, gentle one, is made of food, the breath of water, speech of fire.' And he understood it from him — yes, he understood.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The doubled 'he understood' closes the demonstration. The disciple now knows by experience that mind is matter — the ground is cleared for the teaching of what he truly is.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Experiential conviction completes hearing; the son is ready for the doctrine of the indwelling Self.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Proof received and acknowledged; the discourse can now ascend to the Lord, root of all.

verses.dev: उद्दालको हारुणिः श्वेतकेतुं पुत्रमुवाच स्वप्नान्तं मे सोम्य विजानीहीति यत्रैतत्पुरुषः स्वपिति नाम सता सोम्य तदा संपन्नो भवति स्वमपीतो भवति तस्मादेनं स्वपितीत्याचक्षते स्वं ह्यपीतो भवति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: uddālako hāruṇiḥ śvetaketuṃ putram uvāca svapnāntaṃ me somya vijānīhīti yatraitat puruṣaḥ svapiti nāma satā somya tadā saṃpanno bhavati svam apīto bhavati tasmād enaṃ svapitīty ācakṣate svaṃ hy apīto bhavati || 1 ||
verses.trans: Uddālaka Āruṇi said to his son Śvetaketu: 'Learn from me, gentle one, the state of sleep. When a person sleeps, as it is called, then, gentle one, he has become united with Being; he has gone to his own (svam apīta). Therefore they say of him "he sleeps" (svapiti), for he has gone to his own.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara's treasured etymology: in deep sleep the soul retires into Being itself — its own true nature. Nightly we touch Brahman without knowing it; liberation is to know it.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: In sleep the soul rests in the Lord, its 'own' as the body rests in its soul; sleep is nightly refuge in Him, not loss of selfhood.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the soul in sleep reposes in the Lord ('its own' — its master and home), remaining distinct while embraced; sleep proves dependence, not identity.

verses.dev: स यथा शकुनिः सूत्रेण प्रबद्धो दिशं दिशं पतित्वान्यत्रायतनमलब्ध्वा बन्धनमेवोपश्रयत एवमेव खलु सोम्य तन्मनो दिशं दिशं पतित्वान्यत्रायतनमलब्ध्वा प्राणमेवोपश्रयते प्राणबन्धनं हि सोम्य मन इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yathā śakuniḥ sūtreṇa prabaddho diśaṃ diśaṃ patitvānyatrāyatanam alabdhvā bandhanam evopaśrayata evam eva khalu somya tan mano diśaṃ diśaṃ patitvānyatrāyatanam alabdhvā prāṇam evopaśrayate prāṇabandhanaṃ hi somya mana iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'As a bird tied by a string flies in every direction and, finding no resting place elsewhere, settles on its tether — even so, gentle one, the mind flies in every direction and, finding no resting place elsewhere, settles on the breath; for the mind, gentle one, is tethered to the breath (to Being as life).

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The tethered bird is the wandering mind; its string is the living Being. All experience circles and returns to the one perch — daily evidence of the Self as ground.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The soul's faculties range abroad but rest only in the Lord, their tether and perch.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The mind's tether is prāṇa, and prāṇa's master is the Lord — a chain of dependence ending in Him.

verses.dev: अशनापिपासे मे सोम्य विजानीहीति यत्रैतत्पुरुषोऽशिशिषति नामाप एव तदशितं नयन्ते तद्यथा गोनायोऽश्वनायः पुरुषनाय इत्येवं तदप आचक्षतेऽशनायेति तत्रैतच्छुङ्गमुत्पतितं सोम्य विजानीहि नेदममूलं भविष्यतीति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: aśanāpipāse me somya vijānīhīti yatraitat puruṣo 'śiśiṣati nāmāpa eva tad aśitaṃ nayante tad yathā gonāyo 'śvanāyaḥ puruṣanāya ity evaṃ tad apa ācakṣate 'śanāyeti tatraitac chuṅgam utpatitaṃ somya vijānīhi nedam amūlaṃ bhaviṣyatīti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Learn from me, gentle one, hunger and thirst. When a person hungers, as it is called, it is water that leads away what was eaten (digests it); and as one speaks of a cow-leader, a horse-leader, a man-leader, so water is called "food-leader" (aśanāyā, hunger). There — know, gentle one, that this (body) has sprouted up; it cannot be without a root.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Hunger itself is analysed into the elements' interplay, and the body is declared a sprout — driving the question rootward: nothing stands without a root in Being.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Even appetite obeys the Lord's chemistry; the sprout-image points to Him as the root of the body-plant.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The naming shows scripture's precision; the sprout demands a root — dependence is built into existence.

verses.dev: तस्य क्व मूलं स्यादन्यत्रान्नादेवमेव खलु सोम्यान्नेन शुङ्गेनापो मूलमन्विच्छाद्भिः सोम्य शुङ्गेन तेजो मूलमन्विच्छ तेजसा सोम्य शुङ्गेन सन्मूलमन्विच्छ सन्मूलाः सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः सदायतनाः सत्प्रतिष्ठाः ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tasya kva mūlaṃ syād anyatrānnād evam eva khalu somyānnena śuṅgenāpo mūlam anvicchādbhiḥ somya śuṅgena tejo mūlam anviccha tejasā somya śuṅgena sanmūlam anviccha sanmūlāḥ somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sadāyatanāḥ satpratiṣṭhāḥ || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'And where could its root be but in food? Even so, gentle one, with food as the sprout, seek water as the root; with water as the sprout, seek fire as the root; with fire as the sprout, seek Being as the root. All these creatures, gentle one, have Being as their root, Being as their abode, Being as their support.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The regress runs home: body → food → water → fire → SAT. Root, abode, support — the threefold dependence of all creatures on Being is the metaphysical heart of the chapter.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord is the root from which, the home in which, and the ground on which all beings live — the body-of-God doctrine in seed.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All creatures are rooted in, housed by, and stood upon the independent Lord — three modes of utter dependence.

verses.dev: अथ यत्रैतत्पुरुषः पिपासति नाम तेज एव तत्पीतं नयते तद्यथा गोनायोऽश्वनायः पुरुषनाय इत्येवं तत्तेज आचष्ट उदन्येति तत्रैतदेव शुङ्गमुत्पतितं सोम्य विजानीहि नेदममूलं भविष्यतीति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: atha yatraitat puruṣaḥ pipāsati nāma teja eva tat pītaṃ nayate tad yathā gonāyo 'śvanāyaḥ puruṣanāya ity evaṃ tat teja ācaṣṭa udanyeti tatraitad eva śuṅgam utpatitaṃ somya vijānīhi nedam amūlaṃ bhaviṣyatīti || 5 ||
verses.trans: 'And when a person thirsts, as it is called, it is fire that leads away what was drunk; and as one speaks of a cow-leader, a horse-leader, a man-leader, so that fire is called "water-leader" (udanyā, thirst). There — know, gentle one, that this (body) has sprouted up; it cannot be without a root.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Thirst, like hunger, is elemental traffic; again the sprout, again the call to the root.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's fire draws the waters within; every pang of thirst preaches dependence.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The parallel analysis reinforces the rootward search.

verses.dev: तस्य क्व मूलं स्यादन्यत्राद्भ्योऽद्भिः सोम्य शुङ्गेन तेजो मूलमन्विच्छ तेजसा सोम्य शुङ्गेन सन्मूलमन्विच्छ सन्मूलाः सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः सदायतनाः सत्प्रतिष्ठा यथा तु खलु सोम्येमास्तिस्रो देवताः पुरुषं प्राप्य त्रिवृत्त्रिवृदेकैका भवति तदुक्तं पुरस्तादेव भवत्यस्य सोम्य पुरुषस्य प्रयतो वाङ्मनसि संपद्यते मनः प्राणे प्राणस्तेजसि तेजः परस्यां देवतायाम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: tasya kva mūlaṃ syād anyatrādbhyo 'dbhiḥ somya śuṅgena tejo mūlam anviccha tejasā somya śuṅgena sanmūlam anviccha sanmūlāḥ somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sadāyatanāḥ satpratiṣṭhā yathā tu khalu somyemās tisro devatāḥ puruṣaṃ prāpya trivṛt trivṛd ekaikā bhavati tad uktaṃ purastād eva bhavaty asya somya puruṣasya prayato vāṅ manasi saṃpadyate manaḥ prāṇe prāṇas tejasi tejaḥ parasyāṃ devatāyām || 6 ||
verses.trans: 'And where could its root be but in water? With water as the sprout, seek fire as the root; with fire as the sprout, seek Being as the root. All these creatures have Being as their root, Being as their abode, Being as their support. How each of the three deities becomes threefold on reaching man has been told before. When a person departs, gentle one, his speech merges in the mind, the mind in the breath, the breath in fire, and fire in the highest deity.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The order of death reverses the order of creation: speech → mind → breath → fire → the supreme Being. Dying is the daily sleep written large — return to the root.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: At death the faculties fold inward stage by stage until the soul rests in the supreme Lord — the journey home through His own layers.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The dissolution sequence shows every power returning to the Lord's keeping; He receives what He lent.

verses.dev: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 7 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art (tat tvam asi), Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The mahāvākya itself. Śaṅkara: 'tat' is the pure Being just taught, 'tvam' the innermost consciousness of the hearer; their identity, freed of incompatible adjuncts by implication (lakṣaṇā), is the literal truth — the soul IS Brahman. This sentence, heard rightly, liberates.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Rāmānuja: the identity is read through the body–soul relation. 'That' — Brahman ensouling all — and 'thou' — Brahman ensouling this soul — are one Lord in two modes; the sentence teaches that the inner Self of Śvetaketu is Brahman as his indweller, not that the finite soul is simply God.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva famously reads the phrase with the preceding 'sa ātmā' as 'ātmā tat tvam asi' or as 'a-tat tvam asi' — 'thou art not That' in the sense of not independent — or as 'thou art His': the soul belongs to and resembles the Lord, ever distinct yet utterly dependent. The ninefold repetition hammers dependence, not identity.

verses.dev: यथा सोम्य मधु मधुकृतो निस्तिष्ठन्ति नानात्ययानां वृक्षाणां रसान्समवहारमेकतां रसं गमयन्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yathā somya madhu madhukṛto nistiṣṭhanti nānātyayānāṃ vṛkṣāṇāṃ rasān samavahāram ekatāṃ rasaṃ gamayanti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'As bees, gentle one, make honey by gathering the juices of many different trees and reducing them to one essence,

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Many juices, one honey: diversity merges in Being where individual histories dissolve.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord gathers all souls into Himself in sleep and dissolution, as honey holds many nectars.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Souls gathered in the Lord remain themselves, as drops in honey — undetected, not destroyed.

verses.dev: ते यथा तत्र न विवेकं लभन्तेऽमुष्याहं वृक्षस्य रसोऽस्म्यमुष्याहं वृक्षस्य रसोऽस्मीत्येवमेव खलु सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः सति संपद्य न विदुः सति संपद्यामह इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: te yathā tatra na vivekaṃ labhante 'muṣyāhaṃ vṛkṣasya raso 'smy amuṣyāhaṃ vṛkṣasya raso 'smīty evam eva khalu somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sati saṃpadya na viduḥ sati saṃpadyāmaha iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'and as those juices there have no discrimination — "I am the juice of this tree", "I am the juice of that tree" — even so, gentle one, all these creatures, though they merge into Being (in sleep and death), do not know: "We are merging into Being."',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: We return to Brahman nightly yet emerge ignorant — because we merge without knowledge. Śaṅkara: knowledge makes the same merging liberation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Souls rest in the Lord unknowing; the vidyā's purpose is to make that rest conscious communion.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Unawareness in merging shows the soul's smallness, not its non-existence; the wise enter His rest knowingly.

verses.dev: त इह व्याघ्रो वा सिंहो वा वृको वा वराहो वा कीटो वा पतङ्गो वा दंशो वा मशको वा यद्यद्भवन्ति तदाभवन्ति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: ta iha vyāghro vā siṃho vā vṛko vā varāho vā kīṭo vā pataṅgo vā daṃśo vā maśako vā yad yad bhavanti tad ābhavanti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Here — whether tiger or lion, wolf or boar, worm or moth, gnat or mosquito — whatever they are, that they become again.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Emerging from Being without knowledge, each resumes its old shape, steered by its karmic seed — the wheel persists for the ignorant.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Re-emergence follows each soul's deeds under the Lord's just direction.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Latent individual differences persist through merging — Madhva's proof that souls are never abolished in the Lord.

verses.dev: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The refrain returns after each image: the one essence behind merging and emerging is the hearer's own Self.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Again the indweller is named: the soul's Self-of-self is the Lord of all.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The repetition re-affirms the soul's belonging to the Lord through every cycle.

verses.dev: इमाः सोम्य नद्यः पुरस्तात्प्राच्यः स्यन्दन्ते पश्चात्प्रतीच्यस्ताः समुद्रात्समुद्रमेवापियन्ति स समुद्र एव भवति ता यथा तत्र न विदुरियमहमस्मीयमहमस्मीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: imāḥ somya nadyaḥ purastāt prācyaḥ syandante paścāt pratīcyas tāḥ samudrāt samudram evāpiyanti sa samudra eva bhavati tā yathā tatra na vidur iyam aham asmīyam aham asmīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'These rivers, gentle one, flow east toward the east and west toward the west; from the sea they rise and into the sea they return — they become the sea itself. And as there they do not know "I am this river", "I am that river",

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Rivers from sea to sea: individuality is a passage between two oceans of Being. The names 'Gaṅgā' and 'Yamunā' belong to the journey, not the water.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Souls flow from and to the Lord, their sea; their river-names mark stages of His one life in them.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Rivers in the sea are still river-waters by subtle difference; souls in the Lord remain souls — unknowing of their names, not bereft of being.

verses.dev: एवमेव खलु सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः सत आगम्य न विदुः सत आगच्छामह इति त इह व्याघ्रो वा सिंहो वा वृको वा वराहो वा कीटो वा पतङ्गो वा दंशो वा मशको वा यद्यद्भवन्ति तदाभवन्ति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: evam eva khalu somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sata āgamya na viduḥ sata āgacchāmaha iti ta iha vyāghro vā siṃho vā vṛko vā varāho vā kīṭo vā pataṅgo vā daṃśo vā maśako vā yad yad bhavanti tad ābhavanti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'even so, gentle one, all these creatures, though they come from Being, do not know: "We have come from Being." Here — whether tiger or lion, wolf or boar, worm or moth, gnat or mosquito — whatever they are, that they become again.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Birth too is unconscious emergence from the One; ignorance brackets life at both ends until knowledge dawns.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Creatures issue from the Lord unknowing; revelation exists to tell them their source.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Form follows karmic seed at each emergence, under the Lord's administration.

verses.dev: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Third declaration: the sea into which the rivers vanish is the hearer's own Self — 'thou art That' which receives and sends forth all streams.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The ocean-Lord is the soul's source and home; the refrain renews the call to know Him within.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The soul, river of the Lord's sea, is His and for Him — so the refrain teaches dependence again.

verses.dev: अस्य सोम्य महतो वृक्षस्य यो मूलेऽभ्याहन्याज्जीवन्स्रवेद्यो मध्येऽभ्याहन्याज्जीवन्स्रवेद्योऽग्रेऽभ्याहन्याज्जीवन्स्रवेत्स एष जीवेनात्मनानुप्रभूतः पेपीयमानो मोदमानस्तिष्ठति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: asya somya mahato vṛkṣasya yo mūle 'bhyāhanyāj jīvan sraved yo madhye 'bhyāhanyāj jīvan sraved yo 'gre 'bhyāhanyāj jīvan sravet sa eṣa jīvenātmanānuprabhūtaḥ pepīyamāno modamānas tiṣṭhati || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'If someone were to strike at the root of this great tree, gentle one, it would bleed but live; if at the middle, it would bleed but live; if at the top, it would bleed but live. Pervaded by the living self, it stands firm, drinking in its nourishment and rejoicing.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The tree lives by the one life pervading root, trunk, and crown — life is not in any part but throughout, as the Self pervades the body.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The indwelling soul (and through it the Lord) vivifies the whole tree; pervasion sustains every limb.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The jīva animates the tree under the Lord's sustaining presence; life is the soul's gift to matter, the Lord's gift to the soul.

verses.dev: अस्य यदेकां शाखां जीवो जहात्यथ सा शुष्यति द्वितीयां जहात्यथ सा शुष्यति तृतीयां जहात्यथ सा शुष्यति सर्वं जहाति सर्वः शुष्यति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: asya yad ekāṃ śākhāṃ jīvo jahāty atha sā śuṣyati dvitīyāṃ jahāty atha sā śuṣyati tṛtīyāṃ jahāty atha sā śuṣyati sarvaṃ jahāti sarvaḥ śuṣyati || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'If the living self leaves one branch, that branch withers; if it leaves a second, that withers; if it leaves a third, that withers. If it leaves the whole, the whole withers.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: What withers is the branch, never the life: presence vivifies, departure desiccates — but life itself neither grows nor withers.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The body's vitality is wholly borrowed from the indwelling self; the Lord's withdrawal is matter's collapse.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Departure of the animating soul leaves dead matter — the soul is the body's life, the Lord the soul's.

verses.dev: एवमेव खलु सोम्य विद्धीति होवाच जीवापेतं वाव किलेदं म्रियते न जीवो म्रियत इति स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: evam eva khalu somya viddhīti hovāca jīvāpetaṃ vāva kiledaṃ mriyate na jīvo mriyata iti sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'Know it to be even so, gentle one,' he said: 'this (body), deserted by the living self, dies — but the living self does not die. That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: 'The jīva does not die' — death belongs to the body alone. The deathless life within the tree of the body is the very Being taught from the start: thou art That.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The soul's immortality is declared; deathless, it lives in and for the deathless Lord, its inner Self.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The eternal soul outlives every body — distinct from matter below and from the Lord above, on whom it forever depends.

verses.dev: न्यग्रोधफलमत आहरेतीदं भगव इति भिन्द्धीति भिन्नं भगव इति किमत्र पश्यसीत्यण्व्य इवेमा धाना भगव इत्यासामङ्गैकां भिन्द्धीति भिन्ना भगव इति किमत्र पश्यसीति न किंचन भगव इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: nyagrodhaphalam ata āharetīdaṃ bhagava iti bhinddhīti bhinnaṃ bhagava iti kim atra paśyasīty aṇvya ivemā dhānā bhagava ity āsām aṅgaikāṃ bhinddhīti bhinnā bhagava iti kim atra paśyasīti na kiṃcana bhagava iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Fetch a fruit of that banyan tree.' — 'Here it is, revered sir.' — 'Split it.' — 'It is split, revered sir.' — 'What do you see in it?' — 'These rather fine seeds, revered sir.' — 'Split one of them.' — 'It is split, revered sir.' — 'What do you see in it?' — 'Nothing at all, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The experiment of the seed: at the heart of the visible lies the invisible. 'Nothing at all' — to the eye; everything — to understanding.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The unseen within the seed is the Lord's subtle presence, too fine for sight yet mighty in effect.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The invisible potency in the seed is the Lord's power at work, not bare emptiness.

verses.dev: तं होवाच यं वै सोम्यैतमणिमानं न निभालयस एतस्य वै सोम्यैषोऽणिम्न एवं महान्यग्रोधस्तिष्ठति श्रद्धत्स्व सोम्येति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ hovāca yaṃ vai somyaitam aṇimānaṃ na nibhālayasa etasya vai somyaiṣo 'ṇimna evaṃ mahān nyagrodhas tiṣṭhati śraddhatsva somyeti || 2 ||
verses.trans: He said to him: 'That subtle essence which you do not perceive, gentle one — from that very subtlety this great banyan tree stands. Have faith, gentle one.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: From the imperceptible springs the immense: so from unseen Being stands the world-tree. 'Have faith' — śraddhā bridges the gap until direct knowledge dawns.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord, subtler than the subtle, upholds the vast; faith in revelation precedes vision of Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The great grows from the imperceptible by the Lord's power; trust in scripture grasps what eyes cannot.

verses.dev: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The unseen seed-essence is the Self of the world and of the seer — the refrain plants the truth deeper with each image.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The invisible sustainer of tree and cosmos is the soul's own indweller.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The subtle Lord behind all growth is he to whom the soul belongs.

verses.dev: लवणमेतदुदकेऽवधायाथ मा प्रातरुपसीदथा इति स ह तथा चकार तं होवाच यद्दोषा लवणमुदकेऽवाधा अङ्ग तदाहरेति तद्धावमृश्य न विवेद ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: lavaṇam etad udake 'vadhāyātha mā prātar upasīdathā iti sa ha tathā cakāra taṃ hovāca yad doṣā lavaṇam udake 'vādhā aṅga tad āhareti tad dhāvamṛśya na viveda || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Place this salt in water and come to me in the morning.' He did so. He said to him: 'That salt you placed in the water last evening — please bring it.' He groped for it but did not find it, for it was completely dissolved.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The most beloved of the parables: the salt has vanished to hand and eye yet pervades every drop — as Being, unseizable by the senses, pervades all.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord cannot be grasped as an object, for He is everywhere within His creation as taste in water.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The unseen pervader is known by effect, not by grasp — God's presence is tasted, not handled.

verses.dev: यथा विलीनमेवाङ्गास्यान्तादाचामेति कथमिति लवणमिति मध्यादाचामेति कथमिति लवणमित्यन्तादाचामेति कथमिति लवणमित्यभिप्रास्यैतदथ मोपसीदथा इति तद्ध तथा चकार तच्छश्वत्संवर्तते तं होवाचात्र वाव किल तत्सोम्य न निभालयसेऽत्रैव किलेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: yathā vilīnam evāṅgāsyāntād ācāmeti katham iti lavaṇam iti madhyād ācāmeti katham iti lavaṇam ity antād ācāmeti katham iti lavaṇam ity abhiprāsyaitad atha mopasīdathā iti tad dha tathā cakāra tac chaśvat saṃvartate taṃ hovācātra vāva kila tat somya na nibhālayase 'traiva kileti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Sip from this end — how is it?' — 'Salt.' — 'Sip from the middle — how is it?' — 'Salt.' — 'Sip from that end — how is it?' — 'Salt.' — 'Set it aside and come to me.' He did so — yet it (the salt) exists for ever (in the water). Then he said to him: 'Here too, verily, you do not perceive Being, gentle one — yet here indeed it is.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Top, middle, bottom — salt everywhere, visible nowhere. So Being pervades body and world: not perceived, never absent. For Śaṅkara this is the direct pointer to the Self's omnipresence in experience.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: As taste in every sip, the Lord is in every particle of His body the world — the antaryāmin found wherever one 'tastes'.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord pervades all invisibly, knowable by His effects everywhere — pervasion without identity with the water.

verses.dev: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The salt-truth turns inward: the all-pervading, imperceptible Being is the taster himself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The pervading Lord is the Self of the one who sips and seeks.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The refrain again seals the soul's dependence on the all-pervading Lord.

verses.dev: यथा सोम्य पुरुषं गन्धारेभ्योऽभिनद्धाक्षमानीय तं ततोऽतिजने विसृजेत्स यथा तत्र प्राङ्वोदङ्वाधराङ्वा प्रत्यङ्वा प्रध्मायीताभिनद्धाक्ष आनीतोऽभिनद्धाक्षो विसृष्टः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yathā somya puruṣaṃ gandhārebhyo 'bhinaddhākṣam ānīya taṃ tato 'tijane visṛjet sa yathā tatra prāṅ vodaṅ vādharāṅ vā pratyaṅ vā pradhmāyītābhinaddhākṣa ānīto 'bhinaddhākṣo visṛṣṭaḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Just as, gentle one, a man might be led away blindfolded from the land of Gandhāra and abandoned in a deserted place, and as he would shout there toward east or north or south or west, "I have been brought here blindfolded! I have been left here blindfolded!" —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The parable of the human condition: blindfolded by ignorance, carried far from our homeland (Being), we cry in all directions.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The soul, hooded by karma, wanders far from its Lord and home, crying for direction.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Bound and disoriented, the soul cannot find its way without a guide — the human plight stated.

verses.dev: तस्य यथाभिनहनं प्रमुच्य प्रब्रूयादेतां दिशं गन्धारा एतां दिशं व्रजेति स ग्रामाद्ग्रामं पृच्छन्पण्डितो मेधावी गन्धारानेवोपसंपद्येतैवमेवेहाचार्यवान्पुरुषो वेद तस्य तावदेव चिरं यावन्न विमोक्ष्येऽथ संपत्स्य इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasya yathābhinahanaṃ pramucya prabrūyād etāṃ diśaṃ gandhārā etāṃ diśaṃ vrajeti sa grāmād grāmaṃ pṛcchan paṇḍito medhāvī gandhārān evopasaṃpadyetaivam eveha ācāryavān puruṣo veda tasya tāvad eva ciraṃ yāvan na vimokṣye 'tha saṃpatsya iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'and as someone might remove his blindfold and tell him, "Gandhāra is in that direction; go that way," and he, asking from village to village, informed and capable, would reach Gandhāra itself — even so here, the person who has a teacher knows: "I shall remain (in this world) only so long as I am not released; then I shall arrive home."',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Ācāryavān puruṣo veda — 'he who has a teacher knows': Śaṅkara's charter for the guru. The teacher unties the blindfold (ignorance) and points the way; the seeker, asking village to village (text to text, practice to practice), reaches the homeland that was always his. The delay lasts only as long as the body's momentum (prārabdha); then — union.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The ācārya, the Lord's emissary, removes the soul's bandage and directs it home to Him; instruction, inquiry, and arrival map the path of the surrendered.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Only the guide appointed by the Lord can free the eyes and name the road; the soul reaches its destination — the Lord's realm — by his direction.

verses.dev: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Home, road, and traveller resolve into one Being — the refrain after the parable of the way.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The homeland is the Lord; the refrain names Him as the soul's inmost Self and goal.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The destination belongs to the Lord; the soul reaches Him, never becomes Him.

verses.dev: पुरुषं सोम्योतोपतापिनं ज्ञातयः पर्युपासते जानासि मां जानासि मामिति तस्य यावन्न वाङ्मनसि संपद्यते मनः प्राणे प्राणस्तेजसि तेजः परस्यां देवतायां तावज्जानाति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: puruṣaṃ somyotopatāpinaṃ jñātayaḥ paryupāsate jānāsi māṃ jānāsi mām iti tasya yāvan na vāṅ manasi saṃpadyate manaḥ prāṇe prāṇas tejasi tejaḥ parasyāṃ devatāyāṃ tāvaj jānāti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Around a man gravely ill, gentle one, his kinsmen gather and ask: "Do you know me? Do you know me?" As long as his speech has not merged in his mind, his mind in his breath, his breath in fire, and fire in the highest deity, so long he knows them.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The deathbed observed: recognition lasts while the instruments still function; their staged withdrawal into Being is dying described from within.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The faculties retire into the Lord step by step; consciousness of kin fades as the soul turns inward to its source.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ordered dissolution at death is the Lord receiving each power in sequence.

verses.dev: अथ यदास्य वाङ्मनसि संपद्यते मनः प्राणे प्राणस्तेजसि तेजः परस्यां देवतायामथ न जानाति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadāsya vāṅ manasi saṃpadyate manaḥ prāṇe prāṇas tejasi tejaḥ parasyāṃ devatāyām atha na jānāti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'But when his speech merges in his mind, his mind in his breath, his breath in fire, and fire in the highest deity, then he no longer knows them.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Knowing of objects ceases as the knower returns to the Ground; what remains is not unconsciousness but Being beyond subject-object knowing.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The soul withdrawn into the Lord no longer attends the outer circle; it rests in Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: External cognition ends as the faculties are gathered home by their Master.

verses.dev: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavān vijñāpayatv iti tathā somyeti hovāca || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is this subtlest essence — this whole world has it as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' — 'Revered sir, instruct me still further.' — 'So be it, gentle one,' he said.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: That into which the dying merge is the very Self of the living — the eighth declaration.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The highest deity who receives the dying is the indweller of the living: one Lord at both ends.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The receiver of all at death is the Lord to whom the soul eternally belongs.

verses.dev: पुरुषं सोम्योत हस्तगृहीतमानयन्त्यपहार्षीत्स्तेयमकार्षीत्परशुमस्मै तपतेति स यदि तस्य कर्ता भवति तत एवानृतमात्मानं कुरुते सोऽनृताभिसंधोऽनृतेनात्मानमन्तर्धाय परशुं तप्तं प्रतिगृह्णाति स दह्यतेऽथ हन्यते ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: puruṣaṃ somyota hastagṛhītam ānayanty apahārṣīt steyam akārṣīt paraśum asmai tapateti sa yadi tasya kartā bhavati tata evānṛtam ātmānaṃ kurute so 'nṛtābhisaṃdho 'nṛtenātmānam antardhāya paraśuṃ taptaṃ pratigṛhṇāti sa dahyate 'tha hanyate || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'They bring a man seized by the hand, gentle one, crying: "He has robbed! He has committed theft! Heat the axe for him!" If he is the doer of it, he makes himself untrue; intent on untruth, covering himself with untruth, he grasps the heated axe — he is burnt, and then he is slain.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The ancient ordeal as parable: untruth has no protection against the fire of the Real. He who hides in falsehood is exposed by contact with truth's heat.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Falsehood severs the soul from the Lord of truth; unshielded, it burns.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The lie isolates the soul from the Lord's protection; judgment follows contact.

verses.dev: अथ यदि तस्याकर्ता भवति तत एव सत्यमात्मानं कुरुते स सत्याभिसन्धः सत्येनात्मानमन्तर्धाय परशुं तप्तं प्रतिगृह्णाति स न दह्यतेऽथ मुच्यते ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi tasyākartā bhavati tata eva satyam ātmānaṃ kurute sa satyābhisandhaḥ satyenātmānam antardhāya paraśuṃ taptaṃ pratigṛhṇāti sa na dahyate 'tha mucyate || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'But if he is not the doer of it, he makes himself true; intent on truth, covering himself with truth, he grasps the heated axe — he is not burnt, and then he is released.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Truth shields as armour shields: he who is one with the Real passes through fire unburnt. Śaṅkara: so the knower, clothed in the truth of the Self, passes through the world unscathed and is released (mucyate) — the word for liberation itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Wrapped in truth, the soul is wrapped in the Lord; fire honours its own. Release here prefigures mokṣa by truth-surrender.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Truth aligns the soul with the Lord of truth, whose protection turns the ordeal to acquittal.

verses.dev: स यथा तत्र नादाह्येतैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो इति तद्धास्य विजज्ञाविति विजज्ञाविति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa yathā tatra nādāhyetaitadātmyam idaṃ sarvaṃ tat satyaṃ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo iti tad dhāsya vijajñāv iti vijajñāv iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'As in that case he is not burnt (being grounded in truth), so this whole world has That as its self. That is the Real. That is the Self. That thou art, Śvetaketu.' And he understood it from him — yes, he understood.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The ninth and final declaration, and now the disciple understands (vijajñau) — the doubled verb announces realisation, not mere assent. The Sad-vidyā closes: Being is the world's self, the Real, the Self — and the hearer is That. For Śaṅkara, Chapter 6 is the heart of the entire Vedānta.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Nine times taught, at last received: the soul knows the Lord as its inner Self and itself as His body — intimacy without confusion, the consummation of the teaching.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The son finally grasps the soul's true station: wholly dependent on, beloved of, and forever distinct from the Lord — the knowledge that frees. The repetition certifies the transmission complete.

verses.dev: अधीहि भगव इति होपससाद सनत्कुमारं नारदस्तं होवाच यद्वेत्थ तेन मोपसीद ततस्त ऊर्ध्वं वक्ष्यामीति स होवाच ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: adhīhi bhagava iti hopasasāda sanatkumāraṃ nāradas taṃ hovāca yad vettha tena mopasīda tatas ta ūrdhvaṃ vakṣyāmīti sa hovāca || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Teach me, revered sir' — with these words Nārada approached Sanatkumāra. He said to him: 'Come to me with what you know; then I will tell you what is beyond that.' He said:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The divine sage Nārada himself comes as a student — no attainment short of Self-knowledge exempts anyone from discipleship. The teacher first inventories the known to reveal its limit.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Sanatkumāra's method is gracious: he builds upward from the disciple's holdings toward the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Even celestial sages need the lineage of instruction; knowledge is measured before it is extended.

verses.dev: ऋग्वेदं भगवोऽध्येमि यजुर्वेदं सामवेदमाथर्वणं चतुर्थमितिहासपुराणं पञ्चमं वेदानां वेदं पित्र्यं राशिं दैवं निधिं वाकोवाक्यमेकायनं देवविद्यां ब्रह्मविद्यां भूतविद्यां क्षत्रविद्यां नक्षत्रविद्यां सर्पदेवजनविद्यामेतद्भगवोऽध्येमि ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: ṛgvedaṃ bhagavo 'dhyemi yajurvedaṃ sāmavedam ātharvaṇaṃ caturtham itihāsapurāṇaṃ pañcamaṃ vedānāṃ vedaṃ pitryaṃ rāśiṃ daivaṃ nidhiṃ vākovākyam ekāyanaṃ devavidyāṃ brahmavidyāṃ bhūtavidyāṃ kṣatravidyāṃ nakṣatravidyāṃ sarpadevajanavidyām etad bhagavo 'dhyemi || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Revered sir, I know the Ṛgveda, the Yajurveda, the Sāmaveda, the Atharvan as fourth, legend and ancient lore as fifth, the Veda of Vedas (grammar), the rites for the fathers, mathematics, augury, treasure-finding, dialogues, the one-pointed doctrine, the science of gods, of ritual, of beings, of rulership, of the stars, of serpents and celestial minstrels — this, revered sir, I know.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The grandest curriculum in the Upaniṣads — and all of it about to be classed as mere 'name'. Encyclopaedic learning is not Self-knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Nārada's vast erudition lacks one science — that of the Lord within; the inventory humbles every lesser mastery.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Many sciences, one missing key: knowledge of the Lord, without which the rest cannot save.

verses.dev: सोऽहं भगवो मन्त्रविदेवास्मि नात्मविच्छ्रुतं ह्येव मे भगवद्दृशेभ्यस्तरति शोकमात्मविदिति सोऽहं भगवः शोचामि तं मा भगवाञ्छोकस्य पारं तारयत्विति तं होवाच यद्वै किंचैतदध्यगीष्ठा नामैवैतत् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: so 'haṃ bhagavo mantravid evāsmi nātmavic chrutaṃ hy eva me bhagavaddṛśebhyas tarati śokam ātmavid iti so 'haṃ bhagavaḥ śocāmi taṃ mā bhagavāñ chokasya pāraṃ tārayatv iti taṃ hovāca yad vai kiṃcaitad adhyagīṣṭhā nāmaivaitat || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'But I, revered sir, am only a knower of the words (mantras), not a knower of the Self. I have heard from men like you that the knower of the Self crosses beyond sorrow. I am in sorrow, revered sir. Take me across to the far shore of sorrow.' He said to him: 'Whatever you have studied here is really only name.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The cry at the heart of the chapter: 'mantravid evāsmi nātmavit' — a knower of texts, not of Self; and the promise quoted: 'tarati śokam ātmavit' — the Self-knower crosses sorrow. Śaṅkara hears here the whole motive of Vedānta: sorrow ends only in Self-knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Nārada's confession is the soul's universal plaint; the crossing of sorrow is found in knowing the Lord as one's inner Self — the teacher will lead him there rung by rung.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Sorrow's cure is knowledge of the Lord, not learning's bulk; the great sage models the surrender that precedes instruction.

verses.dev: नाम वा ऋग्वेदो यजुर्वेदः सामवेद आथर्वणश्चतुर्थ इतिहासपुराणः पञ्चमो वेदानां वेदः पित्र्यो राशिर्दैवो निधिर्वाकोवाक्यमेकायनं देवविद्या ब्रह्मविद्या भूतविद्या क्षत्रविद्या नक्षत्रविद्या सर्पदेवजनविद्या नामैवैतन्नामोपास्स्वेति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: nāma vā ṛgvedo yajurvedaḥ sāmaveda ātharvaṇaś caturtha itihāsapurāṇaḥ pañcamo vedānāṃ vedaḥ pitryo rāśir daivo nidhir vākovākyam ekāyanaṃ devavidyā brahmavidyā bhūtavidyā kṣatravidyā nakṣatravidyā sarpadevajanavidyā nāmaivaitan nāmopāssveti || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'Name, verily, is the Ṛgveda, the Yajurveda, the Sāmaveda, the Atharvan as fourth, legend and lore as fifth, grammar, the rites for the fathers, mathematics, augury, treasure-finding, dialogues, the one-pointed doctrine, the sciences of gods, ritual, beings, rulership, stars, serpents and minstrels. All this is only name. Meditate on name.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: All scripture and science are 'name' — the lowest rung, yet a rung: even name, meditated as Brahman, carries the mind upward.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each rung is real and to be revered as the Lord's manifestation at that level; worship of name begins the ascent to Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The hierarchy of meditations starts at name; each step is true worship when offered to the Lord in it.

verses.dev: स यो नाम ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते यावन्नाम्नो गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यो नाम ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवो नाम्नो भूय इति नाम्नो वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo nāma brahmety upāste yāvan nāmno gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo nāma brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo nāmno bhūya iti nāmno vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 5 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on name as Brahman gains free movement as far as name reaches — he who meditates on name as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than name?' — 'There is indeed something greater than name.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The dialogue's engine: each meditation yields its measured freedom, and each prompts the question 'is there more?' — the restlessness that will not stop short of the Infinite.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Every fruit has its boundary; the disciple's holy dissatisfaction is grace drawing him toward the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Graded results for graded worship; the question 'is there more?' propels the ascent ordained by the Lord.

verses.dev: वाग्वाव नाम्नो भूयसी वाग्वा ऋग्वेदं विज्ञापयति यजुर्वेदं सामवेदमाथर्वणं चतुर्थमितिहासपुराणं पञ्चमं वेदानां वेदं पित्र्यं राशिं दैवं निधिं वाकोवाक्यमेकायनं देवविद्यां ब्रह्मविद्यां भूतविद्यां क्षत्रविद्यां नक्षत्रविद्यां सर्पदेवजनविद्यां दिवं च पृथिवीं च वायुं चाकाशं चापश्च तेजश्च देवांश्च मनुष्यांश्च पशूंश्च वयांसि च तृणवनस्पतीञ्श्वापदान्याकीटपतङ्गपिपीलकं धर्मं चाधर्मं च सत्यं चानृतं च साधु चासाधु च हृदयज्ञं चाहृदयज्ञं च यद्वै वाङ्नाभविष्यन्न धर्मो नाधर्मो व्यज्ञापयिष्यन्न सत्यं नानृतं न साधु नासाधु न हृदयज्ञो नाहृदयज्ञो वागेवैतत्सर्वं विज्ञापयति वाचमुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: vāg vāva nāmno bhūyasī vāg vā ṛgvedaṃ vijñāpayati yajurvedaṃ sāmavedam ātharvaṇaṃ caturtham itihāsapurāṇaṃ pañcamaṃ vedānāṃ vedaṃ pitryaṃ rāśiṃ daivaṃ nidhiṃ vākovākyam ekāyanaṃ devavidyāṃ brahmavidyāṃ bhūtavidyāṃ kṣatravidyāṃ nakṣatravidyāṃ sarpadevajanavidyāṃ divaṃ ca pṛthivīṃ ca vāyuṃ cākāśaṃ cāpaś ca tejaś ca devāṃś ca manuṣyāṃś ca paśūṃś ca vayāṃsi ca tṛṇavanaspatīñ śvāpadāny ākīṭapataṅgapipīlakaṃ dharmaṃ cādharmaṃ ca satyaṃ cānṛtaṃ ca sādhu cāsādhu ca hṛdayajñaṃ cāhṛdayajñaṃ ca yad vai vāṅ nābhaviṣyan na dharmo nādharmo vyajñāpayiṣyan na satyaṃ nānṛtaṃ na sādhu nāsādhu na hṛdayajño nāhṛdayajño vāg evaitat sarvaṃ vijñāpayati vācam upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Speech, verily, is greater than name. Speech makes known the four Vedas and all the sciences, heaven and earth, wind and space, water and fire, gods and men, beasts and birds, grasses and trees, animals down to worms, insects, and ants, right and wrong, true and false, good and bad, pleasant and unpleasant. Had there been no speech, neither right nor wrong would be made known, nor true nor false, nor good nor bad, nor pleasant nor unpleasant. Speech alone makes all this known. Meditate on speech.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Name exists only when spoken: speech, the revealer, exceeds the revealed. Even dharma waits on the word — hence the Veda's own medium ranks above its content here.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Speech is the Lord's herald; all distinctions ride on His word-power. Worship it as His instrument.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The revealing power outranks the revealed text; speech serves the Lord as His crier in creation.

verses.dev: स यो वाचं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते यावद्वाचो गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यो वाचं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवो वाचो भूय इति वाचो वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo vācaṃ brahmety upāste yāvad vāco gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo vācaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo vāco bhūya iti vāco vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on speech as Brahman gains free movement as far as speech reaches — he who meditates on speech as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than speech?' — 'There is indeed something greater than speech.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The formula repeats; the seeker presses on — beyond the word lies the speaker.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each attainment kindles the next question, as the Lord draws the soul inward.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ladder continues by the teacher's measured disclosures.

verses.dev: मनो वाव वाचो भूयो यथा वै द्वे वामलके द्वे वा कोले द्वौ वाक्षौ मुष्टिरनुभवत्येवं वाचं च नाम च मनोऽनुभवति स यदा मनसा मनस्यति मन्त्रानधीयीयेत्यथाधीते कर्माणि कुर्वीयेत्यथ कुरुते पुत्रांश्च पशूंश्चेच्छेयेत्यथेच्छत इमं च लोकममुं चेच्छेयेत्यथेच्छते मनो ह्यात्मा मनो हि लोको मनो हि ब्रह्म मन उपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: mano vāva vāco bhūyo yathā vai dve vāmalake dve vā kole dvau vākṣau muṣṭir anubhavaty evaṃ vācaṃ ca nāma ca mano 'nubhavati sa yadā manasā manasyati mantrān adhīyīyety athādhīte karmāṇi kurvīyety atha kurute putrāṃś ca paśūṃś ceccheyety athecchata imaṃ ca lokam amuṃ ceccheyety athecchate mano hy ātmā mano hi loko mano hi brahma mana upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Mind, verily, is greater than speech. As a closed fist holds two myrobalans, two jujubes, or two dice, so does mind hold speech and name. When a man thinks in his mind "let me study the mantras", then he studies; "let me perform rites", then he performs them; "let me desire sons and cattle", then he desires; "let me desire this world and that", then he desires. Mind indeed is the self; mind is the world; mind is Brahman. Meditate on mind.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Mind grasps both word and named as the fist its fruits; all action germinates in thought. 'Mind is Brahman' — at this rung; the rungs themselves will be surpassed.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The inner organ steers all outer service; the Lord is to be sought as the mind's mover.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Mind directs speech as its master, yet is itself directed — the regress points finally to the Lord.

verses.dev: स यो मनो ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते यावन्मनसो गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यो मनो ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवो मनसो भूय इति मनसो वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo mano brahmety upāste yāvan manaso gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo mano brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo manaso bhūya iti manaso vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on mind as Brahman gains free movement as far as mind reaches — he who meditates on mind as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than mind?' — 'There is indeed something greater than mind.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Beyond the thinking instrument lies what sets it thinking.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The ascent continues toward the will behind thought.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each faculty bows to a higher, all to the Highest.

verses.dev: संकल्पो वाव मनसो भूयान्यदा वै संकल्पयतेऽथ मनस्यत्यथ वाचमीरयति तामु नाम्नीरयति नाम्नि मन्त्रा एकं भवन्ति मन्त्रेषु कर्माणि ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: saṃkalpo vāva manaso bhūyān yadā vai saṃkalpayate 'tha manasyaty atha vācam īrayati tām u nāmnīrayati nāmni mantrā ekaṃ bhavanti mantreṣu karmāṇi || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Will, verily, is greater than mind. When a man wills, then he thinks in his mind, then he sends forth speech, and he sends it forth in a name. In the name the mantras become one, and in the mantras the rites.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Behind thinking stands the resolve to think: saṅkalpa is the spring that releases the whole cascade — will, thought, word, name, text, rite.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The will is the soul's helm; rightly turned to the Lord, the whole chain below it serves Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Determination precedes cognition; the orderly cascade displays the Lord's design of the inner life.

verses.dev: तानि ह वा एतानि संकल्पैकायनानि संकल्पात्मकानि संकल्पे प्रतिष्ठितानि समकॢपतां द्यावापृथिवी समकल्पेतां वायुश्चाकाशं च समकल्पन्तापश्च तेजश्च तेषां संकॢप्त्यै वर्षं संकल्पते वर्षस्य संकॢप्त्या अन्नं संकल्पतेऽन्नस्य संकॢप्त्यै प्राणाः संकल्पन्ते प्राणानां संकॢप्त्यै मन्त्राः संकल्पन्ते मन्त्राणां संकॢप्त्यै कर्माणि संकल्पन्ते कर्मणां संकॢप्त्यै लोकः संकल्पते लोकस्य संकॢप्त्यै सर्वं संकल्पते स एष संकल्पः संकल्पमुपास्स्वेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tāni ha vā etāni saṃkalpaikāyanāni saṃkalpātmakāni saṃkalpe pratiṣṭhitāni samakḷpatāṃ dyāvāpṛthivī samakalpetāṃ vāyuś cākāśaṃ ca samakalpantāpaś ca tejaś ca teṣāṃ saṃkḷptyai varṣaṃ saṃkalpate varṣasya saṃkḷptyā annaṃ saṃkalpate 'nnasya saṃkḷptyai prāṇāḥ saṃkalpante prāṇānāṃ saṃkḷptyai mantrāḥ saṃkalpante mantrāṇāṃ saṃkḷptyai karmāṇi saṃkalpante karmaṇāṃ saṃkḷptyai lokaḥ saṃkalpate lokasya saṃkḷptyai sarvaṃ saṃkalpate sa eṣa saṃkalpaḥ saṃkalpam upāssveti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'All these have will as their sole abode, will as their self, and rest on will. Heaven and earth were formed by will; wind and space were formed; water and fire were formed. Through their formation rain takes form; through rain's formation, food; through food's formation, the vital breaths; through the breaths, the mantras; through the mantras, the rites; through the rites, the world; through the world, everything. Such is will. Meditate on will.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Cosmic saṅkalpa: the worlds themselves are crystallised resolve — echoing Being's primal 'may I be many'. The microcosmic will mirrors the macrocosmic.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All formation flows from the Lord's will; the soul's will is its likeness and instrument.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The chain of formations runs on the Supreme Will; created wills participate at His pleasure.

verses.dev: स यः संकल्पं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते संकॢप्तान्वै स लोकान्ध्रुवान्ध्रुवः प्रतिष्ठितान् प्रतिष्ठितोऽव्यथमानानव्यथमानोऽभिसिध्यति यावत्संकल्पस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यः संकल्पं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवः संकल्पाद्भूय इति संकल्पाद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa yaḥ saṃkalpaṃ brahmety upāste saṃkḷptān vai sa lokān dhruvān dhruvaḥ pratiṣṭhitān pratiṣṭhito 'vyathamānān avyathamāno 'bhisidhyati yāvat saṃkalpasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yaḥ saṃkalpaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavaḥ saṃkalpād bhūya iti saṃkalpād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on will as Brahman attains worlds formed by will — himself constant, worlds constant; himself established, worlds established; himself untroubled, worlds untroubled. He gains free movement as far as will reaches — he who meditates on will as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than will?' — 'There is indeed something greater than will.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fruit grows richer rung by rung; still the question stands — what wills the will?
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Stability flows from aligned will; yet higher faculties beckon.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Firm worlds for firm resolve; the ascent continues.

verses.dev: चित्तं वाव संकल्पाद्भूयो यदा वै चेतयतेऽथ संकल्पयतेऽथ मनस्यत्यथ वाचमीरयति तामु नाम्नीरयति नाम्नि मन्त्रा एकं भवन्ति मन्त्रेषु कर्माणि ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: cittaṃ vāva saṃkalpād bhūyo yadā vai cetayate 'tha saṃkalpayate 'tha manasyaty atha vācam īrayati tām u nāmnīrayati nāmni mantrā ekaṃ bhavanti mantreṣu karmāṇi || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Thought (citta), verily, is greater than will. When a man thinks (attends, considers), then he wills, then he thinks in his mind, then he sends forth speech, and he sends it forth in a name. In the name the mantras become one, and in the mantras the rites.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Citta — attentive intelligence — precedes resolve: one wills only what one has considered. The analysis digs ever closer to the conscious core.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Discriminating attention guides the will; the Lord is approached through the deepening interior.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Reflection ranks above resolve in the Lord's hierarchy of instruments.

verses.dev: तस्माद्यद्यपि बहुविदचित्तो भवति नायमस्तीत्येवैनमाहुर्यदयं वेद यद्वा अयं विद्वान्नेत्थमचित्तः स्यादित्यथ यद्यल्पविच्चित्तवान्भवति तस्मा एवोत बुभूषन्ति चित्तं ह्येवैषामेकायनं चित्तमात्मा चित्तं प्रतिष्ठा चित्तमुपास्स्वेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasmād yady api bahuvid acitto bhavati nāyam astīty evainam āhur yad ayaṃ veda yad vā ayaṃ vidvān netthamacittaḥ syād ity atha yady alpavic cittavān bhavati tasmā evota bubhūṣanti cittaṃ hy evaiṣām ekāyanaṃ cittam ātmā cittaṃ pratiṣṭhā cittam upāssveti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Therefore, even if a man of much learning is without attention, people say of him: "He is nothing, whatever he knows; were he truly wise he would not be so inattentive." But if a man of little learning is attentive, people are eager to listen to him. Thought is the sole abode of all these; thought is their self; thought is their support. Meditate on thought.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The world itself prizes presence over erudition — a learned fool is 'nothing'. Attention is the self of all faculties.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Heedfulness toward the Lord outweighs libraries; the attentive heart is His seat.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Alert understanding, however small its store, excels distracted learning — quality of attention is the Lord's gift.

verses.dev: स यश्चित्तं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते चित्तान्वै स लोकान्ध्रुवान्ध्रुवः प्रतिष्ठितान्प्रतिष्ठितोऽव्यथमानानव्यथमानोऽभिसिध्यति यावच्चित्तस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यश्चित्तं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवश्चित्ताद्भूय इति चित्ताद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa yaś cittaṃ brahmety upāste cittān vai sa lokān dhruvān dhruvaḥ pratiṣṭhitān pratiṣṭhito 'vyathamānān avyathamāno 'bhisidhyati yāvac cittasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yaś cittaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavaś cittād bhūya iti cittād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on thought as Brahman attains worlds of thought — himself constant, worlds constant; himself established, worlds established; himself untroubled, worlds untroubled. He gains free movement as far as thought reaches — he who meditates on thought as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than thought?' — 'There is indeed something greater than thought.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Still upward: what does attention serve?
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Beyond attention lies sustained contemplation of the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Another rung secured, another revealed.

verses.dev: ध्यानं वाव चित्ताद्भूयो ध्यायतीव पृथिवी ध्यायतीवान्तरिक्षं ध्यायतीव द्यौर्ध्यायन्तीवापो ध्यायन्तीव पर्वता ध्यायन्तीव देवमनुष्यास्तस्माद्य इह मनुष्याणां महत्तां प्राप्नुवन्ति ध्यानापादांशा इवैव ते भवन्त्यथ येऽल्पाः कलहिनः पिशुना उपवादिनस्तेऽथ ये प्रभवो ध्यानापादांशा इवैव ते भवन्ति ध्यानमुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: dhyānaṃ vāva cittād bhūyo dhyāyatīva pṛthivī dhyāyatīvāntarikṣaṃ dhyāyatīva dyaur dhyāyantīvāpo dhyāyantīva parvatā dhyāyantīva devamanuṣyās tasmād ya iha manuṣyāṇāṃ mahattāṃ prāpnuvanti dhyānāpādāṃśā ivaiva te bhavanty atha ye 'lpāḥ kalahinaḥ piśunā upavādinas te 'tha ye prabhavo dhyānāpādāṃśā ivaiva te bhavanti dhyānam upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Contemplation (dhyāna), verily, is greater than thought. The earth contemplates, as it were; the mid-region contemplates, as it were; heaven contemplates, as it were; the waters contemplate, as it were; the mountains contemplate, as it were; gods and men contemplate, as it were. Therefore those among men who attain greatness here have, as it were, a share of contemplation; while the small are quarrelsome, slanderous, and abusive, the noble have, as it were, a share of contemplation. Meditate on contemplation.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The great things of nature stand in immense stillness — mountains 'meditate'. Human greatness shares this poise; pettiness is its absence. Dhyāna is gathered being, not mere thinking.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Steady absorption is the mark of the Lord's noble servants; creation's calm grandeur models the meditative soul.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Composure reflects rank in the Lord's order; the quarrelsome reveal their distance from contemplation.

verses.dev: स यो ध्यानं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते यावद्ध्यानस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यो ध्यानं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवो ध्यानाद्भूय इति ध्यानाद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo dhyānaṃ brahmety upāste yāvad dhyānasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo dhyānaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo dhyānād bhūya iti dhyānād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on contemplation as Brahman gains free movement as far as contemplation reaches — he who meditates on contemplation as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than contemplation?' — 'There is indeed something greater than contemplation.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Absorption itself must be understood — hence the next rung.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Even meditation seeks its illuminator.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ladder rises toward understanding.

verses.dev: विज्ञानं वाव ध्यानाद्भूयो विज्ञानेन वा ऋग्वेदं विजानाति यजुर्वेदं सामवेदमाथर्वणं चतुर्थमितिहासपुराणं पञ्चमं वेदानां वेदं पित्र्यं राशिं दैवं निधिं वाकोवाक्यमेकायनं देवविद्यां ब्रह्मविद्यां भूतविद्यां क्षत्रविद्यां नक्षत्रविद्यां सर्पदेवजनविद्यां दिवं च पृथिवीं च वायुं चाकाशं चापश्च तेजश्च देवांश्च मनुष्यांश्च पशूंश्च वयांसि च तृणवनस्पतीञ्श्वापदान्याकीटपतङ्गपिपीलकं धर्मं चाधर्मं च सत्यं चानृतं च साधु चासाधु च हृदयज्ञं चाहृदयज्ञं चान्नं च रसं चेमं च लोकममुं च विज्ञानेनैव विजानाति विज्ञानमुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: vijñānaṃ vāva dhyānād bhūyo vijñānena vā ṛgvedaṃ vijānāti yajurvedaṃ sāmavedam ātharvaṇaṃ caturtham itihāsapurāṇaṃ pañcamaṃ vedānāṃ vedaṃ pitryaṃ rāśiṃ daivaṃ nidhiṃ vākovākyam ekāyanaṃ devavidyāṃ brahmavidyāṃ bhūtavidyāṃ kṣatravidyāṃ nakṣatravidyāṃ sarpadevajanavidyāṃ divaṃ ca pṛthivīṃ ca vāyuṃ cākāśaṃ cāpaś ca tejaś ca devāṃś ca manuṣyāṃś ca paśūṃś ca vayāṃsi ca tṛṇavanaspatīñ śvāpadāny ākīṭapataṅgapipīlakaṃ dharmaṃ cādharmaṃ ca satyaṃ cānṛtaṃ ca sādhu cāsādhu ca hṛdayajñaṃ cāhṛdayajñaṃ cānnaṃ ca rasaṃ cemaṃ ca lokam amuṃ ca vijñānenaiva vijānāti vijñānam upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Understanding (vijñāna), verily, is greater than contemplation. By understanding one understands the four Vedas and all the sciences, heaven and earth, wind and space, water and fire, gods and men, beasts and birds, grasses and trees, animals down to worms, insects, and ants, right and wrong, true and false, good and bad, pleasant and unpleasant, food and taste, this world and that — by understanding alone one understands. Meditate on understanding.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Vijñāna is the discerning light in which all the foregoing — texts, worlds, values — are known at all. The survey of knowables returns, now as objects of the knower.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The faculty of understanding is the Lord's lamp in the soul; by it all His domains are read.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Understanding ranks above absorption, for it discerns; discernment is the Lord's choicest endowment.
