verses.dev: स यो विज्ञानं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते विज्ञानवतो वै स लोकाञ्ज्ञानवतोऽभिसिध्यति यावद्विज्ञानस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यो विज्ञानं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवो विज्ञानाद्भूय इति विज्ञानाद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo vijñānaṃ brahmety upāste vijñānavato vai sa lokāñ jñānavato 'bhisidhyati yāvad vijñānasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo vijñānaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo vijñānād bhūya iti vijñānād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on understanding as Brahman attains worlds of understanding and knowledge. He gains free movement as far as understanding reaches — he who meditates on understanding as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than understanding?' — 'There is indeed something greater than understanding.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even the knower-faculty is an instrument; the ascent has not yet reached what is no instrument at all.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Understanding too is sustained — by strength, the next rung — and all rungs by the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ladder mounts on: every endowment confesses a greater.

verses.dev: बलं वाव विज्ञानाद्भूयोऽपि ह शतं विज्ञानवतामेको बलवानाकम्पयते स यदा बली भवत्यथोत्थाता भवत्युत्तिष्ठन्परिचरिता भवति परिचरन्नुपसत्ता भवत्युपसीदन्द्रष्टा भवति श्रोता भवति मन्ता भवति बोद्धा भवति कर्ता भवति विज्ञाता भवति बलेन वै पृथिवी तिष्ठति बलेनान्तरिक्षं बलेन द्यौर्बलेन पर्वता बलेन देवमनुष्या बलेन पशवश्च वयांसि च तृणवनस्पतयः श्वापदान्याकीटपतङ्गपिपीलकं बलेन लोकस्तिष्ठति बलमुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: balaṃ vāva vijñānād bhūyo 'pi ha śataṃ vijñānavatām eko balavān ākampayate sa yadā balī bhavaty athotthātā bhavaty uttiṣṭhan paricaritā bhavati paricarann upasattā bhavaty upasīdan draṣṭā bhavati śrotā bhavati mantā bhavati boddhā bhavati kartā bhavati vijñātā bhavati balena vai pṛthivī tiṣṭhati balenāntarikṣaṃ balena dyaur balena parvatā balena devamanuṣyā balena paśavaś ca vayāṃsi ca tṛṇavanaspatayaḥ śvāpadāny ākīṭapataṅgapipīlakaṃ balena lokas tiṣṭhati balam upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Strength, verily, is greater than understanding. One strong man makes a hundred men of understanding tremble. When a man becomes strong, he rises; rising, he serves; serving, he draws near (the teacher); drawing near, he becomes a seer, a hearer, a thinker, a perceiver, a doer, an understander. By strength the earth stands, by strength the mid-region, by strength heaven, by strength the mountains, by strength gods and men, by strength beasts and birds, grasses and trees, animals down to worms, insects, and ants; by strength the world stands. Meditate on strength.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Strength here is vigour of body and spirit — without it even understanding cannot rise, serve, and sit at the teacher's feet. Śaṅkara notes the sequence: vitality underlies all discipleship.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Capacity to serve is the Lord's enabling gift; the world itself rests on His sustaining strength.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Bala is the Lord's power lent to creatures — Madhva sees in it the energy of Vāyu upholding all under Viṣṇu.

verses.dev: स यो बलं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते यावद्बलस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यो बलं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवो बलाद्भूय इति बलाद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo balaṃ brahmety upāste yāvad balasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo balaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo balād bhūya iti balād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on strength as Brahman gains free movement as far as strength reaches — he who meditates on strength as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than strength?' — 'There is indeed something greater than strength.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Strength itself is fed — by what? The ascent continues.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Power points to its nourisher.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each power confesses its source.

verses.dev: अन्नं वाव बलाद्भूयस्तस्माद्यद्यपि दश रात्रीर्नाश्नीयाद्यद्यु ह जीवेदथवाद्रष्टाश्रोतामन्ताबोद्धाकर्ताविज्ञाता भवत्यथान्नस्यायै द्रष्टा भवति श्रोता भवति मन्ता भवति बोद्धा भवति कर्ता भवति विज्ञाता भवत्यन्नमुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: annaṃ vāva balād bhūyas tasmād yady api daśa rātrīr nāśnīyād yady u ha jīved athavādraṣṭāśrotāmantāboddhākartāvijñātā bhavaty athānnasyāyai draṣṭā bhavati śrotā bhavati mantā bhavati boddhā bhavati kartā bhavati vijñātā bhavaty annam upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Food, verily, is greater than strength. Therefore, if a man abstains from food for ten nights, even though he might live, he becomes a non-seer, a non-hearer, a non-thinker, a non-perceiver, a non-doer, a non-understander. But on the coming of food he becomes a seer, a hearer, a thinker, a perceiver, a doer, an understander. Meditate on food.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The lesson of Śvetaketu's fast restated on the ladder: all faculties are food-funded.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord as anna sustains every power of the soul's instruments.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Food's primacy over strength shows the chain of dependence the Lord has built.

verses.dev: स योऽन्नं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽन्नवतो वै स लोकान्पानवतोऽभिसिध्यति यावदन्नस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति योऽन्नं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवोऽन्नाद्भूय इत्यन्नाद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo 'nnaṃ brahmety upāste 'nnavato vai sa lokān pānavato 'bhisidhyati yāvad annasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo 'nnaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo 'nnād bhūya ity annād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on food as Brahman attains worlds rich in food and drink. He gains free movement as far as food reaches — he who meditates on food as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than food?' — 'There is indeed something greater than food.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Food in turn is born — of water.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The chain of sustenance climbs on.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each material cause yields to its prior.

verses.dev: आपो वावान्नाद्भूयस्यस्तस्माद्यदा सुवृष्टिर्न भवति व्याधीयन्ते प्राणा अन्नं कनीयो भविष्यतीत्यथ यदा सुवृष्टिर्भवत्यानन्दिनः प्राणा भवन्त्यन्नं बहु भविष्यतीत्याप एवेमा मूर्ता येयं पृथिवी यदन्तरिक्षं यद्द्यौर्यत्पर्वता यद्देवमनुष्या यत्पशवश्च वयांसि च तृणवनस्पतयः श्वापदान्याकीटपतङ्गपिपीलकमाप एवेमा मूर्ता अप उपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: āpo vāvānnād bhūyasyas tasmād yadā suvṛṣṭir na bhavati vyādhīyante prāṇā annaṃ kanīyo bhaviṣyatīty atha yadā suvṛṣṭir bhavaty ānandinaḥ prāṇā bhavanty annaṃ bahu bhaviṣyatīty āpa evemā mūrtā yeyaṃ pṛthivī yad antarikṣaṃ yad dyaur yat parvatā yad devamanuṣyā yat paśavaś ca vayāṃsi ca tṛṇavanaspatayaḥ śvāpadāny ākīṭapataṅgapipīlakam āpa evemā mūrtā apa upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Water, verily, is greater than food. Therefore when good rain does not fall, living beings sicken, thinking "food will grow scarce"; but when good rain falls, living beings rejoice, thinking "food will be plentiful". Water indeed, taking solid form, is this earth, the mid-region, heaven, the mountains, gods and men, beasts and birds, grasses and trees, animals down to worms, insects, and ants — water indeed is all these forms. Meditate on water.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: All solidity is condensed water — the moods of all creatures swing with the rains; the ladder is also a meditation on dependence.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's waters take all these shapes; joy and famine hang on His pouring.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Water's shaping power operates under the Rain-giver's command.

verses.dev: स योऽपो ब्रह्मेत्युपास्त आप्नोति सर्वान्कामांस्तृप्तिमान्भवति यावदपां गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति योऽपो ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवोऽद्भ्यो भूय इत्यद्भ्यो वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yo 'po brahmety upāsta āpnoti sarvān kāmāṃs tṛptimān bhavati yāvad apāṃ gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yo 'po brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavo 'dbhyo bhūya ity adbhyo vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on water as Brahman obtains all his desires and becomes satisfied. He gains free movement as far as water reaches — he who meditates on water as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than water?' — 'There is indeed something greater than water.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Water itself is first shown — by fire.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The chain rises toward the luminous.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Higher causes beckon.

verses.dev: तेजो वावाद्भ्यो भूयस्तद्वा एतद्वायुमागृह्याकाशमभितपति तदाहुर्निशोचति निदाघति वर्षिष्यति वा इति तेज एव तत्पूर्वं दर्शयित्वाथापः सृजते तदेतदूर्ध्वाभिश्च तिरश्चीभिश्च विद्युद्भिराह्रादाश्चरन्ति तस्मादाहुर्विद्योतते स्तनयति वर्षिष्यति वा इति तेज एव तत्पूर्वं दर्शयित्वाथापः सृजते तेज उपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: tejo vāvādbhyo bhūyas tad vā etad vāyum āgṛhyākāśam abhitapati tad āhur niśocati nidāghati varṣiṣyati vā iti teja eva tat pūrvaṃ darśayitvāthāpaḥ sṛjate tad etad ūrdhvābhiś ca tiraścībhiś ca vidyudbhir āhrādāś caranti tasmād āhur vidyotate stanayati varṣiṣyati vā iti teja eva tat pūrvaṃ darśayitvāthāpaḥ sṛjate teja upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Fire, verily, is greater than water. Seizing the wind, it heats the air; then people say: "It is sultry, it is scorching hot — it will rain!" Fire thus shows itself first and then creates the waters. Then with lightnings flashing upward and across, thunders roll; therefore people say: "It lightens, it thunders — it will rain!" Fire shows itself first and then creates the waters. Meditate on fire.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Heat precedes and produces rain: fire fathers the waters, as in the creation sequence of Chapter 6.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's fire heralds and births His rains — weather as His visible liturgy.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Fire's precedence over water reflects the created order's fixed ranks.

verses.dev: स यस्तेजो ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते तेजस्वी वै स तेजस्वतो लोकान्भास्वतोऽपहततमस्कानभिसिध्यति यावत्तेजसो गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यस्तेजो ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवस्तेजसो भूय इति तेजसो वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yas tejo brahmety upāste tejasvī vai sa tejasvato lokān bhāsvato 'pahatatamaskān abhisidhyati yāvat tejaso gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yas tejo brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavas tejaso bhūya iti tejaso vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on fire as Brahman, becoming radiant himself, attains radiant, shining worlds free of darkness. He gains free movement as far as fire reaches — he who meditates on fire as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than fire?' — 'There is indeed something greater than fire.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Beyond fire, its field: space.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Radiance leads to the expanse that holds it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Fire too has its greater.

verses.dev: आकाशो वाव तेजसो भूयानाकाशे वै सूर्याचन्द्रमसावुभौ विद्युन्नक्षत्राण्यग्निराकाशेनाह्वयत्याकाशेन शृणोत्याकाशेन प्रतिशृणोत्याकाशे रमत आकाशे न रमत आकाशे जायत आकाशमभिजायत आकाशमुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: ākāśo vāva tejaso bhūyān ākāśe vai sūryācandramasāv ubhau vidyun nakṣatrāṇy agnir ākāśenāhvayaty ākāśena śṛṇoty ākāśena pratiśṛṇoty ākāśe ramata ākāśe na ramata ākāśe jāyata ākāśam abhijāyata ākāśam upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Space, verily, is greater than fire. In space are both sun and moon, lightning, stars, and fire. Through space one calls, through space one hears, through space one answers; in space one rejoices, in space one sorrows; in space one is born, and toward space all things grow. Meditate on space.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Space holds the luminaries and all converse, joy, and birth — the subtlest element, last veil before the inner powers.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's pervasive expanse cradles all lights and all lives.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Space's containing greatness still serves a greater within.

verses.dev: स य आकाशं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्त आकाशवतो वै स लोकान्प्रकाशवतोऽसंबाधानुरुगायवतोऽभिसिध्यति यावदाकाशस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति य आकाशं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगव आकाशाद्भूय इत्याकाशाद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya ākāśaṃ brahmety upāsta ākāśavato vai sa lokān prakāśavato 'saṃbādhān urugāyavato 'bhisidhyati yāvad ākāśasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati ya ākāśaṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagava ākāśād bhūya ity ākāśād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on space as Brahman attains spacious, luminous, unconfined, wide-ranging worlds. He gains free movement as far as space reaches — he who meditates on space as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than space?' — 'There is indeed something greater than space.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Now the ladder turns inward — from the outer elements to memory, the inner light on the past.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: From the outermost expanse to the soul's own faculties — the Lord nearer with each step.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The exterior exhausted, the interior ascent begins.

verses.dev: स्मरो वावाकाशाद्भूयस्तस्माद्यद्यपि बहव आसीरन्न स्मरन्तो नैव ते कंचन शृणुयुर्न मन्वीरन्न विजानीरन्यदा वाव ते स्मरेयुरथ शृणुयुरथ मन्वीरन्नथ विजानीरन्स्मरेण वै पुत्रान्विजानाति स्मरेण पशून्स्मरमुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: smaro vāvākāśād bhūyas tasmād yady api bahava āsīran na smaranto naiva te kaṃcana śṛṇuyur na manvīran na vijānīran yadā vāva te smareyur atha śṛṇuyur atha manvīrann atha vijānīran smareṇa vai putrān vijānāti smareṇa paśūn smaram upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Memory, verily, is greater than space. Therefore, even if many were assembled, if they had no memory they would hear no one, think of no one, understand no one. But when they remember, then they hear, then they think, then they understand. By memory one knows one's sons, by memory one's cattle. Meditate on memory.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Without memory the vast outer world is as nothing: recognition is the thread of all experience. The inner outranks the outer however immense.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Memory binds life into meaning; remembrance of the Lord (smaraṇa) is its highest use.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Smara's superiority to space exalts the inner faculties the Lord directly superintends.

verses.dev: स यः स्मरं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्ते यावत्स्मरस्य गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति यः स्मरं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगवः स्मराद्भूय इति स्मराद्वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yaḥ smaraṃ brahmety upāste yāvat smarasya gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati yaḥ smaraṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagavaḥ smarād bhūya iti smarād vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on memory as Brahman gains free movement as far as memory reaches — he who meditates on memory as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than memory?' — 'There is indeed something greater than memory.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Memory looks back; hope looks forward — and kindles memory itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Beyond recall stands aspiration.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ladder mounts to hope.

verses.dev: आशा वाव स्मराद्भूयस्याशेद्धो वै स्मरो मन्त्रानधीते कर्माणि कुरुते पुत्रांश्च पशूंश्चेच्छत इमं च लोकममुं चेच्छत आशामुपास्स्वेति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: āśā vāva smarād bhūyasy āśeddho vai smaro mantrān adhīte karmāṇi kurute putrāṃś ca paśūṃś cecchata imaṃ ca lokam amuṃ cecchata āśām upāssveti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Hope, verily, is greater than memory. Kindled by hope, memory studies the mantras, performs rites, desires sons and cattle, desires this world and that. Meditate on hope.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Hope is the fire under every effort — memory itself works only when hope lights it. Desire-for-the-beyond, purified, becomes the desire for liberation (mumukṣutva).
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Aspiration moves all endeavour; directed to the Lord it becomes the longing that He rewards with Himself.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Right hope — fixed on the Lord — animates study and rite alike.

verses.dev: स य आशां ब्रह्मेत्युपास्त आशयास्य सर्वे कामाः समृध्यन्त्यमोघा हास्याशिषो भवन्ति यावदाशाया गतं तत्रास्य यथाकामचारो भवति य आशां ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽस्ति भगव आशाया भूय इत्याशाया वाव भूयोऽस्तीति तन्मे भगवान्ब्रवीत्विति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya āśāṃ brahmety upāsta āśayāsya sarve kāmāḥ samṛdhyanty amoghā hāsyāśiṣo bhavanti yāvad āśāyā gataṃ tatrāsya yathākāmacāro bhavati ya āśāṃ brahmety upāste 'sti bhagava āśāyā bhūya ity āśāyā vāva bhūyo 'stīti tan me bhagavān bravītv iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'He who meditates on hope as Brahman — by hope all his desires prosper, and his prayers are never vain. He gains free movement as far as hope reaches — he who meditates on hope as Brahman.' — 'Is there, revered sir, anything greater than hope?' — 'There is indeed something greater than hope.' — 'Tell it to me, revered sir.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: One rung remains before the summit-question: what is hope's own life?
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Aspiration lives by the life within — prāṇa, and prāṇa's Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Hope hangs on life; life on its Giver.

verses.dev: प्राणो वा आशाया भूयान्यथा वा अरा नाभौ समर्पिता एवमस्मिन्प्राणे सर्वं समर्पितं प्राणः प्राणेन याति प्राणः प्राणं ददाति प्राणाय ददाति प्राणो ह पिता प्राणो माता प्राणो भ्राता प्राणः स्वसा प्राण आचार्यः प्राणो ब्राह्मणः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: prāṇo vā āśāyā bhūyān yathā vā arā nābhau samarpitā evam asmin prāṇe sarvaṃ samarpitaṃ prāṇaḥ prāṇena yāti prāṇaḥ prāṇaṃ dadāti prāṇāya dadāti prāṇo ha pitā prāṇo mātā prāṇo bhrātā prāṇaḥ svasā prāṇa ācāryaḥ prāṇo brāhmaṇaḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'The life-breath, verily, is greater than hope. As the spokes are fastened to the hub, so is everything fastened to this life-breath. Life moves by life; life gives life; it gives to life. Life is the father, life the mother, life the brother, life the sister, life the teacher, life the brāhmaṇa.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The wheel image crowns the ladder's first ascent: all sixteen spokes are fixed in prāṇa, the hub. For Śaṅkara, prāṇa here verges on the Self whose mere presence animates every relation — father and mother are such only while life dwells in them.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All relations are relations to the life within them — and that life is the Lord's immediate vesture; honouring prāṇa, one honours the indwelling sustainer of every kin.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: Mukhya-prāṇa is the hub of created existence, the Lord's vicegerent in every body; all family and station subsist by him under Viṣṇu.

verses.dev: स यदि पितरं वा मातरं वा भ्रातरं वा स्वसारं वाचार्यं वा ब्राह्मणं वा किंचिद्भृशमिव प्रत्याह धिक्त्वास्त्वित्येवैनमाहुः पितृहा वै त्वमसि मातृहा वै त्वमसि भ्रातृहा वै त्वमसि स्वसृहा वै त्वमस्याचार्यहा वै त्वमसि ब्राह्मणहा वै त्वमसीति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: sa yadi pitaraṃ vā mātaraṃ vā bhrātaraṃ vā svasāraṃ vācāryaṃ vā brāhmaṇaṃ vā kiṃcid bhṛśam iva pratyāha dhik tvāstv ity evainam āhuḥ pitṛhā vai tvam asi mātṛhā vai tvam asi bhrātṛhā vai tvam asi svasṛhā vai tvam asy ācāryahā vai tvam asi brāhmaṇahā vai tvam asīti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'If one answers harshly, as it were, a father or mother, a brother or sister, a teacher or a brāhmaṇa, people say to him: "Shame on you! You are a slayer of your father, a slayer of your mother, a slayer of your brother, a slayer of your sister, a slayer of your teacher, a slayer of a brāhmaṇa!"',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: While life informs them, persons are sacred — offence against the living is offence against life itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Reverence is owed to the indwelling life; harshness wounds the Lord's residence.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The living temple commands respect for the sake of its inhabitant.

verses.dev: अथ यद्यप्येनानुत्क्रान्तप्राणाञ्छूलेन समासं व्यतिषंदहेन्नैवैनं ब्रूयुः पितृहासीति न मातृहासीति न भ्रातृहासीति न स्वसृहासीति नाचार्यहासीति न ब्राह्मणहासीति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: atha yady apy enān utkrāntaprāṇāñ chūlena samāsaṃ vyatiṣaṃdahen naivainaṃ brūyuḥ pitṛhāsīti na mātṛhāsīti na bhrātṛhāsīti na svasṛhāsīti nācāryahāsīti na brāhmaṇahāsīti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'But if, after the life-breath has departed from them, one should heap their bodies on a pyre with a poker and burn them up, no one would say: "You are a slayer of your father... or a slayer of a brāhmaṇa."',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The corpse proves the point: the father was never the body but the life in it. Personhood resides in prāṇa, not flesh.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Departed of its indweller, the frame is mere matter; the sacredness travelled with the life.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All honour belonged to the inhabitant — vivid proof of the soul's primacy over its house.

verses.dev: प्राणो ह्येवैतानि सर्वाणि भवति स वा एष एवं पश्यन्नेवं मन्वान एवं विजानन्नतिवादी भवति तं चेद्ब्रूयुरतिवाद्यसीत्यतिवाद्यस्मीति ब्रूयान्नापह्नुवीत ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: prāṇo hy evaitāni sarvāṇi bhavati sa vā eṣa evaṃ paśyann evaṃ manvāna evaṃ vijānann ativādī bhavati taṃ ced brūyur ativādy asīty ativādy asmīti brūyān nāpahnuvīta || 4 ||
verses.trans: 'For the life-breath alone is all these. He who sees thus, thinks thus, understands thus becomes a surpassing speaker (ativādin). If people say to him, "You are a surpassing speaker," let him say, "I am a surpassing speaker" — let him not deny it.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: He who knows the hub may speak beyond all partial speakers. Yet Śaṅkara flags the sequel: the true ativādin is the one who surpasses even this — by truth. The ladder's first summit opens onto a higher stair.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Knowledge of the universal life licenses great speech; greater still is speech grounded in the Truth above life — the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The knower of prāṇa outspeaks all; but the next khaṇḍas point past the minister to the Master.

verses.dev: एष तु वा अतिवदति यः सत्येनातिवदति सोऽहं भगवः सत्येनातिवदानीति सत्यं त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्यमिति सत्यं भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: eṣa tu vā ativadati yaḥ satyenātivadati so 'haṃ bhagavaḥ satyenātivadānīti satyaṃ tv eva vijijñāsitavyam iti satyaṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'But he truly surpasses in speech who surpasses in speech by truth.' — 'May I, revered sir, surpass in speech by truth!' — 'But one must desire to understand truth.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand truth.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Above the knower of prāṇa stands the speaker of truth — and truth must be understood, not merely uttered. The dialogue now climbs by inner conditions: each rung the prerequisite of the last.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: True speech is speech of the True One; the desire to understand Him is itself His drawing of the soul.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Truth-grounded speech outranks all eloquence; understanding of the Lord, Truth itself, must be sought.

verses.dev: यदा वै विजानात्यथ सत्यं वदति नाविजानन्सत्यं वदति विजानन्नेव सत्यं वदति विज्ञानं त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्यमिति विज्ञानं भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yadā vai vijānāty atha satyaṃ vadati nāvijānan satyaṃ vadati vijānann eva satyaṃ vadati vijñānaṃ tv eva vijijñāsitavyam iti vijñānaṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Only when one understands does one speak truth; one who does not understand does not speak truth. Only he who understands speaks truth. But one must desire to understand understanding.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand understanding.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Truth presupposes insight; parroted truth is not truth. The regress of conditions begins its descent to the root of all acts.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Understanding precedes true confession of the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Verity of speech flows from clarity of knowledge.

verses.dev: यदा वै मनुतेऽथ विजानाति नामत्वा विजानाति मत्वैव विजानाति मतिस्त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्येति मतिं भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yadā vai manute 'tha vijānāti nāmatvā vijānāti matvaiva vijānāti matis tv eva vijijñāsitavyeti matiṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Only when one reflects does one understand; without reflecting one does not understand. Only he who reflects understands. But one must desire to understand reflection.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand reflection.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Manana — sustained reflection — is the soil of insight: the second limb of Vedāntic practice named in its place.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Pondering the heard teaching ripens it into understanding.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Reasoned reflection under scripture's guidance precedes knowledge.

verses.dev: यदा वै श्रद्दधात्यथ मनुते नाश्रद्दधन्मनुते श्रद्दधदेव मनुते श्रद्धा त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्येति श्रद्धां भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yadā vai śraddadhāty atha manute nāśraddadhan manute śraddadhad eva manute śraddhā tv eva vijijñāsitavyeti śraddhāṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Only when one has faith does one reflect; without faith one does not reflect. Only he who has faith reflects. But one must desire to understand faith.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand faith.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: No one ponders what he does not trust: śraddhā — provisional confidence in scripture and teacher — underlies all inquiry.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Faith is the heart's openness to the Lord's word, mother of all reflection.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Trust in the right sources is knowledge's gate.

verses.dev: यदा वै निस्तिष्ठत्यथ श्रद्दधाति नानिस्तिष्ठञ्छ्रद्दधाति निस्तिष्ठन्नेव श्रद्दधाति निष्ठा त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्येति निष्ठां भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yadā vai nistiṣṭhaty atha śraddadhāti nānistiṣṭhañ chraddadhāti nistiṣṭhann eva śraddadhāti niṣṭhā tv eva vijijñāsitavyeti niṣṭhāṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Only when one is steadfast (in service of the teacher) does one have faith; without steadfastness one has no faith. Only he who is steadfast has faith. But one must desire to understand steadfastness.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand steadfastness.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Niṣṭhā — devoted attendance on the teacher — breeds faith; conviction grows from commitment, not before it.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Constancy in service opens the heart to trust.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Steadfast practice anchors belief.

verses.dev: यदा वै करोत्यथ निस्तिष्ठति नाकृत्वा निस्तिष्ठति कृत्वैव निस्तिष्ठति कृतिस्त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्येति कृतिं भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yadā vai karoty atha nistiṣṭhati nākṛtvā nistiṣṭhati kṛtvaiva nistiṣṭhati kṛtis tv eva vijijñāsitavyeti kṛtiṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Only when one acts (restrains the senses, performs one's duty) is one steadfast; without acting one is not steadfast. Only he who acts is steadfast. But one must desire to understand action.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand action.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Doing precedes standing-fast: discipline of sense and duty is the body of steadfastness.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Active service grounds constancy.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Performance of the ordained sustains all higher states.

verses.dev: यदा वै सुखं लभतेऽथ करोति नासुखं लब्ध्वा करोति सुखमेव लब्ध्वा करोति सुखं त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्यमिति सुखं भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yadā vai sukhaṃ labhate 'tha karoti nāsukhaṃ labdhvā karoti sukham eva labdhvā karoti sukhaṃ tv eva vijijñāsitavyam iti sukhaṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Only when one obtains happiness does one act; obtaining no happiness, one does not act. Only he who obtains happiness acts. But one must desire to understand happiness.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand happiness.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The hidden spring of all endeavour is disclosed: no being acts but for happiness. The whole ladder has been climbing toward the question of joy itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All motion seeks delight — and all delight, knowingly or not, seeks the all-delightful Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Sukha drives action; its true and full form awaits naming.

verses.dev: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखं नाल्पे सुखमस्ति भूमैव सुखं भूमा त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्य इति भूमानं भगवो विजिज्ञास इति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yo vai bhūmā tat sukhaṃ nālpe sukham asti bhūmaiva sukhaṃ bhūmā tv eva vijijñāsitavya iti bhūmānaṃ bhagavo vijijñāsa iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'That which is the Infinite (bhūman) — that is happiness. There is no happiness in the small (alpa). The Infinite alone is happiness. But one must desire to understand the Infinite.' — 'Revered sir, I desire to understand the Infinite.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The chapter's diamond: nālpe sukham asti — no finite thing can ever satisfy, for limitation itself is sorrow's root. Only the Plenitude, Brahman, is joy. Śaṅkara: every craving is the disguised longing for the Infinite Self.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Bhūman is the Lord in His boundless perfection; all small joys are droplets of His ocean, and the soul's thirst is made for that sea alone.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the Bhūman is Viṣṇu, infinite in attributes; happiness in the small is illusory because the small cannot give what only the Full possesses.

verses.dev: यत्र नान्यत्पश्यति नान्यच्छृणोति नान्यद्विजानाति स भूमाथ यत्रान्यत्पश्यत्यन्यच्छृणोत्यन्यद्विजानाति तदल्पं यो वै भूमा तदमृतमथ यदल्पं तन्मर्त्यं स भगवः कस्मिन्प्रतिष्ठित इति स्वे महिम्नि यदि वा न महिम्नीति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: yatra nānyat paśyati nānyac chṛṇoti nānyad vijānāti sa bhūmātha yatrānyat paśyaty anyac chṛṇoty anyad vijānāti tad alpaṃ yo vai bhūmā tad amṛtam atha yad alpaṃ tan martyaṃ sa bhagavaḥ kasmin pratiṣṭhita iti sve mahimni yadi vā na mahimnīti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Where one sees nothing else, hears nothing else, understands nothing else — that is the Infinite. But where one sees another, hears another, understands another — that is the small. The Infinite is the immortal; the small is the mortal.' — 'Revered sir, on what is the Infinite established?' — 'On its own greatness — or not even on greatness.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The definitive description of non-duality: the Infinite is that experience wherein no second remains to be seen, heard, or known. Duality is smallness and death; non-duality is immortality. 'Or not even on greatness': Brahman rests on nothing — even self-establishment is a concession of speech.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Rāmānuja reads: in the Bhūman one sees nothing outside Him, for all is His body — He pervades so completely that no independent 'other' appears. He rests on His own glory, dependent on none.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the Lord sees nothing else because nothing exists outside His knowledge and control; 'the small' is the dependent realm. His establishment is in His own inherent majesty alone.

verses.dev: गोअश्वमिह महिमेत्याचक्षते हस्तिहिरण्यं दासभार्यं क्षेत्राण्यायतनानीति नाहमेवं ब्रवीमि ब्रवीमीति होवाचान्यो ह्यन्यस्मिन्प्रतिष्ठित इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: goaśvam iha mahimety ācakṣate hastihiraṇyaṃ dāsabhāryaṃ kṣetrāṇy āyatanānīti nāham evaṃ bravīmi bravīmīti hovācānyo hy anyasmin pratiṣṭhita iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Here on earth people call cows and horses, elephants and gold, slaves and wives, fields and houses "greatness". I do not speak thus, I do not speak thus,' he said, 'for (in such greatness) one thing is established on another.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Worldly greatness is relational — propped on possessions, hence small. The Infinite leans on nothing; therein lies its immortality.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's greatness is intrinsic, unlike wealth's borrowed lustre.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Dependent greatness is no greatness; only the Independent is truly great.

verses.dev: स एवाधस्तात्स उपरिष्टात्स पश्चात्स पुरस्तात्स दक्षिणतः स उत्तरतः स एवेदं सर्वमित्यथातोऽहंकारादेश एवाहमेवाधस्तादहमुपरिष्टादहं पश्चादहं पुरस्तादहं दक्षिणतोऽहमुत्तरतोऽहमेवेदं सर्वमिति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: sa evādhastāt sa upariṣṭāt sa paścāt sa purastāt sa dakṣiṇataḥ sa uttarataḥ sa evedaṃ sarvam ity athāto 'haṃkārādeśa evāham evādhastād aham upariṣṭād ahaṃ paścād ahaṃ purastād ahaṃ dakṣiṇato 'ham uttarato 'ham evedaṃ sarvam iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'It is below, It is above, It is behind, It is before, It is to the south, It is to the north — It alone is all this. Next, the instruction through the "I": I am below, I am above, I am behind, I am before, I am to the south, I am to the north — I alone am all this.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Infinite is taught first as 'It', then — decisively — as 'I': the Bhūman is not a distant object but the very Self of the meditator. The shift from third to first person is the heart of the upadeśa.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The 'I' here is the Lord as inner Self of the speaker: He who is everywhere is the 'I' of every i — pervasion taught through the soul's own pronoun.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva refers the 'I' to the Lord, the true referent of all self-reference; the meditation is on His omnipresence, not the soul's.

verses.dev: अथात आत्मादेश एवात्मैवाधस्तादात्मोपरिष्टादात्मा पश्चादात्मा पुरस्तादात्मा दक्षिणत आत्मोत्तरत आत्मैवेदं सर्वमिति स वा एष एवं पश्यन्नेवं मन्वान एवं विजानन्नात्मरतिरात्मक्रीड आत्ममिथुन आत्मानन्दः स स्वराड्भवति तस्य सर्वेषु लोकेषु कामचारो भवति अथ येऽन्यथातो विदुरन्यराजानस्ते क्षय्यलोका भवन्ति तेषां सर्वेषु लोकेष्वकामचारो भवति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: athāta ātmādeśa evātmaivādhastād ātmopariṣṭād ātmā paścād ātmā purastād ātmā dakṣiṇata ātmottarata ātmaivedaṃ sarvam iti sa vā eṣa evaṃ paśyann evaṃ manvāna evaṃ vijānann ātmaratir ātmakrīḍa ātmamithuna ātmānandaḥ sa svarāḍ bhavati tasya sarveṣu lokeṣu kāmacāro bhavati atha ye 'nyathāto vidur anyarājānas te kṣayyalokā bhavanti teṣāṃ sarveṣu lokeṣv akāmacāro bhavati || 2 ||
verses.trans: 'Next, the instruction through the Self: the Self alone is below, the Self above, the Self behind, the Self before, the Self to the south, the Self to the north — the Self alone is all this. He who sees thus, thinks thus, understands thus, delighting in the Self, playing with the Self, uniting with the Self, rejoicing in the Self — he becomes self-sovereign (svarāj); in all worlds he moves at will. But those who know otherwise are ruled by others; theirs are perishable worlds, and in no world do they move at will.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Ātmaratir ātmakrīḍaḥ — the liberated delights, plays, and rejoices in the Self alone, needing no second. Svarāj: self-rule, the only sovereignty without subjects. Those who 'know otherwise' — who see duality — serve other masters in dying worlds.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The freed soul sports in communion with the supreme Self, the Lord — joy in Him, play with Him; sovereignty by participation in His freedom.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Delight 'in the Self' means in the Lord, the soul's beloved; the released, ruled by Him alone, are free of every lesser ruler — others remain bound in decaying realms.

verses.dev: तस्य ह वा एतस्यैवं पश्यत एवं मन्वानस्यैवं विजानत आत्मतः प्राण आत्मत आशात्मतः स्मर आत्मत आकाश आत्मतस्तेज आत्मत आप आत्मत आविर्भावतिरोभावावात्मतोऽन्नमात्मतो बलमात्मतो विज्ञानमात्मतो ध्यानमात्मतश्चित्तमात्मतः संकल्प आत्मतो मन आत्मतो वागात्मतो नामात्मतो मन्त्रा आत्मतः कर्माण्यात्मत एवेदं सर्वमिति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: tasya ha vā etasyaivaṃ paśyata evaṃ manvānasyaivaṃ vijānata ātmataḥ prāṇa ātmata āśātmataḥ smara ātmata ākāśa ātmatas teja ātmata āpa ātmata āvirbhāvatirobhāvāv ātmato 'nnam ātmato balam ātmato vijñānam ātmato dhyānam ātmataś cittam ātmataḥ saṃkalpa ātmato mana ātmato vāg ātmato nāmātmato mantrā ātmataḥ karmāṇy ātmata evedaṃ sarvam iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'For him who sees thus, thinks thus, understands thus — from the Self is the life-breath, from the Self hope, from the Self memory, from the Self space, from the Self fire, from the Self water, from the Self appearance and disappearance, from the Self food, from the Self strength, from the Self understanding, from the Self contemplation, from the Self thought, from the Self will, from the Self mind, from the Self speech, from the Self name, from the Self the mantras, from the Self the rites — from the Self alone is all this.',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The whole ladder is now read backward: every rung — prāṇa to name — springs from the Self. What was climbed as a series is seen, from the summit, as the Self's own radiation. This reversal is realisation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All powers and worlds issue from the supreme Self, the Lord; the knower sees every faculty as His gift flowing from Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The entire hierarchy derives from the Lord; the wise trace each power to its single Source.

verses.dev: तदेष श्लोको न पश्यो मृत्युं पश्यति न रोगं नोत दुःखतां सर्वं ह पश्यः पश्यति सर्वमाप्नोति सर्वश इति स एकधा भवति त्रिधा भवति पञ्चधा सप्तधा नवधा चैव पुनश्चैकादशः स्मृतः शतं च दश चैकश्च सहस्राणि च विंशतिराहारशुद्धौ सत्त्वशुद्धिः सत्त्वशुद्धौ ध्रुवा स्मृतिः स्मृतिलम्भे सर्वग्रन्थीनां विप्रमोक्षस्तस्मै मृदितकषायाय तमसस्पारं दर्शयति भगवान्सनत्कुमारस्तं स्कन्द इत्याचक्षते तं स्कन्द इत्याचक्षते ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tad eṣa śloko na paśyo mṛtyuṃ paśyati na rogaṃ nota duḥkhatāṃ sarvaṃ ha paśyaḥ paśyati sarvam āpnoti sarvaśa iti sa ekadhā bhavati tridhā bhavati pañcadhā saptadhā navadhā caiva punaś caikādaśaḥ smṛtaḥ śataṃ ca daśa caikaś ca sahasrāṇi ca viṃśatir āhāraśuddhau sattvaśuddhiḥ sattvaśuddhau dhruvā smṛtiḥ smṛtilambhe sarvagranthīnāṃ vipramokṣas tasmai mṛditakaṣāyāya tamasas pāraṃ darśayati bhagavān sanatkumāras taṃ skanda ity ācakṣate taṃ skanda ity ācakṣate || 2 ||
verses.trans: On this there is this verse: 'The seer sees not death, nor sickness, nor any sorrow; the seer sees only the All and attains the All entirely.' He becomes onefold, threefold, fivefold, sevenfold, ninefold; again he is called elevenfold, and a hundred-and-eleven-fold, and twenty-thousand-fold. When the food is pure, the inner nature becomes pure; when the nature is pure, memory becomes firm; when memory is attained, there is release from all the knots (of the heart). To him whose stains were wiped away, the blessed Sanatkumāra showed the farther shore of darkness. Him they call Skanda — yes, him they call Skanda.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The seer of the All is beyond death, disease, and grief; he multiplies at will, being all. Śaṅkara dwells on the chain: pure food → pure mind → firm remembrance → the snapping of all heart-knots — and the teacher 'shows the shore beyond darkness': ignorance ends, the chapter ends, sorrow ends. Sanatkumāra, the shower of the shore, is honoured as Skanda.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Purity of sustenance purifies the mind for unbroken remembrance (dhruvā smṛti) of the Lord — for Rāmānuja, the very definition of upāsanā that wins release; the ācārya graciously ferries the cleansed soul past the dark.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva: the knots fall by the Lord's grace upon the purified; Sanatkumāra-Skanda, His exalted servant, reveals the far shore. The soul attains the Lord's realm beyond all darkness, retaining its blissful individuality forever.

verses.dev: अथ यदिदमस्मिन्ब्रह्मपुरे दहरं पुण्डरीकं वेश्म दहरोऽस्मिन्नन्तराकाशस्तस्मिन्यदन्तस्तदन्वेष्टव्यं तद्वाव विजिज्ञासितव्यमिति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yad idam asmin brahmapure daharaṃ puṇḍarīkaṃ veśma daharo 'sminn antarākāśas tasmin yad antas tad anveṣṭavyaṃ tad vāva vijijñāsitavyam iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: Now, in this city of Brahman (the body) there is a small lotus-dwelling, and within it a small space. What is within that — that should be sought, that indeed one should desire to understand.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The body is Brahman's city; the heart its lotus-shrine; within it the 'small space' (daharākāśa) — which Śaṅkara identifies as Brahman itself, called small only by its seat, as the next verse will overturn all smallness.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: For Rāmānuja the dahara is the Lord dwelling in the heart's lotus — the most intimate object of meditation, enthroned within His own city.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The small space is Viṣṇu seated in the heart, to be sought there by His grace — near beyond all nearness, yet infinitely other.

verses.dev: तं चेद्ब्रूयुर्यदिदमस्मिन्ब्रह्मपुरे दहरं पुण्डरीकं वेश्म दहरोऽस्मिन्नन्तराकाशः किं तदत्र विद्यते यदन्वेष्टव्यं यद्वाव विजिज्ञासितव्यमिति स ब्रूयात् ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ ced brūyur yad idam asmin brahmapure daharaṃ puṇḍarīkaṃ veśma daharo 'sminn antarākāśaḥ kiṃ tad atra vidyate yad anveṣṭavyaṃ yad vāva vijijñāsitavyam iti sa brūyāt || 2 ||
verses.trans: If they should ask him: 'This small lotus-dwelling in the city of Brahman, and the small space within it — what is there in it that should be sought, that one should desire to understand?' — he should reply:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The objector's doubt — what could so small a space contain? — sets up the great reversal.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The question of the heart's content invites the disclosure of the Lord's fullness within.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Honest inquiry into the indweller precedes the revelation.

verses.dev: यावान्वा अयमाकाशस्तावानेषोऽन्तर्हृदय आकाश उभे अस्मिन्द्यावापृथिवी अन्तरेव समाहिते उभावग्निश्च वायुश्च सूर्याचन्द्रमसावुभौ विद्युन्नक्षत्राणि यच्चास्येहास्ति यच्च नास्ति सर्वं तदस्मिन्समाहितमिति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: yāvān vā ayam ākāśas tāvān eṣo 'ntarhṛdaya ākāśa ubhe asmin dyāvāpṛthivī antar eva samāhite ubhāv agniś ca vāyuś ca sūryācandramasāv ubhau vidyun nakṣatrāṇi yac cāsyehāsti yac ca nāsti sarvaṃ tad asmin samāhitam iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: 'As wide as this (outer) space is, so wide is this space within the heart. In it both heaven and earth are contained, both fire and wind, both sun and moon, lightning and stars — whatever of this world is his here and whatever is not, all that is contained in it.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The reversal: the 'small' space equals all space — the heart's Brahman contains heaven and earth. Inside and outside are one expanse; smallness was only the doorway.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord in the heart is no fragment: the whole cosmos rests in Him there, for He is everywhere whole.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord within is as infinite as the Lord without; His seat is small, His being measureless.

verses.dev: तं चेद्ब्रूयुरस्मिंश्चेदिदं ब्रह्मपुरे सर्वं समाहितं सर्वाणि च भूतानि सर्वे च कामा यदैतज्जरा वाप्नोति प्रध्वंसते वा किं ततोऽतिशिष्यत इति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: taṃ ced brūyur asmiṃś ced idaṃ brahmapure sarvaṃ samāhitaṃ sarvāṇi ca bhūtāni sarve ca kāmā yadaitaj jarā vāpnoti pradhvaṃsate vā kiṃ tato 'tiśiṣyata iti || 4 ||
verses.trans: If they should ask him: 'If in this city of Brahman all this is contained — all beings and all desires — then when old age overtakes it or it perishes, what remains?'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The mortal city raises the mortal question: does the treasure die with the vault?
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The body's decay tests the doctrine of the indweller.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: What survives the city's fall? The answer separates Self from house.

verses.dev: स ब्रूयान्नास्य जरयैतज्जीर्यति न वधेनास्य हन्यत एतत्सत्यं ब्रह्मपुरमस्मिन्कामाः समाहिताः एष आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पो यथा ह्येवेह प्रजा अन्वाविशन्ति यथानुशासनं यं यमन्तमभिकामा भवन्ति यं जनपदं यं क्षेत्रभागं तं तमेवोपजीवन्ति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: sa brūyān nāsya jarayaitaj jīryati na vadhenāsya hanyata etat satyaṃ brahmapuram asmin kāmāḥ samāhitāḥ eṣa ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatso 'pipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpo yathā hy eveha prajā anvāviśanti yathānuśāsanaṃ yaṃ yam antam abhikāmā bhavanti yaṃ janapadaṃ yaṃ kṣetrabhāgaṃ taṃ tam evopajīvanti || 5 ||
verses.trans: He should reply: 'It does not age with the body's aging, nor is it slain when the body is slain. This is the true city of Brahman; in it the desires are contained. This is the Self — free from evil, free from old age, free from death, free from sorrow, free from hunger and thirst, whose desires are true, whose will is true. For just as people here follow command and live dependent on whatever goal they crave — a country or a piece of land —',

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The eight epithets — apahatapāpmā through satyasaṃkalpa — define the Self sought in the heart: untouched by evil, age, death, sorrow, want. Śaṅkara: this is the same Self misknown daily; the chapter will trace its recovery through Prajāpati's teaching.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: These eight attributes are Rāmānuja's charter of the Lord's nature (and of the freed soul's likeness to Him): blemishless, deathless, true-willed. The dahara is the Lord so described.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The epithets belong to Viṣṇu in the heart — sinless, unaging, of infallible will; the soul that knows Him gains, by His gift, freedom in His likeness.

verses.dev: तद्यथेह कर्मजितो लोकः क्षीयत एवमेवामुत्र पुण्यजितो लोकः क्षीयते तद्य इहात्मानमननुविद्य व्रजन्त्येतांश्च सत्यान्कामांस्तेषां सर्वेषु लोकेष्वकामचारो भवत्यथ य इहात्मानमनुविद्य व्रजन्त्येतांश्च सत्यान्कामांस्तेषां सर्वेषु लोकेषु कामचारो भवति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: tad yatheha karmajito lokaḥ kṣīyata evam evāmutra puṇyajito lokaḥ kṣīyate tad ya ihātmānam ananuvidya vrajanty etāṃś ca satyān kāmāṃs teṣāṃ sarveṣu lokeṣv akāmacāro bhavaty atha ya ihātmānam anuvidya vrajanty etāṃś ca satyān kāmāṃs teṣāṃ sarveṣu lokeṣu kāmacāro bhavati || 6 ||
verses.trans: '— and as here a world won by work perishes, so there a world won by merit perishes. Those who depart from here without having found the Self and these true desires — for them there is no freedom of movement in any world. But those who depart from here having found the Self and these true desires — for them there is freedom of movement in all worlds.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Merit's worlds wear out like wages spent; only finding the Self yields unconditioned freedom. The refrain divides all destinies in two.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Perishable rewards versus the imperishable Lord: the soul that finds Him within moves free in His domains forever.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Earned heavens expire; knowing the indwelling Lord earns inexhaustible liberty by His grace.

verses.dev: स यदि पितृलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य पितरः समुत्तिष्ठन्ति तेन पितृलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: sa yadi pitṛlokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya pitaraḥ samuttiṣṭhanti tena pitṛlokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 1 ||
verses.trans: If he desires the world of the fathers, by his mere will the fathers rise to meet him; possessed of that world of the fathers, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: For the knower, will and fact coincide — satyasaṃkalpa demonstrated: desire no longer begs, it creates.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The freed soul's wishes flower instantly by the Lord's true-willed nature shared with it.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The released enjoy fulfilment at a thought, by the Lord's standing grant.

verses.dev: अथ यदि मातृलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य मातरः समुत्तिष्ठन्ति तेन मातृलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi mātṛlokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya mātaraḥ samuttiṣṭhanti tena mātṛlokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 2 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of the mothers, by his mere will the mothers rise to meet him; possessed of that world of the mothers, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Each cherished bond reappears at will — none lost, all transfigured.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Holy affections are restored in the Lord's realm at a wish.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The mothers' world answers the mere resolve of the freed.

verses.dev: अथ यदि भ्रातृलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य भ्रातरः समुत्तिष्ठन्ति तेन भ्रातृलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi bhrātṛlokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya bhrātaraḥ samuttiṣṭhanti tena bhrātṛlokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 3 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of brothers, by his mere will the brothers rise to meet him; possessed of that world of brothers, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Brotherhood at will — the catalogue continues.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Companionship blooms by resolve in His world.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each relation is granted as wished.

verses.dev: अथ यदि स्वसृलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य स्वसारः समुत्तिष्ठन्ति तेन स्वसृलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi svasṛlokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya svasāraḥ samuttiṣṭhanti tena svasṛlokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 4 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of sisters, by his mere will the sisters rise to meet him; possessed of that world of sisters, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Sisters likewise — will is now world.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Every dear form returns at a thought.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Wishes are honoured in His presence.

verses.dev: अथ यदि सखिलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य सखायः समुत्तिष्ठन्ति तेन सखिलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi sakhilokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya sakhāyaḥ samuttiṣṭhanti tena sakhilokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 5 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of friends, by his mere will the friends rise to meet him; possessed of that world of friends, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Friendship springs up at resolve.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The fellowship of the freed is wish-woven.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Companions arise as desired.

verses.dev: अथ यदि गन्धमाल्यलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य गन्धमाल्ये समुत्तिष्ठतस्तेन गन्धमाल्यलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi gandhamālyalokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya gandhamālye samuttiṣṭhatas tena gandhamālyalokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 6 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of perfumes and garlands, by his mere will perfumes and garlands rise; possessed of that world of perfumes and garlands, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even fragrance obeys — matter answers the knower's mind.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Delights of sense, purified, attend the freed in His garden.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Pleasures rise sanctified at the will of the released.

verses.dev: अथ यद्यन्नपानलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्यान्नपाने समुत्तिष्ठतस्तेनान्नपानलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ ७ ॥
verses.iast: atha yady annapānalokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsyānnapāne samuttiṣṭhatas tenānnapānalokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 7 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of food and drink, by his mere will food and drink rise; possessed of that world of food and drink, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Sustenance without seeking — the table of the true-willed.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's plenty serves the wish of His own.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Nourishment springs at resolve in His realm.

verses.dev: अथ यदि गीतवादित्रलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य गीतवादित्रे समुत्तिष्ठतस्तेन गीतवादित्रलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ ८ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi gītavāditralokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya gītavāditre samuttiṣṭhatas tena gītavāditralokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 8 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of song and music, by his mere will song and music rise; possessed of that world of song and music, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Music at a thought — joy unmediated by effort.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The freed sing with the Lord's minstrels at will.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Celestial music attends the wish of the blessed.

verses.dev: अथ यदि स्त्रीलोककामो भवति संकल्पादेवास्य स्त्रियः समुत्तिष्ठन्ति तेन स्त्रीलोकेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ ९ ॥
verses.iast: atha yadi strīlokakāmo bhavati saṃkalpād evāsya striyaḥ samuttiṣṭhanti tena strīlokena saṃpanno mahīyate || 9 ||
verses.trans: And if he desires the world of women, by his mere will women rise to meet him; possessed of that world of women, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Scripture withholds nothing from the list: all worlds of desire stand open — and, being satisfied at will, lose their tyranny.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: All chaste delights are granted; nothing binds where the Lord's will and the soul's are one.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Enjoyments befitting the soul's grade arise sinless in liberation.

verses.dev: यं यमन्तमभिकामो भवति यं कामं कामयते सोऽस्य संकल्पादेव समुत्तिष्ठति तेन संपन्नो महीयते ॥ १० ॥
verses.iast: yaṃ yam antam abhikāmo bhavati yaṃ kāmaṃ kāmayate so 'sya saṃkalpād eva samuttiṣṭhati tena saṃpanno mahīyate || 10 ||
verses.trans: Whatever goal he longs for, whatever desire he desires, it rises by his mere will; possessed of it, he is exalted.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The general law closes the list: for the Self-knower, will is fulfilment — because the Self is the source of every world desired.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Satyasaṃkalpatva, shared from the Lord, crowns the freed soul's state.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All wishes are met by the Lord for His own, each to its capacity.

verses.dev: त इमे सत्याः कामा अनृतापिधानास्तेषां सत्यानां सतामनृतमपिधानं यो यो ह्यस्येतः प्रैति न तमिह दर्शनाय लभते ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: ta ime satyāḥ kāmā anṛtāpidhānās teṣāṃ satyānāṃ satām anṛtam apidhānaṃ yo yo hy asyetaḥ praiti na tam iha darśanāya labhate || 1 ||
verses.trans: These true desires are covered over by the untrue. Though they are true, the untrue is their covering. For whoever of one's own departs from here, one can no longer see him here.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The true desires lie in the heart, hidden by the untrue — by ignorance and outward craving. The dead are not gone but unseen, veiled from eyes turned outward.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord and His treasures dwell within, curtained by the soul's untruth — karma and forgetfulness.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Truth within, falsehood atop: the Lord's gifts await the removal of the veil He alone can lift.

verses.dev: अथ ये चास्येह जीवा ये च प्रेता यच्चान्यदिच्छन्न लभते सर्वं तदत्र गत्वा विन्दतेऽत्र ह्यस्यैते सत्याः कामा अनृतापिधानास्तद्यथापि हिरण्यनिधिं निहितमक्षेत्रज्ञा उपर्युपरि सञ्चरन्तो न विन्देयुरेवमेवेमाः सर्वाः प्रजा अहरहर्गच्छन्त्य एतं ब्रह्मलोकं न विन्दन्त्यनृतेन हि प्रत्यूढाः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atha ye cāsyeha jīvā ye ca pretā yac cānyad icchan na labhate sarvaṃ tad atra gatvā vindate 'tra hy asyaite satyāḥ kāmā anṛtāpidhānās tad yathāpi hiraṇyanidhiṃ nihitam akṣetrajñā upary upari saṃcaranto na vindeyur evam evemāḥ sarvāḥ prajā ahar ahar gacchantya etaṃ brahmalokaṃ na vindanty anṛtena hi pratyūḍhāḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: Both the living who belong to him and the departed, and whatever else one desires and does not obtain — all that one finds by going there (within); for there indeed are these true desires of his, covered by the untrue. As people ignorant of the field might walk again and again over a buried treasure of gold and never find it, even so all these creatures go day after day into this Brahman-world (in deep sleep) and do not find it, for they are carried astray by the untrue.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara's most poignant image: we walk daily over buried gold — entering Brahman in every deep sleep, never finding what we tread upon, deflected by untruth. The treasure is not far; the map is the vidyā.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Nightly the soul rests on the Lord and wakes unknowing; the untruth of saṃsāra hides the gold beneath its feet until the ācārya marks the spot.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Daily we pass over the indwelling Lord like the ignorant over treasure; revelation alone discloses the field.

verses.dev: स वा एष आत्मा हृदि तस्यैतदेव निरुक्तं हृद्ययमिति तस्माद्धृदयमहरहर्वा एवंवित्स्वर्गं लोकमेति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa vā eṣa ātmā hṛdi tasyaitad eva niruktaṃ hṛdy ayam iti tasmād dhṛdayam ahar ahar vā evaṃvit svargaṃ lokam eti || 3 ||
verses.trans: This Self is in the heart. Its very etymology is this: 'It is in the heart' (hṛdi ayam), hence 'heart' (hṛdayam). He who knows thus goes day by day into the heavenly world (in deep sleep, into Brahman).

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The word 'heart' itself preserves the secret: hṛdi-ayam — 'this one is in the heart'. The knower's nightly entry becomes conscious homecoming.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Language enshrines the Lord's address; the knower visits Him daily within.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Etymology testifies to the indweller; the wise enter His presence each night knowingly.

verses.dev: अथ य एष संप्रसादोऽस्माच्छरीरात्समुत्थाय परं ज्योतिरुपसंपद्य स्वेन रूपेणाभिनिष्पद्यत एष आत्मेति होवाचैतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्मेति तस्य ह वा एतस्य ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: atha ya eṣa saṃprasādo 'smāc charīrāt samutthāya paraṃ jyotir upasaṃpadya svena rūpeṇābhiniṣpadyata eṣa ātmeti hovācaitad amṛtam abhayam etad brahmeti tasya ha vā etasya brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti || 4 ||
verses.trans: Now, that serene one (the soul in deep peace) who, rising out of this body and reaching the highest light, appears in its own true form — that is the Self,' he said; 'that is the immortal, the fearless; that is Brahman. And the name of this Brahman is the True (Satyam).

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The saṃprasāda — the soul serene in deep sleep — rising to the supreme Light and standing forth in its own form: Śaṅkara reads liberation itself, the Self appearing as what it always was. Its name: Satyam, the Real.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The freed soul, leaving the body, reaches the supreme Light — the Lord — and blossoms into its own pure form in His presence: the school's classic account of mukti.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Rising to the highest Light (Viṣṇu), the soul gains its own true form of bliss — distinct, dependent, fulfilled. Brahman's name 'Satyam' marks His absolute reality.

verses.dev: तानि ह वा एतानि त्रीण्यक्षराणि सतीयमिति तद्यत्सत्तदमृतमथ यत्ति तन्मर्त्यमथ यद्यं तेनोभे यच्छति यदनेनोभे यच्छति तस्माद्यमहरहर्वा एवंवित्स्वर्गं लोकमेति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: tāni ha vā etāni trīṇy akṣarāṇi satīyam iti tad yat sat tad amṛtam atha yat ti tan martyam atha yad yaṃ tenobhe yacchati yad anenobhe yacchati tasmād yam ahar ahar vā evaṃvit svargaṃ lokam eti || 5 ||
verses.trans: This name has three syllables: sat-ī-yam. The 'sat' is the immortal; the 'ti' is the mortal; the 'yam' is what holds the two together — because by it the two are held (yam), it is 'yam'. He who knows thus goes day by day into the heavenly world.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even the name dissected teaches: immortal, mortal, and the binder of both — Brahman as the Real embracing and transcending the perishable.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Satyam parsed reveals the Lord holding spirit and matter together as His body — the school's cosmology in a word.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The syllables map the hierarchy: the immortal Lord, mortal world, and His controlling bond between them.

verses.dev: अथ य आत्मा स सेतुर्विधृतिरेषां लोकानामसंभेदाय नैतं सेतुमहोरात्रे तरतो न जरा न मृत्युर्न शोको न सुकृतं न दुष्कृतं सर्वे पाप्मानोऽतो निवर्तन्तेऽपहतपाप्मा ह्येष ब्रह्मलोकः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha ya ātmā sa setur vidhṛtir eṣāṃ lokānām asaṃbhedāya naitaṃ setum ahorātre tarato na jarā na mṛtyur na śoko na sukṛtaṃ na duṣkṛtaṃ sarve pāpmāno 'to nivartante 'pahatapāpmā hy eṣa brahmalokaḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: Now, the Self is the bridge (setu), the upholder, that these worlds may not fall into confusion. Day and night do not cross that bridge, nor old age, nor death, nor sorrow, nor good deeds, nor evil deeds. All evils turn back from it, for this Brahman-world is free of evil.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Self is the dam that keeps the worlds distinct and the flood of time out: night and day, age, death — even merit — stop at its edge. Beyond the bridge nothing dual survives.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord is the setu upholding cosmic order; crossing to Him, the soul leaves time, death, and karma's pairs behind.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Lord bridges and bounds the worlds; evils cannot approach Him, and His realm is stainless.

verses.dev: तस्माद्वा एतं सेतुं तीर्त्वान्धः सन्ननन्धो भवति विद्धः सन्नविद्धो भवत्युपतापी सन्ननुपतापी भवति तस्माद्वा एतं सेतुं तीर्त्वापि नक्तमहरेवाभिनिष्पद्यते सकृद्विभातो ह्येवैष ब्रह्मलोकः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tasmād vā etaṃ setuṃ tīrtvāndhaḥ sann anandho bhavati viddhaḥ sann aviddho bhavaty upatāpī sann anupatāpī bhavati tasmād vā etaṃ setuṃ tīrtvāpi naktam ahar evābhiniṣpadyate sakṛd vibhāto hy evaiṣa brahmalokaḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: Therefore, having crossed that bridge, the blind man is blind no more, the wounded is wounded no more, the afflicted is afflicted no more. Having crossed that bridge, even night appears as day, for this Brahman-world is luminous once and for all.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Crossing heals all conditions: blindness, wounds, grief — adjuncts of the body left on the near shore. 'Once luminous' — sakṛd vibhāta — the world of Brahman knows no alternation of light.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: In the Lord's realm every infirmity falls away and unsetting light reigns — the eternal day of His presence.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Beyond the bridge the soul stands whole in Viṣṇu's never-dimming light.

verses.dev: तद्य एवैतं ब्रह्मलोकं ब्रह्मचर्येणानुविन्दन्ति तेषामेवैष ब्रह्मलोकस्तेषां सर्वेषु लोकेषु कामचारो भवति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tad ya evaitaṃ brahmalokaṃ brahmacaryeṇānuvindanti teṣām evaiṣa brahmalokas teṣāṃ sarveṣu lokeṣu kāmacāro bhavati || 3 ||
verses.trans: Now, those who find this Brahman-world by brahmacarya (the disciplined life of a student of the sacred) — theirs is this Brahman-world, and theirs is freedom of movement in all worlds.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The toll of the bridge is brahmacarya — the gathered, continent life turned wholly toward Brahman.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Disciplined devotion is the road by which the Lord's world is found.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Holy discipline, ordained by the Lord, wins entry to His realm.

verses.dev: अथ यद्यज्ञ इत्याचक्षते ब्रह्मचर्यमेव तद्ब्रह्मचर्येण ह्येव यो ज्ञाता तं विन्दतेऽथ यदिष्टमित्याचक्षते ब्रह्मचर्यमेव तद्ब्रह्मचर्येण ह्येवेष्ट्वात्मानमनुविन्दते ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yad yajña ity ācakṣate brahmacaryam eva tad brahmacaryeṇa hy eva yo jñātā taṃ vindate 'tha yad iṣṭam ity ācakṣate brahmacaryam eva tad brahmacaryeṇa hy eveṣṭvātmānam anuvindate || 1 ||
verses.trans: Now, what people call sacrifice (yajña) is really brahmacarya, for only by brahmacarya does the knower find That. And what people call the offering (iṣṭa) is really brahmacarya, for only having sought (iṣṭvā) by brahmacarya does one find the Self.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: All ritual vocabulary is reinterpreted: the true sacrifice is the disciplined search; etymologies (yajña/jñātā, iṣṭa/iṣṭvā) baptise the old rites into the new quest.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Every sacred act finds its essence in devoted discipline aimed at the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Rites are fulfilled in the discipline that seeks their inner object — the Lord.

verses.dev: अथ यत्सत्त्रायणमित्याचक्षते ब्रह्मचर्यमेव तद्ब्रह्मचर्येण ह्येव सत आत्मनस्त्राणं विन्दतेऽथ यन्मौनमित्याचक्षते ब्रह्मचर्यमेव तद्ब्रह्मचर्येण ह्येवात्मानमनुविद्य मनुते ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: atha yat sattrāyaṇam ity ācakṣate brahmacaryam eva tad brahmacaryeṇa hy eva sata ātmanas trāṇaṃ vindate 'tha yan maunam ity ācakṣate brahmacaryam eva tad brahmacaryeṇa hy evātmānam anuvidya manute || 2 ||
verses.trans: And what people call the long sacrificial session (sattrāyaṇa) is really brahmacarya, for by brahmacarya one finds the protection (trāṇa) of the real (sat) Self. And what people call the vow of silence (mauna) is really brahmacarya, for only having found the Self by brahmacarya does one (truly) think.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Sattra and mauna too dissolve into the one discipline; true silence is the stillness of one who has found.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Session and silence alike are modes of dwelling on the Lord.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All observances converge in the quest of the Self's Protector.

verses.dev: अथ यदनाशकायनमित्याचक्षते ब्रह्मचर्यमेव तदेष ह्यात्मा न नश्यति यं ब्रह्मचर्येणानुविन्दतेऽथ यदरण्यायनमित्याचक्षते ब्रह्मचर्यमेव तदरश्च ह वै ण्यश्चार्णवौ ब्रह्मलोके तृतीयस्यामितो दिवि तदैरं मदीयं सरस्तदश्वत्थः सोमसवनस्तदपराजिता पूर्ब्रह्मणः प्रभुविमितं हिरण्मयम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: atha yad anāśakāyanam ity ācakṣate brahmacaryam eva tad eṣa hy ātmā na naśyati yaṃ brahmacaryeṇānuvindate 'tha yad araṇyāyanam ity ācakṣate brahmacaryam eva tad araś ca ha vai ṇyaś cārṇavau brahmaloke tṛtīyasyām ito divi tad airaṃ madīyaṃ saras tad aśvatthaḥ somasavanas tad aparājitā pūr brahmaṇaḥ prabhuvimitaṃ hiraṇmayam || 3 ||
verses.trans: And what people call the course of fasting (anāśakāyana) is really brahmacarya, for the Self one finds by brahmacarya does not perish (na naśyati). And what people call the way to the forest (araṇyāyana) is really brahmacarya: Ara and Ṇya are two seas in the Brahman-world, in the third heaven from here; there is the lake Airammadīya, there the fig-tree Somasavana, there Aparājitā, the unconquered city of Brahman, and the golden hall built by the Lord.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Fasting and forest-dwelling are likewise the one discipline; the celestial geography — the two seas hidden in 'araṇya' — paints the goal in the colours of paradise for the meditator on the qualified Brahman.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The wondrous landscape of brahmaloka — seas, lake, tree, unconquered city, golden hall — describes the Lord's realm awaiting the disciplined soul.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The detailed splendours of the Lord's world reward the ascetic path; the unconquered city is His alone.

verses.dev: तद्य एवैतावरं च ण्यं चार्णवौ ब्रह्मलोके ब्रह्मचर्येणानुविन्दन्ति तेषामेवैष ब्रह्मलोकस्तेषां सर्वेषु लोकेषु कामचारो भवति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tad ya evaitāv araṃ ca ṇyaṃ cārṇavau brahmaloke brahmacaryeṇānuvindanti teṣām evaiṣa brahmalokas teṣāṃ sarveṣu lokeṣu kāmacāro bhavati || 4 ||
verses.trans: Those who find these two seas, Ara and Ṇya, in the Brahman-world by brahmacarya — theirs is this Brahman-world, and theirs is freedom of movement in all worlds.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The promise repeats: discipline finds the world that is freedom itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's realm and roaming-freedom crown the brahmacārin.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Entry and liberty are granted to the disciplined seeker.

verses.dev: अथ या एता हृदयस्य नाड्यस्ताः पिङ्गलस्याणिम्नस्तिष्ठन्ति शुक्लस्य नीलस्य पीतस्य लोहितस्येत्यसौ वा आदित्यः पिङ्गल एष शुक्ल एष नील एष पीत एष लोहितः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yā etā hṛdayasya nāḍyas tāḥ piṅgalasyāṇimnas tiṣṭhanti śuklasya nīlasya pītasya lohitasyety asau vā ādityaḥ piṅgala eṣa śukla eṣa nīla eṣa pīta eṣa lohitaḥ || 1 ||
verses.trans: Now, these channels (nāḍīs) of the heart are filled with the subtle essence of tawny, white, blue, yellow, and red — and yonder sun is tawny, white, blue, yellow, and red.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The heart's channels share the sun's colours: inner and outer light are continuous, vein and ray one system.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord strings heart and sun with the same coloured threads — His pathways for the soul.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The correspondence of nāḍī and ray is the Lord's built highway between body and heaven.

verses.dev: तद्यथा महापथ आतत उभौ ग्रामौ गच्छतीमं चामुं चैवमेवैता आदित्यस्य रश्मय उभौ लोकौ गच्छन्तीमं चामुं चामुष्मादादित्यात्प्रतायन्ते ता आसु नाडीषु सृप्ता आभ्यो नाडीभ्यः प्रतायन्ते तेऽमुष्मिन्नादित्ये सृप्ताः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tad yathā mahāpatha ātata ubhau grāmau gacchatīmaṃ cāmuṃ caivam evaitā ādityasya raśmaya ubhau lokau gacchantīmaṃ cāmuṃ cāmuṣmād ādityāt pratāyante tā āsu nāḍīṣu sṛptā ābhyo nāḍībhyaḥ pratāyante te 'muṣminn āditye sṛptāḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: As a great highway runs to two villages, this one and that one, so the sun's rays go to both worlds, this one and that one. They stretch from yonder sun and slip into these channels; and stretching from these channels, they slip into yonder sun.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: A two-way royal road: rays into veins, veins into rays — the soul's commute between body and the world of light.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord keeps the road open in both directions until the final one-way journey.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The linked system serves the soul's traffic as the Lord ordains.

verses.dev: तद्यत्रैतत्सुप्तः समस्तः संप्रसन्नः स्वप्नं न विजानात्यासु तदा नाडीषु सृप्तो भवति तं न कश्चन पाप्मा स्पृशति तेजसा हि तदा संपन्नो भवति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tad yatraitat suptaḥ samastaḥ saṃprasannaḥ svapnaṃ na vijānāty āsu tadā nāḍīṣu sṛpto bhavati taṃ na kaścana pāpmā spṛśati tejasā hi tadā saṃpanno bhavati || 3 ||
verses.trans: When one is asleep, composed, serene, and knows no dream, he has slipped into these channels; no evil touches him then, for he is then filled with light.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: In dreamless sleep the soul rests in radiance beyond sin's reach — the nightly preview of the sinless state.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Sleep's serenity is the Lord's embrace within the luminous channels.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The sleeper kept in light untouched by evil rests in the Lord's keeping.

verses.dev: अथ यत्रैतदबलिमानं नीतो भवति तमभित आसीना आहुर्जानासि मां जानासि मामिति स यावदस्माच्छरीरादनुत्क्रान्तो भवति तावज्जानाति ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: atha yatraitad abalimānaṃ nīto bhavati tam abhita āsīnā āhur jānāsi māṃ jānāsi mām iti sa yāvad asmāc charīrād anutkrānto bhavati tāvaj jānāti || 4 ||
verses.trans: And when one is brought to weakness (in dying), those sitting around him say: 'Do you know me? Do you know me?' As long as he has not departed from this body, he knows them.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The deathbed scene returns from Chapter 6: recognition lingers at the threshold.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The indweller attends the soul to the last moment of embodied knowing.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Cognition holds while the Lord retains the soul in its house.

verses.dev: अथ यत्रैतदस्माच्छरीरादुत्क्रामत्यथैतैरेव रश्मिभिरूर्ध्वमाक्रमते स ओमिति वा होद्वा मीयते स यावत्क्षिप्येन्मनस्तावदादित्यं गच्छत्येतद्वै खलु लोकद्वारं विदुषां प्रपदनं निरोधोऽविदुषाम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: atha yatraitad asmāc charīrād utkrāmaty athaitair eva raśmibhir ūrdhvam ākramate sa om iti vā hodvā mīyate sa yāvat kṣipyen manas tāvad ādityaṃ gacchaty etad vai khalu lokadvāraṃ viduṣāṃ prapadanaṃ nirodho 'viduṣām || 5 ||
verses.trans: But when he departs from this body, he ascends by these very rays. He goes up with the syllable OM (or as he utters it, his mind is borne up); in the time the mind takes to flash, he reaches the sun. That, verily, is the door of the worlds — an entrance for the knowers, a barrier for the ignorant.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The knower exits by the heart's ray, OM as his vehicle, swift as thought to the sun — the door that admits knowledge and refuses ignorance.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Death's gate opens to the meditator borne on praṇava; the sun-door is the Lord's threshold, shut to the unprepared.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: OM, the Lord's name, lifts the qualified through the solar gate; the ignorant find it barred.

verses.dev: तदेष श्लोकः । शतं चैका च हृदयस्य नाड्यस्तासां मूर्धानमभिनिःसृतैका । तयोर्ध्वमायन्नमृतत्वमेति विष्वङ्ङन्या उत्क्रमणे भवन्त्युत्क्रमणे भवन्ति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: tad eṣa ślokaḥ | śataṃ caikā ca hṛdayasya nāḍyas tāsāṃ mūrdhānam abhiniḥsṛtaikā | tayordhvam āyann amṛtatvam eti viṣvaṅṅ anyā utkramaṇe bhavanty utkramaṇe bhavanti || 6 ||
verses.trans: On this there is this verse: 'A hundred and one are the channels of the heart; one of them rises up to the crown of the head. Going upward by that one, a man reaches immortality; the others serve for departing in various directions — for departing in various directions.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The celebrated śloka (shared with the Kaṭha): one channel of the hundred-and-one leads through the crown to deathlessness; the rest scatter the unknowing into many births. The repetition closes the khaṇḍa.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The suṣumnā to the crown is the Lord's private stair for His meditator — the start of the arcirādi ascent.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: One channel, by the Lord's design, bears the worthy to immortality; the many disperse the rest as their karma directs.

verses.dev: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः सोऽन्वेष्टव्यः स विजिज्ञासितव्यः स सर्वांश्च लोकानाप्नोति सर्वांश्च कामान्यस्तमात्मानमनुविद्य विजानातीति ह प्रजापतिरुवाच ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatso 'pipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ so 'nveṣṭavyaḥ sa vijijñāsitavyaḥ sa sarvāṃś ca lokān āpnoti sarvāṃś ca kāmān yas tam ātmānam anuvidya vijānātīti ha prajāpatir uvāca || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'The Self which is free from evil, free from old age, free from death, free from sorrow, free from hunger and thirst, whose desires are true, whose will is true — that should be sought, that one should desire to understand. He obtains all worlds and all desires who has found and understands that Self.' Thus spoke Prajāpati.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Prajāpati's proclamation — the same eight-fold description as 8.1 — is the thesis the whole narrative will unfold through four progressive teachings. Śaṅkara: the story dramatises how easily the Self is mistaken for its reflections.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's herald announces the prize: the blemishless Self — for Rāmānuja, the Lord Himself and, derivatively, the freed soul conformed to Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The proclamation describes Viṣṇu, the flawless one; seeking Him is the supreme quest of gods and souls alike.

verses.dev: तद्धोभये देवासुरा अनुबुबुधिरे ते होचुर्हन्त तमात्मानमन्वेच्छामो यमात्मानमन्विष्य सर्वांश्च लोकानाप्नोति सर्वांश्च कामानितीन्द्रो हैव देवानामभिप्रवव्राज विरोचनोऽसुराणां तौ हासंविदानावेव समित्पाणी प्रजापतिसकाशमाजग्मतुः ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tad dhobhaye devāsurā anububudhire te hocur hanta tam ātmānam anvecchāmo yam ātmānam anviṣya sarvāṃś ca lokān āpnoti sarvāṃś ca kāmān itīndro haiva devānām abhipravavrāja virocano 'surāṇāṃ tau hāsaṃvidānāv eva samitpāṇī prajāpatisakāśam ājagmatuḥ || 2 ||
verses.trans: Both the gods and the demons heard of this. They said: 'Come, let us seek that Self by seeking which one obtains all worlds and all desires.' Indra set out from among the gods, Virocana from among the demons. Without conferring, the two came, fuel in hand, into the presence of Prajāpati.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Gods and demons alike crave the Self — but, as the story shows, what each finds depends on the depth of his hearing. Even Indra arrives as a humble student, fuel in hand.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Both orders of beings seek; both submit to discipleship — but grace and fitness will divide their harvests.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The same words reach different natures differently — Madhva's doctrine that doctrine sorts hearers by their intrinsic kind.

verses.dev: तौ ह द्वात्रिंशतं वर्षाणि ब्रह्मचर्यमूषतुस्तौ ह प्रजापतिरुवाच किमिच्छन्ताववास्तमिति तौ होचतुर्य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः सोऽन्वेष्टव्यः स विजिज्ञासितव्यः स सर्वांश्च लोकानाप्नोति सर्वांश्च कामान्यस्तमात्मानमनुविद्य विजानातीति भगवतो वचो वेदयन्ते तमिच्छन्ताववास्तमिति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tau ha dvātriṃśataṃ varṣāṇi brahmacaryam ūṣatus tau ha prajāpatir uvāca kim icchantāv avāstam iti tau hocatur ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatso 'pipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ so 'nveṣṭavyaḥ sa vijijñāsitavyaḥ sa sarvāṃś ca lokān āpnoti sarvāṃś ca kāmān yas tam ātmānam anuvidya vijānātīti bhagavato vaco vedayante tam icchantāv avāstam iti || 3 ||
verses.trans: The two lived the disciplined life of students for thirty-two years. Then Prajāpati asked them: 'Desiring what have you been living here?' They said: 'People report this as your word: "The Self which is free from evil... he obtains all worlds and all desires who has found and understands that Self." Desiring that, we have been living here.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Thirty-two years of service before a single word of teaching — the price of admission to the highest knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Long brahmacarya tests and ripens; the Lord's teacher measures the vessel before pouring.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Decades of discipline precede instruction — the protocol of the eternal school.

verses.dev: तौ ह प्रजापतिरुवाच य एषोऽक्षिणि पुरुषो दृश्यत एष आत्मेति होवाचैतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्मेत्यथ योऽयं भगवोऽप्सु परिख्यायते यश्चायमादर्शे कतम एष इत्येष उ एवैषु सर्वेष्वन्तेषु परिख्यायत इति होवाच ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tau ha prajāpatir uvāca ya eṣo 'kṣiṇi puruṣo dṛśyata eṣa ātmeti hovācaitad amṛtam abhayam etad brahmety atha yo 'yaṃ bhagavo 'psu parikhyāyate yaś cāyam ādarśe katama eṣa ity eṣa u evaiṣu sarveṣv anteṣu parikhyāyata iti hovāca || 4 ||
verses.trans: Prajāpati said to them: 'The person that is seen in the eye — that is the Self,' he said; 'that is the immortal, the fearless; that is Brahman.' — 'But, revered sir, the one who is perceived in water and the one in a mirror — which of these is he?' — 'He indeed is perceived in all these,' he replied.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The teacher speaks truly — the witness in the eye IS the Self — but ambiguously: the students hear 'the reflection'. Śaṅkara: scripture tests; the shallow take the shadow, the deep press on.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The first lesson is deliberately graded; the Lord-as-teacher meets each student at his capacity.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The真 teaching hides in plain speech; natures reveal themselves by what they grasp.

verses.dev: उदशराव आत्मानमवेक्ष्य यदात्मनो न विजानीथस्तन्मे प्रब्रूतमिति तौ होदशरावेऽवेक्षांचक्राते तौ ह प्रजापतिरुवाच किं पश्यथ इति तौ होचतुः सर्वमेवेदमावां भगव आत्मानं पश्याव आ लोमभ्य आ नखेभ्यः प्रतिरूपमिति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: udaśarāva ātmānam avekṣya yad ātmano na vijānīthas tan me prabrūtam iti tau hodaśarāve 'vekṣāṃ cakrāte tau ha prajāpatir uvāca kiṃ paśyatha iti tau hocatuḥ sarvam evedam āvāṃ bhagava ātmānaṃ paśyāva ā lomabhya ā nakhebhyaḥ pratirūpam iti || 1 ||
verses.trans: 'Look at yourselves in a pan of water, and whatever you do not understand of the Self, tell me.' They looked in the pan of water. Prajāpati asked: 'What do you see?' They said: 'Revered sir, we see ourselves entire, a likeness down to the very hairs and nails.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The water shows them — a body. They mistake the reflection for the proclaimed Self: the universal error of identifying 'I' with the seen.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The mirror-test exposes body-identification, the first knot to be cut.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The shallow reading takes shape for Self — the demonic reading, as the sequel shows.

verses.dev: तौ ह प्रजापतिरुवाच साध्वलंकृतौ सुवसनौ परिष्कृतौ भूत्वोदशरावेऽवेक्षेथामिति तौ ह साध्वलंकृतौ सुवसनौ परिष्कृतौ भूत्वोदशरावेऽवेक्षांचक्राते तौ ह प्रजापतिरुवाच किं पश्यथ इति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: tau ha prajāpatir uvāca sādhvalaṃkṛtau suvasanau pariṣkṛtau bhūtvodaśarāve 'vekṣethām iti tau ha sādhvalaṃkṛtau suvasanau pariṣkṛtau bhūtvodaśarāve 'vekṣāṃ cakrāte tau ha prajāpatir uvāca kiṃ paśyatha iti || 2 ||
verses.trans: Prajāpati said: 'Adorn yourselves well, dress finely, and groom yourselves, and then look into the pan of water.' They adorned themselves well, dressed finely, groomed themselves, and looked into the pan of water. Prajāpati asked: 'What do you see?'
